Section 1:
Introduced Version
_____
DIGEST OF INTRODUCED BILL
Citations Affected: IC 4-6; IC 4-21.5-3-6; IC 4-22-2.3-6; IC 5-27-3-3;
IC 6-1.1-23-10; IC 6-6-5.1-21; IC 6-8.1-8; IC 7.1-3-1-13.5;
IC 22-2-6-2; IC 22-4-13.3; IC 22-9-1-3; IC 23-0.5-9-58; IC 23-2.5;
IC 24-4.4; IC 24-4.5; IC 24-4.6-1-103; IC 24-4.7-1-1; IC 24-4.9-2-6;
IC 24-5; IC 24-7; IC 24-9; IC 24-10-2-1; IC 24-12-5-1; IC 25-11-1-2;
IC 25-34.1-6-2.5; IC 26-1; IC 26-2; IC 27-1-31-1; IC 27-7-3-22;
IC 28-1; IC 28-2; IC 28-5-1-6; IC 28-7; IC 28-8-6; IC 28-11-1;
IC 28-15-11-18; IC 30-4-8-2; IC 31-16-15-25; IC 32-28-15-1;
IC 32-30-10.5-5; IC 34-7-4-2; IC 34-30-2.1; IC 34-31-2-6;
IC 34-55-8-7; IC 34-57-2-1; IC 35-38-1-18; IC 35-45-7; IC 35-52;
IC 36-1-8-11; IC 36-2; IC 37.
Synopsis: Reorganization of consumer lending laws. Repeals the
statutes governing first lien mortgage lending, small loans, mortgage
rescue protection fraud, and home loan practices and the Uniform
Consumer Credit Code. Recodifies the repealed statutes in a new title
of the Indiana Code concerning consumer lending (Title 37). Conforms
the structure and organization of the recodified statutes to the
requirements of the general assembly's drafting manual. Makes
conforming changes to cross-references.
Effective: July 1, 2026.
Baldwin
January 5, 2026, read first time and referred to Committee on Insurance and Financial
Institutions.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
Introduced
Second Regular Session of the 124th General Assembly (2026)
PRINTING CODE. Amendments: Whenever an existing statute (or a section of the Indiana
Constitution) is being amended, the text of the existing provision will appear in this style type,
additions will appear in this style type, and deletions will appear in this style type.
Additions: Whenever a new statutory provision is being enacted (or a new constitutional
provision adopted), the text of the new provision will appear in this style type. Also, the
word NEW will appear in that style type in the introductory clause of each SECTION that adds
a new provision to the Indiana Code or the Indiana Constitution.
Conflict reconciliation: Text in a statute in this style type or this style type reconciles conflicts
between statutes enacted by the 2025 Regular Session of the General Assembly.
SENATE BILL No. 169
A BILL FOR AN ACT to amend the Indiana Code concerning trade
regulation.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the State of Indiana:

40 that dissolution of the restraining order will make collection of the
41 judgment more likely.

38 The court may conduct a hearing on the petition and may authorize the
39 auditor of the county to issue a warrant for the amount of the
40 reasonable expenses. The county treasurer shall pay the warrant
41 without an appropriation for the disbursement.

32 (4) by cashier's check;

36 (as defined in IC 4-8.1-2-7).
37 However, if a tax liability payment is made by bank draft, check,
38 cashier's check, or money order, the liability is not finally discharged
39 and the person has not paid the tax until the draft, check, or money
40 order has been honored by the institution on which it is drawn. If the
41 payment is made by credit card, debit card, charge card, or similar
42 method, the liability is not finally discharged and the person has not

10
1 paid the tax until the department receives payment or credit from the
2 institution responsible for making the payment or credit. The
3 department may contract with a bank or credit card vendor for
4 acceptance of bank or credit cards. However, if there is a vendor

6 charged directly to the department's account, the department or credit

17 SECTION 12. IC 6-8.1-8-8, AS AMENDED BY P.L.99-2011,
18 SECTION 3, IS AMENDED TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE
19 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 8. After a tax warrant becomes a judgment under
20 section 2 of this chapter, a tax warrant is returned uncollected to the
21 department under section 3 of this chapter, or the taxpayer does not pay
22 the amount demanded under section 2(b) of this chapter and the
23 taxpayer has taken an action under section 2(n) of this chapter to
24 foreclose the lien, the department may take any of the following actions
25 without judicial proceedings.
26 (1) The department may levy upon the property of the taxpayer
27 that is held by a financial institution by sending a claim to the
28 financial institution. Upon receipt of a claim under this
29 subdivision, the financial institution shall surrender to the
30 department the taxpayer's property. If the taxpayer's property
31 exceeds the amount owed to the state by the taxpayer, the
32 financial institution shall surrender the taxpayer's property in an
33 amount equal to the amount owed. After receiving the
34 department's notice of levy, the financial institution is required to
35 place a sixty (60) day hold on or restriction on the withdrawal of
36 funds the taxpayer has on deposit or subsequently deposits, in an
37 amount not to exceed the amount owed.
38 (2) The department may garnish the accrued earnings and wages
39 of a taxpayer by sending a notice to the taxpayer's employer. Upon
40 receipt of a notice under this subdivision, an employer shall
41 garnish the accrued earnings and wages of the taxpayer in an
42 amount equal to the full amount that is subject to garnishment
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
11
1 under IC 24-4.5-5. IC 37-2-6. The amount garnished shall be
2 remitted to the department. The employer is entitled to a fee in an
3 amount equal to the fee allowed under IC 24-4.5-5-105(5).
4 IC 37-2-6-4(e). However, the fee shall be borne entirely by the
5 taxpayer.
6 (3) The department may levy upon and sell property and may:
7 (A) take immediate possession of the property and store it in
8 a secure place; or
9 (B) leave the property in the custody of the taxpayer;
10 until the day of the sale. The department shall provide notice of
11 the sale in one (1) newspaper, as provided in IC 5-3-1-2. If the
12 property is left in the custody of the taxpayer, the department may
13 require the taxpayer to provide a joint and several delivery bond,
14 in an amount and with a surety acceptable to the department. At
15 any time before the sale, any owner or part owner of the property
16 may redeem the property from the judgment by paying the
17 department the amount of the judgment. The proceeds of the sale
18 shall be applied first to the collection expenses and second to the
19 payment of the delinquent taxes and penalties. Any balance
20 remaining shall be paid to the taxpayer.

1 estate, education, public accommodations, employment, or the

30 practice and if the alleged discriminatory practice is of a

32 discrimination are alleged to have occurred; and
33 (5) a statement as to any other action, civil or criminal, instituted
34 in any other form based upon the same grievance alleged in the
35 complaint, together with a statement as to the status or disposition
36 of the other action.
37 No complaint shall be valid unless filed within one hundred eighty
38 (180) days from the date of the occurrence of the alleged
39 discriminatory practice.

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
19
1 (1) it shall not be a discriminatory practice to maintain separate

13 particular business or enterprise; and
14 (3) it shall not be a discriminatory practice for a private or

17 (r) "Disabled" or "disability" means the physical or mental condition
18 of a person that constitutes a substantial disability. In reference to

37 JULY 1, 2026]: Sec. 3. (a) A supplier may not commit an unfair,
38 abusive, or deceptive act, omission, or practice in connection with a
39 consumer transaction. Such an act, omission, or practice by a supplier
40 is a violation of this chapter whether it occurs before, during, or after
41 the transaction. An act, omission, or practice prohibited by this section
42 includes both implicit and explicit misrepresentations.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
23
1 (b) Without limiting the scope of subsection (a), the following acts,
2 and the following representations as to the subject matter of a
3 consumer transaction, made orally, in writing, or by electronic
4 communication, by a supplier, are deceptive acts:
5 (1) That such subject of a consumer transaction has sponsorship,
6 approval, performance, characteristics, accessories, uses, or
7 benefits it does not have which the supplier knows or should
8 reasonably know it does not have.
9 (2) That such subject of a consumer transaction is of a particular
10 standard, quality, grade, style, or model, if it is not and if the
11 supplier knows or should reasonably know that it is not.
12 (3) That such subject of a consumer transaction is new or unused,
13 if it is not and if the supplier knows or should reasonably know
14 that it is not.
15 (4) That such subject of a consumer transaction will be supplied
16 to the public in greater quantity than the supplier intends or
17 reasonably expects.
18 (5) That replacement or repair constituting the subject of a
19 consumer transaction is needed, if it is not and if the supplier
20 knows or should reasonably know that it is not.
21 (6) That a specific price advantage exists as to such subject of a
22 consumer transaction, if it does not and if the supplier knows or
23 should reasonably know that it does not.
24 (7) That the supplier has a sponsorship, approval, or affiliation in
25 such consumer transaction the supplier does not have, and which
26 the supplier knows or should reasonably know that the supplier
27 does not have.
28 (8) That such consumer transaction involves or does not involve
29 a warranty, a disclaimer of warranties, or other rights, remedies,
30 or obligations, if the representation is false and if the supplier
31 knows or should reasonably know that the representation is false.
32 (9) That the consumer will receive a rebate, discount, or other
33 benefit as an inducement for entering into a sale or lease in return

24
1 presumption that the supplier has represented that the supplier
2 will deliver or complete the subject of the consumer transaction
3 within a reasonable time, according to the course of dealing or the
4 usage of the trade.
5 (11) That the consumer will be able to purchase the subject of the
6 consumer transaction as advertised by the supplier, if the supplier
7 does not intend to sell it.
8 (12) That the replacement or repair constituting the subject of a
9 consumer transaction can be made by the supplier for the estimate
10 the supplier gives a customer for the replacement or repair, if the
11 specified work is completed and:
12 (A) the cost exceeds the estimate by an amount equal to or
13 greater than ten percent (10%) of the estimate;
14 (B) the supplier did not obtain written permission from the
15 customer to authorize the supplier to complete the work even
16 if the cost would exceed the amounts specified in clause (A);
17 (C) the total cost for services and parts for a single transaction
18 is more than seven hundred fifty dollars ($750); and
19 (D) the supplier knew or reasonably should have known that
20 the cost would exceed the estimate in the amounts specified in
21 clause (A).
22 (13) That the replacement or repair constituting the subject of a
23 consumer transaction is needed, and that the supplier disposes of
24 the part repaired or replaced earlier than seventy-two (72) hours
25 after both:
26 (A) the customer has been notified that the work has been
27 completed; and
28 (B) the part repaired or replaced has been made available for
29 examination upon the request of the customer.
30 (14) Engaging in the replacement or repair of the subject of a
31 consumer transaction if the consumer has not authorized the
32 replacement or repair, and if the supplier knows or should
33 reasonably know that it is not authorized.
34 (15) The act of misrepresenting the geographic location of the
35 supplier by listing an alternate business name or an assumed
36 business name (as described in IC 23-0.5-3-4) in a local telephone
37 directory if:
38 (A) the name misrepresents the supplier's geographic location;
39 (B) the listing fails to identify the locality and state of the
40 supplier's business;
41 (C) calls to the local telephone number are routinely forwarded
42 or otherwise transferred to a supplier's business location that
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
25
1 is outside the calling area covered by the local telephone
2 directory; and
3 (D) the supplier's business location is located in a county that
4 is not contiguous to a county in the calling area covered by the
5 local telephone directory.
6 (16) The act of listing an alternate business name or assumed
7 business name (as described in IC 23-0.5-3-4) in a directory

9 (A) the name misrepresents the supplier's geographic location;

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
27
1 (d) If a supplier shows by a preponderance of the evidence that an
2 act resulted from a bona fide error notwithstanding the maintenance of
3 procedures reasonably adopted to avoid the error, such act shall not be
4 deceptive within the meaning of this chapter.
5 (e) It shall be a defense to any action brought under this chapter that
6 the representation constituting an alleged deceptive act was one made
7 in good faith by the supplier without knowledge of its falsity and in
8 reliance upon the oral or written representations of the manufacturer,
9 the person from whom the supplier acquired the product, any testing
10 organization, or any other person provided that the source thereof is
11 disclosed to the consumer.
12 (f) For purposes of subsection (b)(12), a supplier that provides
13 estimates before performing repair or replacement work for a customer
14 shall give the customer a written estimate itemizing as closely as
15 possible the price for labor and parts necessary for the specific job
16 before commencing the work.
17 (g) For purposes of subsection (b)(15) and (b)(16), a telephone
18 company or other provider of a telephone directory or directory
19 assistance service or its officer or agent is immune from liability for
20 publishing the listing of an alternate business name or assumed
21 business name of a supplier in its directory or directory assistance data
22 base unless the telephone company or other provider of a telephone
23 directory or directory assistance service is the same person as the
24 supplier who has committed the deceptive act.
25 (h) For purposes of subsection (b)(18), it is an affirmative defense
26 to any action brought under this chapter that the product has been
27 altered by a person other than the defendant to render the product
28 completely incapable of serving its original purpose.

28 transaction has any improvements, appurtenances, uses,
29 characteristics, or associated benefits that it does not have and
30 that the person knows or reasonably should know it does not
31 have.

40 (iv) A savings bank.

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

1 mortgage transaction.
2 (D) A closing agent (as defined in IC 27-7-3.7-1).
3 (7) Fail to provide the notice required under subsection (d), within
4 the time specified in subsection (d), if the person is a seller in a
5 real estate transaction described in subsection (d).
6 (d) This subsection applies to a real estate transaction that involves
7 a land contract between the seller and the buyer in the transaction. If
8 the real estate that is the subject of the transaction is subject to any
9 encumbrance, including any tax lien, foreclosure action, legal
10 judgment, or other encumbrance affecting the title to the real estate, the
11 seller must provide written notice by certified mail, return receipt
12 requested, of the encumbrance to the buyer:
13 (1) not later than the time the land contract is executed, if the
14 encumbrance is created before or at the time the land contract is
15 executed; or
16 (2) not later than ten (10) business days after the encumbrance is
17 created, if the encumbrance is created after the land contract is
18 executed.

31 (2) protects the personal records from misuse.

35 requirements under IC 24-4.9-3-4.
36 (i) A person to whom this section applies may not dispose of
37 personal records without first:
38 (1) shredding, incinerating, or mutilating the personal records; or
39 (2) erasing or otherwise rendering illegible or unusable the
40 personal information contained in the records.

42 the person shall, as part of the winding up of the business, safeguard

47
1 any personal records maintained by the person in accordance with this

32 company shall, for the purpose of this subdivision, be considered
33 to be unimpaired so long as the sound value of its assets over and
34 above its liabilities, exclusive of liabilities for capital notes,
35 debentures, and capital stock, as determined by the department,
36 equals or exceeds the minimum capital or capital stock required
37 by the department for a bank or trust company);
38 (8) has neglected or refused, for a period of thirty (30) days, to
39 comply with the terms of a duly issued order of the department,
40 essential to preserve the solvency of the financial institution;
41 (9) has failed to pay the fees charged by the department under
42 IC 28-11-3-5 after due notice of the amount of the fee has been
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
48
1 given;
2 (10) has breached a fiduciary duty under IC 30-4-3-6; or
3 (11) has violated IC 30-4-3-7 in a way that has caused or may
4 cause harm to fiduciary accounts.
5 (b) When the department makes a determination to take possession
6 of the business and property of a financial institution under subsection
7 (a), the department shall:
8 (1) make a finding to that effect and enter that finding on the
9 records of the proceedings of the department; and
10 (2) cause a certified copy of the finding to be served on the
11 president or other executive officer actively in charge of the
12 financial institution and demand possession of the business,
13 property, and records of the financial institution from the officer.
14 The financial institution shall immediately surrender the
15 possession to the department.
16 (c) The department or its receiver is not required to become the
17 owner of any property to fulfill the liquidation requirements of this
18 chapter.

25 parents, children, siblings, mothers-in-law and fathers-in-law,

11 income. The term does not include:
12 (A) a customer of the person; or
13 (B) any other person whose obligation to make a payment of
14 salary, wages, compensation, or other income to a consumer is
15 not based on the provision of services by the consumer for or
16 on behalf of that person.
17 (9) "Employer integrated wage access services" means the
18 business by a provider of delivering to a consumer access to
19 earned but unpaid income on the basis of:
20 (A) employment;
21 (B) income; or
22 (C) attendance;
23 data obtained directly or indirectly from an employer.
24 (10) "Federally insured depository financial institution" means:
25 (A) a bank;
26 (B) a credit union;
27 (C) a savings and loan association;
28 (D) a trust company;
29 (E) a corporate fiduciary;
30 (F) a savings association;
31 (G) a savings bank;
32 (H) an industrial bank; or
33 (I) an industrial loan company;
34 that is organized under the law of the United States or any state of
35 the United States and that has federally or privately insured
36 deposits as permitted by state or federal law.
37 (11) "Fee" includes the following, however denominated:
38 (A) An amount charged by a provider for:
39 (i) expedited delivery; or
40 (ii) other delivery;
41 of proceeds to a consumer.
42 (B) A subscription or membership fee imposed by a provider
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
61
1 for a bona fide group of services that include earned wage
2 access services.
3 (C) An amount that:
4 (i) is paid by an employer to a provider on a consumer's
5 behalf; and
6 (ii) entitles the consumer to receive proceeds at reduced or
7 no cost to the consumer.
8 The term does not include a voluntary tip, gratuity, or donation
9 paid to a provider.
10 (12) "Individual" means a natural person.
11 (13) "Key individual" means an individual ultimately responsible
12 for establishing or directing policies and procedures of a licensee,
13 such as an executive officer, manager, director, or trustee.
14 (14) "Licensee" means a person licensed under this chapter.
15 (15) "NMLSR" means the Nationwide Multistate Licensing
16 System and Registry:
17 (A) developed by the Conference of State Bank Supervisors
18 and the American Association of Residential Mortgage
19 Regulators; and
20 (B) owned and operated by the State Regulatory Registry,
21 LLC, or by any successor or affiliated entity;
22 for the licensing and registry of persons in financial services
23 industries.
24 (16) "Outstanding proceeds" means proceeds that:
25 (A) have been paid to a consumer by a provider; and
26 (B) have not yet been repaid to the provider.
27 (17) "Passive investor" means a person that:
28 (A) does not have the power to elect a majority of key
29 individuals or executive officers, managers, directors, trustees,
30 or other persons exercising managerial authority over a person
31 in control of a licensee;
32 (B) is not employed by and does not have any managerial
33 duties with respect to the licensee or a person in control of the
34 licensee;
35 (C) does not have the power to exercise, directly or indirectly,
36 a controlling influence over the management or policies of the
37 licensee or a person in control of the licensee; and
38 (D) either:
39 (i) attests to as facts the characteristics of passivity set forth
40 in clauses (A) through (C), in a form and by a medium
41 prescribed by the director; or
42 (ii) commits to the characteristics of passivity set forth in
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
62
1 clauses (A) through (C) in a written document.
2 (18) "Person" means any individual, general partnership, limited
3 partnership, limited liability company, corporation, trust,
4 association, joint stock corporation, or other corporate entity, as
5 so identified by the director.
6 (19) "Proceeds" means a payment that:
7 (A) is made to a consumer by a provider; and
8 (B) is based on earned but unpaid income.
9 (20) "Provider" means a person in the business of offering and
10 providing earned wage access services to consumers. The term
11 does not include the following:
12 (A) A service provider that is not contractually obligated to
13 fund proceeds delivered as part of the earned wage access
14 services, such as a payroll service provider that verifies a
15 consumer's available earnings.
16 (B) An employer that offers a portion of salary, wages,
17 compensation, or other income directly to its employees or
18 independent contractors before the normally scheduled pay
19 date.
20 (C) An entity that offers or provides earned wage access
21 services and reports a consumer's payment or nonpayment of
22 either outstanding proceeds of the earned wage access services
23 or fees, voluntary tips, gratuities, or other donations in
24 connection with the earned wage access services to a
25 consumer reporting agency (as defined in the Federal Fair
26 Credit Reporting Act (15 U.S.C. 1681 et seq.)).
27 (21) "Small loan" has the meaning set forth in IC 24-4.5-7-104.

33 chapter shall do the following:
34 (1) Develop and implement policies and procedures to:

36 (B) address complaints from consumers;
37 in an expedient manner.

40 (A) offer that consumer at least one (1) reasonable option to

42 (B) clearly explain to the consumer how to elect each no cost
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
65
1 option offered;

4 (i) color;
5 (ii) font;

7 (iv) general location;

11 associated with the delivery of the proceeds is not the default

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
66
1 other donation:
2 (i) is voluntary; and

23 (A) IC 6-1.1-22-2(c).
24 (B) IC 8-23-7.
25 (C) IC 8-23-23.
26 (D) IC 10-17-2-3.
27 (E) IC 10-17-3-2.
28 (F) IC 12-14-13.
29 (G) IC 12-14-16.
30 (j) The state and its agencies and instrumentalities are required to
31 pay the recording fees and charges that this section prescribes.
32 (k) This subsection applies to a county other than a county
33 containing a consolidated city. The county treasurer shall distribute
34 money collected by the county recorder under subsection (c)(9) as
35 follows:
36 (1) Sixty percent (60%) of the money collected by the county
37 recorder under subsection (c)(9) shall be distributed to the units
38 in the county that have established an affordable housing fund
39 under IC 5-20-5-15.5 for deposit in the fund. The amount to be
40 distributed to a unit is the amount available for distribution
41 multiplied by a fraction. The numerator of the fraction is the
42 population of the unit. The denominator of the fraction is the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
85
1 population of all units in the county that have established an
2 affordable housing fund. The population to be used for a county
3 that establishes an affordable housing fund is the population of
4 the county outside any city or town that has established an
5 affordable housing fund.
6 (2) Forty percent (40%) of the money collected by the county
7 recorder under subsection (c)(9) shall be distributed to the
8 treasurer of state for deposit in the affordable housing and
9 community development fund established under IC 5-20-4-7 for
10 the purposes of the fund.
11 Money shall be distributed under this subsection before the sixteenth
12 day of the month following the month in which the money is collected
13 from the county recorder.
14 (l) This subsection applies to a county described in subsection
15 (c)(10). The county treasurer shall distribute money collected by the
16 county recorder under subsection (c)(10) as follows:
17 (1) Sixty percent (60%) of the money collected by the county
18 recorder under subsection (c)(10) shall be deposited in the
19 housing trust fund established under IC 36-7-15.1-35.5(e) for the
20 purposes of the fund.
21 (2) Forty percent (40%) of the money collected by the county
22 recorder under subsection (c)(10) shall be distributed to the
23 treasurer of state for deposit in the affordable housing and
24 community development fund established under IC 5-20-4-7 for
25 the purposes of the fund.
26 Money shall be distributed under this subsection before the sixteenth
27 day of the month following the month in which the money is collected
28 from the county recorder.
29 (m) The county recorder may also include a cross-reference or
30 multiple cross-references identified in a document for recording under
31 this section. For cross-references not otherwise required by statute or
32 county ordinance, the person submitting the document for recording
33 shall clearly identify on the front page of the instrument the specific
34 cross-reference or cross-references to be included with the recorded
35 documents.

9 SECTION 98. IC 37 IS ADDED TO THE INDIANA CODE AS A
10 NEW TITLE TO READ AS FOLLOWS [EFFECTIVE JULY 1, 2026]:
11 TITLE 37. CONSUMER LENDING
12 ARTICLE 1. FIRST LIEN MORTGAGE LENDING
13 Chapter 1. General Provisions
14 Sec. 1. Notwithstanding any other provision of this article or
15 IC 37-2, the department may adopt rules under IC 4-22-2, to
16 remain effective until codified in the Indiana Code, in order to
17 provide for a system of licensing creditors and mortgage loan
18 originators that meets the requirements of:
19 (1) the Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing
20 Act of 2008 (H.R. 3221 Title V) and the interpretations of that
21 Act issued by the Secretary of Housing and Urban
22 Development and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau;
23 and
24 (2) the subsequent amendment of the Secure and Fair
25 Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 by the
26 Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief, and Consumer
27 Protection Act (P.L. 115-174, 132 Stat. 1296).
28 Sec. 2. (a) This article shall be liberally construed and applied
29 to promote the following purposes and policies:
30 (1) To permit and encourage the development of fair and
31 economically sound first lien mortgage lending practices.
32 (2) To conform the regulation of first lien mortgage lending
33 practices to applicable state and federal laws, rules,
34 regulations, policies, and guidance.
35 (b) A reference to a requirement imposed by this article includes
36 reference to a related rule of the department adopted under this
37 article.
38 (c) A reference to a federal law in this article is a reference to
39 the law as in effect December 31, 2024.
40 Sec. 3. This article is a general statute intended as a unified
41 coverage of its subject matter. No part of this article may be
42 considered to be impliedly repealed by subsequent legislation if
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
89
1 such construction can reasonably be avoided.
2 Sec. 4. The provisions of this article are severable, so that if:
3 (1) any provisions of this article; or
4 (2) the application of this article to any person or
5 circumstances;
6 is held invalid, the invalidity does not affect other provisions or
7 applications of this article that can be given effect without the
8 invalid provision or application.
9 Sec. 5. (a) Except as provided in subsection (b), this article
10 applies to a first lien mortgage transaction:
11 (1) that is secured by an interest in:
12 (A) a dwelling; or
13 (B) residential real estate upon which a dwelling is
14 constructed or intended to be constructed;
15 in Indiana; and
16 (2) the closing for which takes place after June 30, 2026.
17 (b) This article does not apply to a first lien mortgage
18 transaction if:
19 (1) the debtor is not a resident of Indiana at the time the
20 transaction is entered into; and
21 (2) the laws of the debtor's state of residence require that the
22 transaction be made under the laws of the state of the debtor's
23 residence.
24 Sec. 6. This article does not apply to the following:
25 (1) Extensions of credit to government or governmental
26 agencies or instrumentalities.
27 (2) A first lien mortgage transaction in which the debt is
28 incurred primarily for a purpose other than a personal,
29 family, or household purpose.
30 (3) An extension of credit primarily for a business, a
31 commercial, or an agricultural purpose.
32 (4) Except for IC 37-1-3-4(b), IC 37-1-3-8, IC 37-1-3-19(d),
33 and IC 37-1-3-19(e), a first lien mortgage transaction made:
34 (A) in compliance with the requirements of; and
35 (B) by a community development corporation (as defined
36 in IC 4-4-28-2) acting as a subrecipient of funds from;
37 the Indiana housing and community development authority
38 established by IC 5-20-1-3.
39 (5) Except for IC 37-1-3-4(b), IC 37-1-3-8, IC 37-1-3-19(d),
40 and IC 37-1-3-19(e), a first lien mortgage transaction made by
41 an entity that exclusively uses funds provided by the United
42 States Department of Housing and Urban Development under
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
90
1 Title 1 of the federal Housing and Community Development
2 Act of 1974, Public Law 93-383, as amended (42 U.S.C. 5301
3 et seq.).
4 (6) An extension of credit originated by the following:
5 (A) A depository institution.
6 (B) Subsidiaries that are not licensed under this article and
7 that are:
8 (i) owned and controlled by a depository institution; and
9 (ii) regulated by a federal banking agency.
10 (C) An institution regulated by the Farm Credit
11 Administration.
12 (7) Except for IC 37-1-3-4(b), IC 37-1-3-8, IC 37-1-3-19(d),
13 and IC 37-1-3-19(e), a credit union service organization that
14 is majority owned, directly or indirectly, by one (1) or more
15 credit unions.
16 (8) A person that does not engage as a creditor in first lien

38 (D) Agrees to comply with all law, rules, directives, and

40 mortgage loan originators sponsored by the person, as the
41 director determines necessary to ensure compliance with
42 the federal Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage

2 Indiana law.
3 (9) A first lien mortgage transaction originated by a

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

1 (A) Subject to clause (B), the organization originates only
2 one (1) or both of the following types of mortgage
3 transactions:
4 (i) Zero (0) interest first lien mortgage transactions.

6 transactions.

21 to originate the mortgage transactions described in clause
22 (A).

26 (16) A bona fide nonprofit organization if the following
27 criteria are satisfied:
28 (A) For each calendar year that the organization seeks the
29 exemption provided by this subdivision, the organization
30 certifies, not later than December 31 of the preceding
31 calendar year and on a form prescribed by the director
32 and accompanied by such documentation as required by
33 the director, that the organization is a bona fide nonprofit
34 organization.
35 (B) The director determines that the organization
36 originates only mortgage transactions that are favorable to
37 the debtor. For purposes of this clause, a mortgage
38 transaction is favorable to the debtor if the director
39 determines that the terms of the mortgage transaction are
40 consistent with terms of mortgage transactions made in a
41 public or charitable context, rather than in a commercial
42 context.

1 Sec. 7. (a) If a person licensed or required to be licensed by the

29 regulated under this article, also conduct activities of a loan broker
30 subject to IC 23-2.5.
31 Sec. 9. A licensee may carry on other business at a location
32 where the licensee engages in first lien mortgage transactions
33 unless the licensee carries on other business for the purpose of
34 evasion or violation of this article.
35 Chapter 2. Definitions
36 Sec. 1. The definitions set forth in this chapter apply throughout
37 this article.
38 Sec. 2. "Affiliate", with respect to any person subject to this
39 article, means a person that, directly or indirectly, through one (1)
40 or more intermediaries:
41 (1) controls;
42 (2) is controlled by; or
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
94
1 (3) is under common control with;
2 the person subject to this article.
3 Sec. 3. "Agreement" means the bargain of the parties in fact as
4 found in the parties' language or by implication from other
5 circumstances, including course of dealing or usage of trade or
6 course of performance.
7 Sec. 4. "Agricultural products" includes agricultural products,
8 horticultural products, viticultural products, dairy products,
9 livestock, wildlife, poultry, bees, forest products, fish and shellfish,
10 any products raised or produced on farms, and any products
11 processed or manufactured from products raised or produced on
12 farms.
13 Sec. 5. "Agricultural purpose" means a purpose related to the
14 production, harvest, exhibition, marketing, transportation,
15 processing, or manufacture of agricultural products by a natural
16 person who cultivates, plants, propagates, or nurtures the
17 agricultural products.
18 Sec. 6. "Balloon payment", with respect to a mortgage
19 transaction, means any payment:
20 (1) that the creditor requires the debtor to make at any time
21 during the term of the mortgage;
22 (2) that represents the entire amount of the outstanding
23 balance with respect to the mortgage; and
24 (3) the entire amount of which is due as of a specified date or
25 at the end of a specified period;
26 if the aggregate amount of the minimum periodic payments
27 required under the mortgage would not fully amortize the
28 outstanding balance by the specified date or at the end of the
29 specified period. The term does not include a payment required by
30 a creditor under a due-on-sale clause (as defined in 12 U.S.C.
31 1701j-3(a)) or a payment required by a creditor under a provision
32 in the mortgage that permits the creditor to accelerate the debt
33 upon the debtor's default or failure to abide by the material terms
34 of the mortgage.
35 Sec. 7. "Bona fide nonprofit organization" means an
36 organization that does the following, as determined by the director,
37 under criteria established by the director:
38 (1) Maintains tax exempt status under Section 501(c)(3) of the
39 Internal Revenue Code.
40 (2) Promotes affordable housing or provides home ownership
41 education or similar services.
42 (3) Conducts the organization's activities in a manner that
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
95
1 serves public or charitable purposes.
2 (4) Receives funding and revenue and charges fees in a
3 manner that does not encourage the organization or the
4 organization's employees to act other than in the best interests
5 of the organization's clients.
6 (5) Compensates the organization's employees in a manner
7 that does not encourage employees to act other than in the
8 best interests of the organization's clients.
9 (6) Provides to, or identifies for, debtors mortgage
10 transactions with terms that are favorable to the debtor (as
11 described in IC 37-1-1-6(16)) and comparable to mortgage
12 transactions and housing assistance provided under
13 government housing assistance programs.
14 (7) Maintains certification by the United States Department
15 of Housing and Urban Development or employs counselors
16 who are certified by the Indiana housing and community
17 development authority.
18 Sec. 8. "Civil court" means any court in Indiana having
19 jurisdiction of civil cases.
20 Sec. 9. "Consumer credit sale" is a sale of goods, services, or an
21 interest in land in which:
22 (1) credit is granted by a person who engages as a seller in
23 credit transactions of the same kind;
24 (2) the buyer is a person other than an organization;
25 (3) the goods, services, or interest in land are purchased
26 primarily for a personal, family, or household purpose;
27 (4) either the debt is payable in installments or a credit service
28 charge is made; and
29 (5) with respect to a sale of goods or services, either:
30 (A) the amount of credit extended, the written credit limit,
31 or the initial advance does not exceed the exempt threshold
32 amount, as adjusted in accordance with the annual
33 adjustment of the exempt threshold amount, specified in
34 Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as
35 applicable); or
36 (B) the debt is secured by personal property used or
37 expected to be used as the principal dwelling of the buyer.
38 Sec. 10. "Credit" means the right granted by a creditor to a
39 debtor to defer payment of debt or to incur debt and defer its
40 payment.
41 Sec. 11. "Creditor" means a person:
42 (1) that regularly engages in the extension of first lien
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
96
1 mortgage transactions that are subject to a credit service
2 charge or loan finance charge, as applicable, or are payable
3 by written agreement in more than four (4) installments (not
4 including a down payment); and
5 (2) to which the obligation is initially payable, either on the
6 face of the note or contract, or by agreement if there is not a
7 note or contract.
8 The term does not include a person described in section 39 of this
9 chapter in a tablefunded transaction. A creditor may be an
10 individual, a limited liability company, a sole proprietorship, a
11 partnership, a trust, a joint venture, a corporation, an
12 unincorporated organization, or other form of entity, however
13 organized.
14 Sec. 12. "Department" refers to the department of financial
15 institutions.
16 Sec. 13. "Depository institution" has the meaning set forth in
17 the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (12 U.S.C. 1813(c)) and includes
18 any credit union.
19 Sec. 14. "Director" refers to the director of the department of
20 financial institutions or the director's designee.
21 Sec. 15. "Dwelling" means a residential structure that contains
22 one (1) to four (4) units, regardless of whether the structure is
23 attached to real property. The term includes an individual:
24 (1) condominium unit;
25 (2) cooperative unit;
26 (3) mobile home; or
27 (4) trailer;
28 that is used as a residence.
29 Sec. 16. "Employee" means an individual who is paid wages or
30 other compensation by an employer required under federal income
31 tax law to file Form W-2 on behalf of the individual.
32 Sec. 17. "Federal banking agencies" means the Board of
33 Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the
34 Comptroller of the Currency, the Office of Thrift Supervision, the
35 National Credit Union Administration, and the Federal Deposit
36 Insurance Corporation.
37 Sec. 18. "First lien mortgage transaction" means:
38 (1) a consumer loan; or
39 (2) a consumer credit sale;
40 that is or will be used by the debtor primarily for personal, family,
41 or household purposes and that is secured by a mortgage or a land
42 contract (or another consensual security interest equivalent to a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
97
1 mortgage or a land contract) that constitutes a first lien on a
2 dwelling or on residential real estate upon which a dwelling is
3 constructed or intended to be constructed.
4 Sec. 19. "Immediate family member" means a spouse, child,
5 sibling, parent, grandparent, or grandchild. The term includes
6 stepparents, stepchildren, stepsiblings, and adoptive relationships.
7 Sec. 20. "Individual" means a natural person.
8 Sec. 21. "Land contract" means a contract for the sale of real
9 estate in which the seller of the real estate retains legal title to the
10 real estate until the total contract price is paid by the buyer.
11 Sec. 22. "Licensee" means a person licensed to engage in
12 mortgage transactions as a creditor.
13 Sec. 23. "Loan" includes:
14 (1) the creation of debt by:
15 (A) the creditor's payment of or agreement to pay money
16 to the debtor or to a third party for the account of the
17 debtor; or
18 (B) the extension of credit by a person who engages as a
19 seller in credit transactions primarily secured by an
20 interest in land;
21 (2) the creation of debt by a credit to an account with the
22 creditor upon which the debtor is entitled to draw
23 immediately; and
24 (3) the forbearance of debt arising from a loan.
25 Sec. 24. "Loan brokerage business" means any activity in which
26 a person, in return for any consideration from any source,
27 procures, attempts to procure, or assists in procuring, a mortgage
28 transaction from a third party or any other person, whether or not
29 the person seeking the mortgage transaction actually obtains the
30 mortgage transaction.
31 Sec. 25. "Loan processor or underwriter" means an individual
32 who performs clerical or support duties as an employee at the
33 direction of, and subject to the supervision and instruction of, a
34 person licensed to engage in mortgage transactions or a person
35 exempt from licensing. For purposes of this section, the term
36 "clerical or support duties" may include, after the receipt of an
37 application, the following:
38 (1) The receipt, collection, distribution, and analysis of
39 information common for the processing or underwriting of a
40 mortgage transaction.
41 (2) The communication with a consumer to obtain the
42 information necessary for the processing or underwriting of
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
98
1 a loan, to the extent that the communication does not include:
2 (A) offering or negotiating loan rates or terms; or
3 (B) counseling consumers about mortgage transaction
4 rates or terms.
5 The term does not include an individual who is an employee of a
6 person that is not engaged in mortgage transactions as a creditor
7 if that person is permitted to voluntarily register with the
8 department to sponsor the individual under IC 37-1-1-6(8) to
9 engage solely in the activities described in this subdivision.
10 Sec. 26. "Mortgage loan originator" means an individual who,
11 for compensation or gain, or in the expectation of compensation or
12 gain, regularly engages in taking a mortgage transaction
13 application or in offering or negotiating the terms of a mortgage
14 transaction that either is made under this article or under IC 37-2
15 is made by an employee of a person licensed to engage in mortgage
16 transactions or by an employee of a person that is exempt from
17 licensing, while the employee is engaging in the loan brokerage
18 business. The term does not include the following:
19 (1) An individual engaged solely as a loan processor or
20 underwriter as long as the individual works exclusively as an
21 employee of a person licensed to engage in mortgage
22 transactions or as an employee of a person exempt from
23 licensing. However, the term includes an individual who is
24 licensed as a mortgage loan originator under this article and
25 750 IAC 9-3 and who is an employee of a person that is not
26 engaged in mortgage transactions as a creditor if that person
27 voluntarily registers with the department to sponsor the
28 individual under IC 37-1-1-6(8) to engage solely as a third
29 party processor or underwriter.
30 (2) Unless the person or entity is compensated by:
31 (A) a creditor;
32 (B) a loan broker;
33 (C) another mortgage loan originator; or
34 (D) any agent of a creditor, a loan broker, or another
35 mortgage loan originator described in clauses (A) through
36 (C);
37 a person or entity that performs only real estate brokerage
38 activities and is licensed or registered in accordance with
39 applicable state law.
40 (3) A person solely involved in extensions of credit relating to
41 timeshare plans (as defined in 11 U.S.C. 101(53D)).
42 Sec. 27. "Mortgage servicer" means the last person to whom a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
99
1 mortgagor or the mortgagor's successor in interest has been
2 instructed by a mortgagee to send payments on a loan secured by
3 a mortgage.
4 Sec. 28. "Mortgage transaction" means:
5 (1) a consumer loan; or
6 (2) a consumer credit sale;
7 that is or will be used by the debtor primarily for personal, family,
8 or household purposes and that is secured by a mortgage or a land
9 contract (or another consensual security interest equivalent to a
10 mortgage or a land contract) on a dwelling or on residential real
11 estate upon which a dwelling is constructed or intended to be
12 constructed.
13 Sec. 29. "Nationwide Multistate Licensing System and Registry"
14 (or "Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry" or
15 "NMLSR") means a multistate licensing system owned and
16 operated by the State Regulatory Registry, LLC, or by any
17 successor or affiliated entity, for the licensing and registration of
18 creditors, mortgage loan originators, and other persons in the
19 mortgage or financial services industries. The term includes any
20 other name or acronym that may be assigned to the system by the
21 State Regulatory Registry, LLC, or by any successor or affiliated
22 entity.
23 Sec. 30. "Organization" means a corporation, a government or
24 government subdivision, an agency, a trust, an estate, a
25 partnership, a limited liability company, a cooperative, an
26 association, a joint venture, an unincorporated organization, or
27 any other entity, however organized.
28 Sec. 31. "Payable in installments", with respect to a debt or an
29 obligation, means that payment is required or permitted by written
30 agreement to be made in more than four (4) installments not
31 including a down payment.
32 Sec. 32. "Person" includes an individual or an organization.
33 Sec. 33. "Principal" of a mortgage transaction means the total
34 of:
35 (1) the net amount paid to, receivable by, or paid or payable
36 for the account of the debtor; and
37 (2) to the extent that payment is deferred, amounts actually
38 paid or to be paid by the creditor for registration, certificate
39 of title, or license fees if not included in subdivision (1).
40 Sec. 34. "Real estate brokerage activity" means any activity that
41 involves offering or providing real estate brokerage services to the
42 public, including the following:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
100
1 (1) Acting as a real estate agent or real estate broker for a
2 buyer, seller, lessor, or lessee of real property.
3 (2) Bringing together parties interested in the sale, purchase,
4 lease, rental, or exchange of real property.
5 (3) Negotiating, on behalf of any party, any part of a contract
6 relating to the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange of real
7 property (other than in connection with providing financing
8 with respect to the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange
9 of real property).
10 (4) Engaging in any activity for which a person engaged in the
11 activity is required to be registered or licensed as a real estate
12 agent or real estate broker under any applicable law.
13 (5) Offering to engage in any activity, or act in any capacity,
14 described in this subsection.
15 Sec. 35. "Registered mortgage loan originator" means any
16 individual who:
17 (1) meets the definition of mortgage loan originator and is an
18 employee of:
19 (A) a depository institution;
20 (B) a subsidiary that is owned and controlled by a
21 depository institution and regulated by a federal banking
22 agency; or
23 (C) an institution regulated by the Farm Credit
24 Administration; and
25 (2) is registered with, and maintains a unique identifier
26 through, the NMLSR.
27 Sec. 36. "Regularly engaged", with respect to a person who
28 extends or originates first lien mortgage transactions, refers to a
29 person who:
30 (1) extended or originated more than five (5) first lien
31 mortgage transactions in the preceding calendar year; or
32 (2) extends or originates, or will extend or originate, more
33 than five (5) first lien mortgage transactions in the current
34 calendar year if the person did not extend or originate more
35 than five (5) first lien mortgage transactions in the preceding
36 calendar year.
37 Sec. 37. "Residential real estate" means any real property that
38 is located in Indiana and on which there is located or intended to
39 be constructed a dwelling.
40 Sec. 38. "Revolving first lien mortgage transaction" means a
41 first lien mortgage transaction in which:
42 (1) the creditor permits the debtor to obtain advances from
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
101
1 time to time;
2 (2) the unpaid balances of principal, finance charges, and
3 other appropriate charges are debited to an account; and
4 (3) the debtor has the privilege of paying the balances in
5 installments.
6 Sec. 39. "Tablefunded" means a transaction in which:
7 (1) a person closes a first lien mortgage transaction in the
8 person's own name as a mortgagee with funds provided by
9 one (1) or more other persons; and
10 (2) the transaction is assigned, not later than one (1) business
11 day after the funding of the transaction, to the mortgage
12 creditor providing the funding.
13 Sec. 40. "Unique identifier" means a number or other identifier
14 assigned by protocols established by the NMLSR.
15 Chapter 3. Miscellaneous Provisions
16 Sec. 1. (a) A creditor or mortgage servicer shall provide, in
17 writing, an accurate payoff amount for a first lien mortgage
18 transaction to the debtor not later than seven (7) business days
19 (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and Sundays) after the
20 creditor or mortgage servicer receives the debtor's written request
21 for the accurate payoff amount. A payoff statement provided by a
22 creditor or mortgage servicer under this subsection must show the
23 date the statement was prepared and itemize the unpaid principal
24 balance and each fee, charge, or other sum included within the
25 payoff amount. A creditor or mortgage servicer who fails to
26 provide an accurate payoff amount is liable for:
27 (1) one hundred dollars ($100) if an accurate payoff amount
28 is not provided by the creditor or mortgage servicer not later
29 than seven (7) business days (excluding legal public holidays,
30 Saturdays, and Sundays) after the creditor or mortgage
31 servicer receives the debtor's first written request; and
32 (2) the greater of:
33 (A) one hundred dollars ($100); or
34 (B) the loan finance charge that accrues on the first lien
35 mortgage transaction from the date the creditor or
36 mortgage servicer receives the first written request until
37 the date on which the accurate payoff amount is provided;
38 if an accurate payoff amount is not provided by the creditor
39 or mortgage servicer not later than seven (7) business days
40 (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and Sundays)
41 after the creditor or mortgage servicer receives the debtor's
42 second written request, and the creditor or mortgage servicer
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
102
1 fails to comply with subdivision (1).
2 (b) This subsection applies to a first lien mortgage transaction,
3 or the refinancing or consolidation of a first lien mortgage
4 transaction, that:
5 (1) is closed after June 30, 2009; and
6 (2) has an interest rate that is subject to change at one (1) or
7 more times during the term of the first lien mortgage
8 transaction.
9 A creditor in a transaction to which this subsection applies may not
10 contract for and may not charge the debtor a prepayment fee or
11 penalty.
12 (c) This subsection applies to a first lien mortgage transaction
13 with respect to which any installment or minimum payment due is
14 delinquent for at least sixty (60) days. The creditor, servicer, or the
15 creditor's agent shall acknowledge a written offer made in
16 connection with a proposed short sale not later than five (5)
17 business days (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and
18 Sundays) after the date of the offer if the offer complies with the
19 requirements for a qualified written request set forth in 12 U.S.C.
20 2605(e)(1)(B). The creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent is required
21 to acknowledge a written offer made in connection with a proposed
22 short sale from a third party acting on behalf of the debtor only if
23 the debtor has provided written authorization for the creditor,
24 servicer, or creditor's agent to do so. Not later than thirty (30)
25 business days (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and
26 Sundays) after receipt of an offer under this subsection, the
27 creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent shall respond to the offer
28 with an acceptance or a rejection of the offer. The thirty (30) day
29 period described in this subsection may be extended for not more
30 than fifteen (15) business days (excluding legal public holidays,
31 Saturdays, and Sundays) if, before the end of the thirty (30) day
32 period, the creditor, the servicer, or the creditor's agent notifies the
33 debtor of the extension and the reason the extension is needed.
34 Payment accepted by a creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent in
35 connection with a short sale constitutes payment in full satisfaction
36 of the first lien mortgage transaction unless the creditor, servicer,
37 or creditor's agent obtains:
38 (1) the following statement: "The debtor remains liable for
39 any amount still owed under the first lien mortgage
40 transaction."; or
41 (2) a statement substantially similar to the statement set forth
42 in subdivision (1);
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
103
1 acknowledged by the initials or signature of the debtor, on or
2 before the date on which the short sale payment is accepted. As
3 used in this subsection, "short sale" means a transaction in which
4 the property that is the subject of a first lien mortgage transaction
5 is sold for an amount that is less than the amount of the debtor's
6 outstanding obligation under the first lien mortgage transaction.
7 A creditor or mortgage servicer that fails to respond to an offer
8 within the time prescribed by this subsection is liable in accordance
9 with 12 U.S.C. 2605(f) in any action brought under that section.
10 (d) This section is not intended to provide the owner of real
11 estate subject to the issuance of process under a judgment or
12 decree of foreclosure any protection or defense against a deficiency
13 judgment for purposes of the borrower protections from liability
14 that must be disclosed under 12 CFR 1026.38(p)(3) on the form
15 required by 12 CFR 1026.38 ("Closing Disclosures" form under
16 the Amendments to the 2013 Integrated Mortgage Disclosures Rule
17 Under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (Regulation X)
18 and the Truth In Lending Act (Regulation Z) and the 2013 Loan
19 Originator Rule Under the Truth in Lending Act (Regulation Z)).
20 Sec. 2. (a) Except as provided in subsection (b), the creditor
21 shall comply with disclosure requirements applicable to first lien
22 mortgage transactions in the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15
23 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
24 (b) Disclosures are not required if the transaction is exempt
25 from the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
26 Sec. 3. (a) A violation of a state or federal law, regulation, or
27 rule applicable to first lien mortgage transactions is a violation of
28 this article.
29 (b) The department may enforce penalty provisions set forth in
30 15 U.S.C. 1640 for violations of disclosure requirements applicable
31 to first lien mortgage transactions.
32 Sec. 4. (a) Unless a person subject to this article has first
33 obtained a mortgage license from the department and annually
34 maintains the license, the person shall not engage in Indiana as a
35 creditor in first lien mortgage transactions. A separate mortgage
36 license is required for each legal entity that engages in Indiana as
37 a creditor in first lien mortgage transactions. However, a separate
38 mortgage license is not required for each branch of a legal entity
39 licensed by the department.
40 (b) Each:
41 (1) creditor licensed by the department to engage in mortgage
42 transactions; and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
104
1 (2) person exempt from licensing that:
2 (A) employs a licensed mortgage loan originator; or
3 (B) sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as
4 permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
5 shall register with and maintain a valid unique identifier issued by
6 the NMLSR. Each licensed mortgage loan originator must be
7 employed by, or sponsored as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by
8 750 IAC 9, and associated with, a licensed creditor (or a person
9 exempt from licensing) that is registered with the NMLSR.
10 (c) An individual engaging solely in loan processor or
11 underwriter activities shall not represent to the public, through
12 advertising or other means of communicating or providing
13 information, including the use of business cards, stationery,
14 brochures, signs, rate lists, or other promotional items, that the
15 individual can or will perform any of the activities of a mortgage
16 loan originator. An individual who:
17 (1) is licensed as a mortgage loan originator under this article
18 and 750 IAC 9-3; and
19 (2) is sponsored by a person, as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8),
20 to engage solely as a third party loan processor or
21 underwriter;
22 is subject to the prohibition set forth in this subsection with respect
23 to the individual's engagement under the sponsorship.
24 (d) An applicant for a mortgage license must apply for the
25 license in the form prescribed by the director. Each form:
26 (1) must contain content as set forth by rule, instruction, or
27 procedure of the director; and
28 (2) may be changed or updated as necessary by the director to
29 carry out the purposes of this article.
30 (e) To fulfill the purposes of this article, the director may
31 establish relationships or contracts with the NMLSR or other
32 entities designated by the NMLSR to:
33 (1) collect and maintain records; and
34 (2) process transaction fees or other fees related to licensees
35 or other persons subject to this article.
36 (f) For the purpose of participating in the NMLSR, the director
37 or the department may:
38 (1) waive or modify, in whole or in part, by rule or order, any
39 of the requirements of this article; and
40 (2) establish new requirements as reasonably necessary to
41 participate in the NMLSR.
42 Sec. 5. (a) The department shall receive and act on all
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
105
1 applications for licenses to engage in first lien mortgage
2 transactions. Applications must be made as prescribed by the
3 director. If, at any time, the information or record contained in:
4 (1) an application filed under this section; or
5 (2) a renewal application filed under section 10 of this
6 chapter;
7 is or becomes inaccurate or incomplete in a material respect, the
8 applicant shall promptly file a correcting amendment with the
9 department.
10 (b) A license may not be issued unless the department finds that
11 the professional training and experience, financial responsibility,
12 character, and fitness of:
13 (1) the applicant and any significant affiliate of the applicant;
14 (2) each executive officer, director, or manager of the
15 applicant, or any other individual having a similar status or
16 performing a similar function for the applicant; and
17 (3) if known, each person directly or indirectly owning of
18 record or owning beneficially at least ten percent (10%) of the
19 outstanding shares of any class of equity security of the
20 applicant;
21 are such as to warrant belief that the business will be operated
22 honestly and fairly within the purposes of this article.
23 (c) The director is entitled to request evidence of compliance
24 with this section at:
25 (1) the time of application;
26 (2) the time of renewal of a license; or
27 (3) any other time considered necessary by the director.
28 (d) Evidence of compliance with this section must include:
29 (1) criminal background checks, as described in section 6 of
30 this chapter, including a national criminal history background
31 check (as defined in IC 10-13-3-12) by the Federal Bureau of
32 Investigation, for any individual described in subsection (b);
33 (2) credit histories as described in section 7 of this chapter;
34 (3) surety bond requirements as described in section 8 of this
35 chapter;
36 (4) a review of licensure actions in Indiana and in other states;
37 and
38 (5) other background checks considered necessary by the
39 director.
40 (e) For purposes of this section and in order to reduce the points
41 of contact that the director has to maintain for purposes of this
42 section, the director may use the NMLSR as a channeling agent for
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
106
1 requesting and distributing information to and from any source as
2 directed by the director.
3 (f) The department may deny an application under this section
4 if the director of the department determines that the application
5 was submitted for the benefit of, or on behalf of, a person who does
6 not qualify for a license.
7 (g) Upon written request, the applicant is entitled to a hearing
8 on the question of the qualifications of the applicant for a license
9 in the manner provided in IC 4-21.5.
10 (h) The applicant shall pay the following fees at the time
11 designated by the department:
12 (1) An initial license fee as established by the department
13 under IC 28-11-3-5.
14 (2) An annual renewal fee as established by the department
15 under IC 28-11-3-5.
16 (3) Examination fees as established by the department under
17 IC 28-11-3-5.
18 (i) A fee as established by the department under IC 28-11-3-5
19 may be charged for each day a fee under subsection (h)(2) or (h)(3)
20 is delinquent.
21 (j) Except in a transaction approved under section 20 of this
22 chapter, a license issued under this section is not assignable or
23 transferable.
24 (k) If the department of state revenue notifies the department
25 that a person is on the most recent tax warrant list, the department
26 shall not issue or renew the person's license until:
27 (1) the person provides to the department a statement from
28 the department of state revenue that the person's tax warrant
29 has been satisfied; or
30 (2) the department receives a notice from the commissioner of
31 the department of state revenue under IC 6-8.1-8-2(k).
32 Sec. 6. (a) When the director requests a national criminal
33 history background check under section 5(d)(1) of this chapter for
34 an individual described in section 5(b) of this chapter, the director
35 shall require the individual to submit fingerprints to the
36 department, state police department, or NMLSR, as directed, at
37 the time evidence of compliance is requested under section 5(c) of
38 this chapter. The individual to whom the request is made shall pay
39 any fees or costs associated with processing and evaluating the
40 fingerprints and the national criminal history background check.
41 The national criminal history background check may be used by
42 the director to determine the individual's compliance with this
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
107
1 section. The director or the department may not release the results
2 of the national criminal history background check to any private
3 entity.
4 (b) For purposes of this section and in order to reduce the points
5 of contact that the Federal Bureau of Investigation may have to
6 maintain for purposes of this section, the director may use the
7 NMLSR as a channeling agent for requesting information from
8 and distributing information to the United States Department of
9 Justice or any governmental agency.
10 Sec. 7. (a) If the director requests a credit report for an
11 individual described in section 5(b) of this chapter, the individual
12 to whom the request is made shall pay any fees or costs associated
13 with procuring the report.
14 (b) The individual must submit personal history and experience
15 information in a form prescribed by the NMLSR, including the
16 submission of authorization for the NMLSR or the director to
17 obtain an independent credit report obtained from a consumer
18 reporting agency described in Section 603(p) of the Fair Credit
19 Reporting Act (15 U.S.C. 1681a(p)).
20 (c) The director may consider one (1) or more of the following
21 when determining if an individual has demonstrated financial
22 responsibility:
23 (1) Bankruptcies filed within the last ten (10) years.
24 (2) Current outstanding judgments, except judgments solely
25 as a result of medical expenses.
26 (3) Current outstanding tax liens or other government liens or
27 filings.
28 (4) Foreclosures within the past three (3) years.
29 (5) A pattern of serious delinquent accounts within the past
30 three (3) years.
31 Sec. 8. (a) Each:
32 (1) creditor; and
33 (2) person that is exempt (either under this article or under
34 IC 37-2) from licensing and that:
35 (A) employs a licensed mortgage loan originator; or
36 (B) sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as
37 permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
38 must be covered by a surety bond in accordance with this section.
39 (b) A surety bond must:
40 (1) provide coverage for:
41 (A) a creditor; or
42 (B) a person that is exempt from licensing and that
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
108
1 employs a licensed mortgage loan originator, or that
2 sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as permitted
3 by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
4 in an amount as prescribed in subsection (d);
5 (2) be in a form prescribed by the director;
6 (3) be in effect:
7 (A) during the term of the creditor's license; or
8 (B) at any time during which the person exempt from
9 licensing employs a licensed mortgage loan originator or
10 sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as permitted
11 by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
12 as applicable;
13 (4) subject to subsection (c), remain in effect during the two
14 (2) years after:
15 (A) the license of the creditor is surrendered or
16 terminated; or
17 (B) the person exempt from licensing ceases to employ a
18 licensed mortgage loan originator, or ceases to sponsor a
19 licensed mortgage loan originator as permitted by
20 IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9, or to offer financial
21 services to individuals in Indiana, whichever is later;
22 as applicable;
23 (5) be payable to the department for the benefit of:
24 (A) the state; and
25 (B) individuals who reside in Indiana when they agree to
26 receive financial services from the creditor or the person
27 exempt from licensing, as applicable;
28 (6) be issued by a bonding, surety, or insurance company
29 authorized to do business in Indiana and rated at least "A-"
30 by at least one (1) nationally recognized investment rating
31 service; and
32 (7) have payment conditioned upon:
33 (A) the creditor's or any of the creditor's licensed
34 mortgage loan originators'; or
35 (B) the exempt person's or any of the exempt person's
36 licensed mortgage loan originators';
37 noncompliance with or violation of this chapter, 750 IAC 9, or
38 other federal or state laws or regulations applicable to
39 mortgage lending.
40 (c) The director may adopt rules or guidance documents with
41 respect to the requirements for a surety bond as necessary to
42 accomplish the purposes of this article. Upon written request from:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
109
1 (1) a creditor described in subsection (a)(1); or
2 (2) an exempt person described in subsection (a)(2);
3 the director may, at the discretion of the director, waive or shorten
4 the two (2) year period set forth in subsection (b)(4) during which
5 a surety bond required by this section must remain in effect after
6 the occurrence of an event described in subsection (b)(4)(A) or
7 (b)(4)(B), as applicable.
8 (d) The penal sum of the surety bond shall be maintained in an
9 amount that reflects the dollar amount of mortgage transactions
10 originated as determined by the director. If the principal amount
11 of a surety bond required under this section is reduced by payment
12 of a claim or judgment, the creditor or exempt person for whom
13 the bond is issued shall immediately notify the director of the
14 reduction and, not later than thirty (30) days after notice by the
15 director, file a new or an additional surety bond in an amount set
16 by the director. The amount of the new or additional bond set by
17 the director must be at least the amount of the bond before
18 payment of the claim or judgment.
19 (e) If for any reason a surety terminates a bond issued under
20 this section, the creditor or the exempt person shall immediately
21 notify the department and file a new surety bond in an amount
22 determined by the director.
23 (f) Cancellation of a surety bond issued under this section does
24 not affect any liability incurred or accrued during the period when
25 the surety bond was in effect.
26 (g) The director may obtain satisfaction from a surety bond
27 issued under this section if the director incurs expenses, issues a
28 final order, or recovers a final judgment under this chapter.
29 (h) Notices required under this section must be made in writing
30 and submitted through the NMLSR or any other electronic
31 registration system that may be approved by the director.
32 Sec. 9. (a) Subject to subsection (f), the director shall designate
33 the NMLSR to serve as the sole entity responsible for:
34 (1) processing applications and renewals for mortgage
35 licenses;
36 (2) issuing unique identifiers for licensees and persons exempt
37 from licensing that employ or sponsor a licensed mortgage
38 loan originator under this article; and
39 (3) performing other services that the director determines are
40 necessary for the orderly administration of the department's
41 mortgage licensing system.
42 (b) Subject to the confidentiality provisions contained in
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
110
1 IC 5-14-3, this section, and IC 28-1-2-30, the director may
2 regularly report significant or recurring violations of this article
3 to the NMLSR.
4 (c) Subject to the confidentiality provisions contained in
5 IC 5-14-3, this section, and IC 28-1-2-30, the director may report
6 complaints received regarding mortgage licensees to the NMLSR.
7 (d) The director may report publicly adjudicated licensure
8 actions against a licensee to the NMLSR.
9 (e) The director shall establish a process in which licensees may
10 challenge information reported to the NMLSR by the department.
11 (f) The director's authority to designate the NMLSR under
12 subsection (a) is subject to the following:
13 (1) Information stored in the NMLSR is subject to the
14 confidentiality provisions of IC 5-14-3 and IC 28-1-2-30. A
15 person may not:
16 (A) obtain information from the NMLSR, unless the
17 person is authorized to do so by statute;
18 (B) initiate any civil action based on information obtained
19 from the NMLSR if the information is not otherwise
20 available to the person under any other state law; or
21 (C) initiate any civil action based on information obtained
22 from the NMLSR if the person could not have initiated the
23 action based on information otherwise available to the
24 person under any other state law.
25 (2) Documents, materials, and other forms of information in
26 the control or possession of the NMLSR that are confidential
27 under IC 28-1-2-30 and that are:
28 (A) furnished by the director, the director's designee, or a
29 licensee; or
30 (B) otherwise obtained by the NMLSR;
31 are confidential and privileged by law and are not subject to
32 inspection under IC 5-14-3, subject to subpoena, subject to
33 discovery, or admissible in evidence in any civil action.
34 However, the director may use the documents, materials, or
35 other information available to the director in furtherance of
36 any action brought in connection with the director's duties
37 under this article.
38 (3) Disclosure of documents, materials, and information:
39 (A) to the director; or
40 (B) by the director;
41 under this subsection does not result in a waiver of any
42 applicable privilege or claim of confidentiality with respect to

1 the documents, materials, or information.
