32 Sec. 10. (a) A mortgage license issued by the department must
33 be renewed through the NMLSR not later than December 31 of
34 each calendar year. The minimum standards for license renewal
35 for a creditor include the following:
36 (1) The creditor has continued to meet the surety bond
37 requirement under section 8 of this chapter.
38 (2) The creditor has filed the creditor's call report in a
39 manner that satisfies section 19(d) of this chapter.
40 (3) The creditor has paid all required fees for renewal of the
41 license.
42 (4) The creditor and individuals described in section 5(b) of
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
113
1 this chapter have certified to the department that they
2 continue to meet all the standards for licensing contained in
3 section 5 of this chapter.
4 (5) The creditor has provided in the creditor's renewal
5 application:
6 (A) any information describing material changes in the
7 information contained in the creditor's original application
8 for licensure, or in any previous application, including any
9 previous renewal application; and
10 (B) any other information the director requires in order to
11 evaluate the renewal of the license.
12 (b) A license issued by the department authorizing a person to
13 engage in first lien mortgage transactions as a creditor under this
14 article may be revoked or suspended by the department if the
15 person fails to:
16 (1) file any renewal form required by the department; or
17 (2) pay any license renewal fee described under section 5 of
18 this chapter;
19 not later than sixty (60) days after the due date.
20 (c) A person whose license is revoked or suspended under this
21 section may do either of the following:
22 (1) Pay all delinquent fees and apply for reinstatement of the
23 license.
24 (2) Appeal the revocation or suspension to the department for
25 an administrative review under IC 4-21.5-3. Pending the
26 decision resulting from the hearing under IC 4-21.5-3
27 concerning the license revocation or suspension, the license
28 remains in force.
29 (d) If, at any time, the information or record contained in:
30 (1) an original application for licensure filed under section 5
31 of this chapter; or
32 (2) a renewal application filed under this section;
33 is or becomes inaccurate or incomplete in a material respect, the
34 applicant shall promptly file a correcting amendment with the
35 department.
36 Sec. 11. (a) The department may issue to a person licensed as a
37 creditor to engage in first lien mortgage transactions an order to
38 show cause why the person's license should not be revoked or
39 suspended for a period determined by the department.
40 (b) An order issued under subsection (a) must:
41 (1) include:
42 (A) a statement of the place, date, and time for a meeting
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
114
1 with the department, which date may not be less than ten
2 (10) days from the date of the order;
3 (B) a description of the action contemplated by the
4 department; and
5 (C) a statement of the facts or conduct supporting the
6 issuance of the order; and
7 (2) be accompanied by a notice stating that the licensee is
8 entitled to:
9 (A) a reasonable opportunity to be heard; and
10 (B) show the licensee's compliance with all lawful
11 requirements for retention of the license;
12 at the meeting described in subdivision (1)(A).
13 (c) After the meeting described in subsection (b)(1)(A), the
14 department may revoke or suspend the license if the department
15 finds that:
16 (1) the licensee has repeatedly and willfully violated:
17 (A) this article or any applicable rule, order, or guidance
18 document adopted or issued by the department; or
19 (B) any other state or federal law, regulation, or rule
20 applicable to first lien mortgage transactions;
21 (2) the licensee does not meet the licensing qualifications
22 contained in section 5 of this chapter;
23 (3) the licensee obtained the license for the benefit of, or on
24 behalf of, another person;
25 (4) the licensee knowingly or intentionally made material
26 misrepresentations to, or concealed material information
27 from, the department; or
28 (5) facts or conditions exist that, had they existed at the time
29 the licensee applied for the license, would have been grounds
30 for the department to deny the issuance of the license.
31 (d) Whenever the department revokes or suspends a license, the
32 department shall enter an order to that effect and notify the
33 licensee of:
34 (1) the revocation or suspension;
35 (2) if a suspension has been ordered, the duration of the
36 suspension;
37 (3) the procedure for appealing the revocation or suspension
38 under IC 4-21.5-3-6; and
39 (4) any other terms and conditions that apply to the
40 revocation or suspension.
41 Not later than five (5) days after the entry of the order, the
42 department shall deliver to the licensee a copy of the order and the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
115
1 findings supporting the order.
2 (e) Any person holding a license as a creditor to engage in first
3 lien mortgage transactions may relinquish the license by notifying
4 the department in writing of the relinquishment. However, a
5 relinquishment under this subsection does not affect the person's
6 liability for acts previously committed and coming within the scope
7 of this article.
8 (f) If the director determines it to be in the public interest, the
9 director may pursue revocation of a license of a licensee that has
10 relinquished the license under subsection (e).
11 (g) If a person's license is revoked, suspended, or relinquished,
12 the revocation, suspension, or relinquishment does not impair or
13 affect any obligation owed by any person under any preexisting
14 lawful contract.
15 (h) If the director has just cause to believe an emergency exists
16 from which it is necessary to protect the interests of the public, the
17 director may proceed with the revocation of a license through an
18 emergency or another temporary order under IC 4-21.5-4.
19 Sec. 12. (a) If the director determines that a current or former
20 director, an officer, or a manager of a creditor:
21 (1) has committed a violation of a statute, a rule, a final cease
22 and desist order, any condition imposed in writing by the
23 director in connection with the granting of any application or
24 other request by the creditor, or any written agreement
25 between the creditor and the director or the department;
26 (2) has committed fraudulent or unconscionable conduct; or
27 (3) has been convicted of a felony under the laws of Indiana or
28 any other jurisdiction;
29 the director, subject to subsection (b), may issue and serve upon
30 the officer, director, or manager a notice of the director's intent to
31 issue an order removing the person from the person's office or
32 employment, an order prohibiting any participation by the person
33 in the conduct of the affairs of any creditor, or an order both
34 removing the person and prohibiting the person's participation.
35 (b) A violation, practice, or breach specified in subsection (a) is
36 subject to the authority of the director under subsections (a) and
37 (c) if the director finds any of the following:
38 (1) The interests of the creditor's customers could be seriously
39 prejudiced by reason of the violation or practice.
40 (2) The violation, practice, or breach involves an act of fraud,
41 dishonesty, theft, breach of trust, money laundering, or the
42 wrongful taking of property on the part of the officer,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
116
1 director, or manager involved.
2 (3) The violation, practice, or breach demonstrates a willful
3 or continuing disregard by the officer, director, or manager
4 for state and federal laws and regulations, and for the
5 consumer protections contained in this article.
6 (c) A person who has been convicted of a felony under the laws
7 of Indiana or any other jurisdiction may not serve as an officer, a
8 director, or a manager of a creditor, or serve in any similar
9 capacity, unless the person obtains the written consent of the
10 director.
11 (d) A creditor that willfully permits a person to serve the
12 creditor in violation of subsection (c) is subject to a civil penalty of
13 five hundred dollars ($500) for each day the violation continues.
14 (e) A creditor shall give the department written notice of the
15 resignation, discharge, or termination of an employee, independent
16 contractor, or agent against whom allegations were made that
17 accused the employee, independent contractor, or agent of:
18 (1) violating this article or other laws, regulations, rules, or
19 industry standards of conduct applicable to first lien
20 mortgage transactions; or
21 (2) fraud, dishonesty, theft, breach of trust, money
22 laundering, or the wrongful taking of property.
23 The creditor shall provide the department the notice required
24 under this subsection not later than thirty (30) days after the
25 effective date of the resignation, discharge, or termination.
26 Sec. 13. (a) A notice issued under this chapter must:
27 (1) be in writing;
28 (2) contain a statement of the facts constituting the alleged
29 practice, violation, or breach;
30 (3) state the facts alleged in support of the violation, practice,
31 or breach;
32 (4) state the director's intention to enter an order under
33 section 15(a) of this chapter;
34 (5) be delivered to the board of directors of the creditor;
35 (6) be delivered to the officer, director, or manager
36 concerned;
37 (7) specify the procedures that must be followed to initiate a
38 hearing to contest the facts alleged; and
39 (8) if the director suspends or prohibits an officer, a director,
40 or a manager of the creditor from participating in the affairs
41 of the creditor, as described in subsection (e), include a
42 statement of the suspension or prohibition.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
117
1 (b) If a hearing is requested not later than ten (10) days after
2 service of the written notice, the department shall hold a hearing
3 concerning the alleged practice, violation, or breach. The hearing
4 shall be held not later than forty-five (45) days after receipt of the
5 request. The department, based on the evidence presented at the
6 hearing, shall enter a final order under section 15 of this chapter.
7 (c) If no hearing is requested within the time specified in
8 subsection (b), the director may proceed to issue a final order
9 under section 15 of this chapter on the basis of the facts set forth in
10 the written notice.
11 (d) An officer, a director, or a manager who is removed from a
12 position under a removal order that has become final may not
13 participate in the conduct of the affairs of any mortgage licensee
14 without the approval of the director.
15 (e) The director may, for the protection of the creditor or the
16 interests of its customers, suspend from office or prohibit from
17 participation in the affairs of the creditor an officer, a director, or
18 a manager of a creditor who is the subject of a written notice
19 served by the director under section 12(a) of this chapter. A
20 suspension or prohibition under this subsection becomes effective
21 upon service of the notice under section 12(a) of this chapter.
22 Unless stayed by a court in a proceeding authorized by subsection
23 (f), the suspension or prohibition remains in effect pending
24 completion of the proceedings related to the notice served under
25 section 12(a) of this chapter and until the effective date of an order
26 entered by the department under subsection (b) or the director
27 under subsection (c). Copies of the notice shall also be served upon
28 the creditor or affiliate of which the person is an officer, a director,
29 or a manager.
30 (f) Not more than fifteen (15) days after an officer, a director, or
31 a manager has been suspended from office or prohibited from
32 participation in the conduct of the affairs of the creditor or affiliate
33 under subsection (e), the officer, director, or manager may apply
34 to a court having jurisdiction for a stay of the suspension or
35 prohibition pending completion of the proceedings related to the
36 written notice served under section 12(a) of this chapter, and the
37 court may stay the suspension or prohibition.
38 (g) The department shall maintain an official record of a
39 proceeding under this chapter.
40 Sec. 14. If the director enters into a consent to a final order
41 under section 15 of this chapter with a creditor, a director, an
42 officer, or a manager, the director is not required to issue and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
118
1 serve a notice of charges upon the creditor, director, officer, or
2 manager under section 12 of this chapter. A consent agreement
3 may be negotiated and entered into before or after the issuance of
4 a notice of charges. The director shall provide a copy of the consent
5 order to the board of directors of the creditor.
6 Sec. 15. (a) If, after a hearing described in section 13(b) of this
7 chapter, the department finds that the conditions specified in
8 section 12 of this chapter have been established, the department
9 may issue a final order. If a hearing is not requested within the
10 time specified in section 13(b) of this chapter, the director may
11 issue a final order on the basis of the facts set forth in the written
12 notice served under section 12(a) of this chapter.
13 (b) Unless the director has entered into a consent agreement
14 described in section 14 of this chapter, a final order must include
15 separately stated findings of fact and conclusions of law for all
16 aspects of the order.
17 (c) In a final order under this section, the department or the
18 director, as appropriate, may order one (1) or more of the
19 following with respect to an officer, a director, or a manager of a
20 creditor:
21 (1) The removal of the officer, director, or manager from the
22 person's office, position, or employment.
23 (2) A prohibition against any participation by the officer,
24 director, or manager in the conduct of the affairs of any
25 creditor.
26 (3) If the subject of the order is an officer or a director of a
27 creditor, and subject to section 17 of this chapter, the
28 imposition of a civil penalty not to exceed fifteen thousand
29 dollars ($15,000) for each practice, violation, or act that:
30 (A) is described in section 12 of this chapter; and
31 (B) is found to exist by the department or the director.
32 (d) A final order shall be issued in writing not later than ninety
33 (90) days after conclusion of a hearing held under section 13(b) of
34 this chapter, unless this period is waived or extended with the
35 written consent of all parties or for good cause shown.
36 (e) If the officer, director, or manager does not appear
37 individually or by an authorized representative at a hearing held
38 under section 13(b) of this chapter, the officer, director, or
39 manager is considered to have consented to the issuance of a final
40 order.
41 (f) The remedies provided in this chapter are in addition to
42 other remedies contained in this article.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
119
1 Sec. 16. (a) A final order issued under this chapter is effective on
2 the eleventh day after service of the order. However, a final order
3 issued upon consent under section 14 of this chapter is effective at
4 the time specified in the order.
5 (b) A final order remains effective and enforceable as provided
6 in the order.
7 (c) The department or a reviewing court may stay, modify, or
8 vacate a final order.
9 Sec. 17. (a) The director or the department, as appropriate, shall
10 consider the following factors in determining the amount of a civil
11 penalty that should be assessed against a director or an officer in
12 a final order issued under section 15(c)(3) of this chapter:
13 (1) The appropriateness of the civil penalty with respect to the
14 financial resources and good faith of the individual charged.
15 (2) The gravity of the practice, violation, or act.
16 (3) The history of previous practices, violations, or acts.
17 (4) The economic benefit derived by the individual from the
18 practice, violation, or act.
19 (5) Other factors that justice requires.
20 (b) A creditor may not indemnify a director or an officer for a
21 civil penalty imposed in a final order under section 15(c)(3) of this
22 chapter.
23 (c) Civil penalties shall be deposited in the financial institutions
24 fund established by IC 28-11-2-9.
25 Sec. 18. The director may enforce any of the following by
26 applying for appropriate relief to a court having jurisdiction:
27 (1) An order issued under this chapter.
28 (2) A written agreement entered into by the department or the
29 director and any director, officer, or employee of a creditor.
30 (3) Any condition imposed in writing by the department or the
31 director on any director, officer, or employee of a creditor.
32 Sec. 19. (a) Every licensee shall maintain records in a manner
33 that will enable the department to determine whether the licensee
34 is complying with this article. The record keeping system of a
35 licensee is sufficient if the licensee makes the required information
36 reasonably available. The department shall determine the
37 sufficiency of the records and whether the licensee has made the
38 required information reasonably available. The department shall
39 be given free access to the records wherever the records are
40 located. Records concerning any first lien mortgage transaction
41 shall be retained for two (2) years after the making of the final
42 entry relating to the transaction, but in the case of a revolving first
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
120
1 lien mortgage transaction, the two (2) years required under this
2 subsection is measured from the date of each entry relating to the
3 transaction. A person that voluntarily registers with the
4 department under IC 37-1-1-6(8) for the purpose of sponsoring
5 licensed mortgage loan originators shall:
6 (1) cooperate with the department; and
7 (2) provide access to records and documents;
8 as required by the department in carrying out examinations of the
9 activities of the licensed mortgage loan originators sponsored by
10 the person.
11 (b) The unique identifier of any person originating a mortgage
12 transaction must be clearly shown on all mortgage transaction
13 application forms and any other documents as required by the
14 director.
15 (c) Every licensee shall use automated examination and
16 regulatory software designated by the director, including third
17 party software. Use of the software consistent with guidance and
18 policies issued by the director is not a violation of IC 28-1-2-30.
19 (d) Each:
20 (1) creditor licensed to engage in mortgage transactions by the

38 IAC 9;

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
121
1 prescribed by the department.
2 (f) A licensed creditor shall file notification with the department
3 if the licensee:
4 (1) has a change in name, address, or any of its principals;
5 (2) opens a new branch, closes an existing branch, or relocates
6 an existing branch;

10 creditor's activities;
11 not later than thirty (30) days after the date of the event described

18 date of the event described in this subsection.

21 licensee has had the person's authority to do business in the
22 securities, commodities, banking, financial services, insurance, real
23 estate, or real estate appraisal industry revoked or suspended by
24 Indiana or by any other state, federal, or foreign governmental
25 agency or self-regulatory organization. The licensee shall file the
26 notification required by this subsection not later than thirty (30)
27 days after the date of the event described in this subsection.
28 Sec. 20. (a) As used in this section, "control" means possession
29 of the power directly or indirectly to:
30 (1) direct or cause the direction of the management or policies
31 of a creditor, whether through the beneficial ownership of
32 voting securities, by contract, or otherwise; or
33 (2) vote at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the voting
34 securities of a creditor, whether the voting rights are derived
35 through the beneficial ownership of voting securities, by
36 contract, or otherwise.
37 (b) An organization or an individual acting directly, indirectly,
38 or through or in concert with one (1) or more other organizations
39 or individuals may not acquire control of any creditor unless the
40 department has received and approved an application for change
41 in control. The department has not more than one hundred twenty
42 (120) days after receipt of an application to issue a notice
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
122
1 approving the proposed change in control. The application must
2 contain the name and address of the organization, individual, or
3 individuals who propose to acquire control and any other
4 information required by the director.
5 (c) The period for approval under subsection (b) may be
6 extended:
7 (1) in the discretion of the director for an additional thirty
8 (30) days; and
9 (2) not more than two (2) additional times for not more than
10 forty-five (45) days each time if:
11 (A) the director determines that the organization,
12 individual, or individuals who propose to acquire control
13 have not submitted substantial evidence of the
14 qualifications described in subsection (d);
15 (B) the director determines that any material information
16 submitted is substantially inaccurate; or
17 (C) the director has been unable to complete the
18 investigation of the organization, individual, or individuals
19 who propose to acquire control because of any delay
20 caused by or the inadequate cooperation of the
21 organization, individual, or individuals.
22 (d) The department shall issue a notice approving the
23 application only after it is satisfied that both of the following apply:
24 (1) The organization, individual, or individuals who propose
25 to acquire control are qualified by competence, experience,
26 character, and financial responsibility to control and operate
27 the creditor in a legal and proper manner.
28 (2) The interests of the owners and creditors of the creditor
29 and the interests of the public generally will not be
30 jeopardized by the proposed change in control.
31 (e) The director may determine, in the director's discretion, that
32 subsection (b) does not apply to a transaction if the director
33 determines that the direct or beneficial ownership of the creditor
34 will not change as a result of the transaction.
35 (f) The president or other chief executive officer of a creditor
36 shall report to the director any transfer or sale of securities of the
37 creditor that results in direct or indirect ownership by a holder or
38 an affiliated group of holders of at least ten percent (10%) of the
39 outstanding securities of the creditor. The report required by this
40 subsection must be made not later than ten (10) days after the
41 transfer of the securities on the books of the creditor.
42 (g) Depending on the circumstances of the transaction, the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
123
1 director may reserve the right to require the organization,
2 individual, or individuals who propose to acquire control of a
3 creditor licensed by the department to engage in mortgage
4 transactions, to apply for a new license under section 4 of this
5 chapter, instead of acquiring control of the licensee under this
6 section.
7 Sec. 21. A creditor in a first lien mortgage transaction shall
8 comply with IC 6-1.1-12-43, to the extent applicable.
9 Sec. 22. (a) A violation by a creditor in a first lien mortgage
10 transaction of Section 125 of the Consumer Credit Protection Act
11 (15 U.S.C. 1635) (concerning a debtor's right to rescind a
12 transaction) constitutes a violation of this article. A creditor may
13 not accrue interest during the period when a first lien mortgage
14 transaction may be rescinded under Section 125 of the Consumer
15 Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1635).
16 (b) A creditor must make available for disbursement the
17 proceeds of a transaction subject to subsection (a) on the later of:
18 (1) the date the creditor is reasonably satisfied that the debtor
19 has not rescinded the transaction; or
20 (2) the first business day after the expiration of the rescission
21 period under subsection (a).
22 Sec. 23. A creditor in a first lien mortgage transaction that:
23 (1) qualifies as a home equity conversion mortgage under the
24 Federal Housing Administration's program; or
25 (2) otherwise constitutes a reverse mortgage;
26 shall provide the debtor with a pamphlet that is approved by the
27 department and that describes the availability of reverse mortgage
28 counseling services provided by housing counselors approved by
29 the Secretary of the United States Department of Housing and
30 Urban Development, as provided in 24 CFR 206.41(a). The debtor
31 must receive the counseling described in this section and present
32 the creditor with the certificate described in 24 CFR 206.41(c)
33 before the creditor may make a first lien mortgage transaction
34 described in this section to the debtor.
35 Chapter 4. Administration
36 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to a person that engages as a
37 creditor in first lien mortgage transactions in Indiana. The
38 authority of this chapter remains in effect, whether a licensee,
39 individual, or person subject to this article acts or claims to act
40 under any licensing or registration law of Indiana or claims to act
41 without such authority.
42 Sec. 2. (a) In addition to other powers granted by this article,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
124
1 the department within the limitations provided by law may:
2 (1) receive and act on complaints, take action designed to
3 obtain voluntary compliance with this article, or commence
4 proceedings on the department's own initiative;
5 (2) counsel persons and groups on their rights and duties
6 under this article;
7 (3) establish programs for the education of consumers with
8 respect to credit practices and problems;
9 (4) make studies appropriate to effectuate the purposes and
10 policies of this article and make the results available to the
11 public;
12 (5) adopt, amend, and repeal rules, orders, policies, and forms
13 to carry out the provisions of this article;
14 (6) maintain more than one (1) office within Indiana; and
15 (7) appoint any necessary attorneys, hearing examiners,
16 clerks, and other employees and agents and fix their
17 compensation, and authorize attorneys appointed under this
18 section to appear for and represent the department in court.
19 (b) Liability may not be imposed under this article for an act
20 done or omitted in conformity with a rule, written notice, written
21 opinion, written interpretation, or written directive of the
22 department notwithstanding the fact that after the act is done or
23 omitted the rule, written notice, written opinion, written
24 interpretation, or written directive may be:
25 (1) amended or repealed; or
26 (2) determined by judicial or other authority to be invalid;
27 for any reason.
28 Sec. 3. (a) In administering this article and in order to determine
29 whether the provisions of this article are being complied with by
30 persons engaging in acts subject to this article, the department may
31 examine the records of persons and may make investigations of
32 persons as may be necessary to determine compliance. Records
33 subject to examination under this section include the following:
34 (1) Training, operating, and policy manuals.
35 (2) Minutes of:
36 (A) management meetings; and
37 (B) other meetings.
38 (3) Financial records, credit files, and data bases.
39 (4) Other records that the department determines are
40 necessary to perform its investigation or examination.
41 The department may also administer oaths or affirmations,
42 subpoena witnesses, and compel the attendance of witnesses,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
125
1 including officers, principals, mortgage loan originators,
2 employees, independent contractors, agents, and customers of
3 licensees, and other individuals or persons subject to this article.
4 The department may also adduce evidence and require the
5 production of any matter that is relevant to an investigation. The
6 department shall determine the sufficiency of the records
7 maintained and whether the person has made the required
8 information reasonably available. The records concerning any
9 transaction subject to this article shall be retained for two (2) years
10 after the making of the final entry relating to the first lien
11 mortgage transaction, but in the case of a revolving first lien
12 mortgage transaction the two (2) year period is measured from the
13 date of each entry.
14 (b) The department's examination and investigatory authority
15 under this article includes the following:
16 (1) The authority to require a creditor to refund overcharges
17 resulting from the creditor's noncompliance with the terms of
18 a first lien mortgage transaction.
19 (2) The authority to require a creditor to comply with the
20 penalty provisions set forth in IC 37-1-3-1.
21 (3) The authority to investigate complaints filed with the
22 department by debtors.
23 (c) The department shall be given free access to the records
24 wherever the records are located. In making any examination or
25 investigation authorized by this article, the director may control
26 access to any documents and records of the licensee or person
27 under examination or investigation. The director may take
28 possession of the documents and records or place a person in
29 exclusive charge of the documents and records in the place where
30 the documents are usually kept. During the period of control, a
31 licensee or person may not remove or attempt to remove any of the
32 documents and records except under a court order or with the
33 consent of the director. Unless the director has reasonable grounds
34 to believe the documents or records of the licensee or person have
35 been, or are, at risk of being altered or destroyed for purposes of
36 concealing a violation of this article, the licensee or person shall
37 have access to the documents or records as necessary to conduct
38 the licensee's or person's ordinary business affairs. If the person's
39 records are located outside Indiana, the records shall be made
40 available to the department at a convenient location within
41 Indiana, or the person shall pay the reasonable and necessary
42 expenses for the department or the department's representative to
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
126
1 examine the records where they are maintained. The department
2 may designate comparable officials of the state in which the
3 records are located to inspect the records on behalf of the
4 department.
5 (d) Upon a person's failure without lawful excuse to obey a
6 subpoena or to give testimony and upon reasonable notice by the
7 department to all affected persons, the department may apply to
8 any civil court with jurisdiction for an order compelling
9 compliance.
10 (e) The department shall not make public:
11 (1) the name or identity of a person whose acts or conduct the
12 department investigates under this section; or
13 (2) the facts discovered in the investigation.
14 However, this subsection does not apply to civil actions or
15 enforcement proceedings under this article.
16 (f) To discover violations of this article or to secure information
17 necessary for the enforcement of this article, the department may
18 investigate any:
19 (1) licensee; or
20 (2) person that the department suspects to be operating:
21 (A) without a license, when a license is required under this
22 article; or
23 (B) otherwise in violation of this article.
24 The department has all investigatory and enforcement authority
25 under this article that the department has under IC 28-11 with
26 respect to financial institutions. If the department conducts an
27 investigation under this section, the licensee or other person
28 investigated shall pay all reasonably incurred costs of the
29 investigation in accordance with the fee schedule adopted under
30 IC 28-11-3-5. Any costs required to be paid under this section shall
31 be paid not later than sixty (60) days after the person being
32 assessed the costs receives a notice from the department of the
33 costs assessed. The department may impose a fee, in an amount
34 fixed by the department under IC 28-11-3-5, for each day the
35 assessed costs are not paid, beginning on the first day after the
36 sixty (60) day period described in this subsection.
37 (g) If a creditor contracts with an outside vendor to provide a
38 service that would otherwise be undertaken internally by the
39 creditor and be subject to the department's routine examination
40 procedures, the person that provides the service to the creditor
41 shall, at the request of the director, submit to an examination by
42 the department. If the director determines that an examination
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
127
1 under this subsection is necessary or desirable, the examination
2 may be made at the expense of the person to be examined. If the
3 person to be examined under this subsection refuses to permit the
4 examination to be made, the director may order any creditor that
5 is licensed by the department to engage in mortgage transactions
6 and that receives services from the person refusing the
7 examination to:
8 (1) discontinue receiving one (1) or more services from the
9 person; or
10 (2) otherwise cease conducting business with the person.
11 Sec. 4. To carry out the purposes of this article, the director
12 may:
13 (1) retain attorneys, accountants, or other professionals and
14 specialists as examiners, auditors, or investigators to conduct
15 or assist in the conduct of examinations or investigations;
16 (2) enter into agreements or relationships with other
17 government officials or regulatory associations in order to
18 improve efficiencies and reduce regulatory burden by
19 sharing:
20 (A) resources;
21 (B) standardized or uniform methods or procedures; and
22 (C) documents, records, information, or evidence obtained
23 under this section;
24 (3) use, hire, contract, or employ public or privately available
25 analytical systems, methods, or software to examine or
26 investigate a licensee, an individual, or a person subject to this
27 article;
28 (4) accept and rely on examination or investigation reports
29 made by other government officials within or outside Indiana;
30 and
31 (5) accept audit reports made by an independent certified
32 public accountant for the licensee, individual, or person
33 subject to this article in the course of that part of the
34 examination covering the same general subject matter as the

36 the examination, report of investigation, or other writing of
37 the director.
38 Sec. 5. It is a violation of this article for a person or individual
39 subject to this article to:
40 (1) directly or indirectly employ any scheme, device, or
41 artifice to defraud or mislead borrowers or lenders or to
42 defraud any person;
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
128
1 (2) engage in any unfair or deceptive practice toward any
2 person;
3 (3) obtain property by fraud or misrepresentation;
4 (4) solicit or enter into a contract with a borrower that
5 provides in substance that the person or individual subject to
6 this article may earn a fee or commission through "best
7 efforts" to obtain a loan even though no loan is actually
8 obtained for the borrower;
9 (5) solicit, advertise, or enter into a contract for specific
10 interest rates, points, or other financing terms unless the
11 terms are actually available at the time of soliciting,
12 advertising, or contracting;
13 (6) conduct any business covered by this article without
14 holding a valid license as required under this article, or assist
15 or aid and abet any person in the conduct of business under
16 this article without a valid license as required under this
17 article;
18 (7) fail to make disclosures as required by this article or
19 regulation adopted under this article and any other applicable
20 state or federal law regulation;
21 (8) fail to comply with this article or rules adopted under this
22 article, or fail to comply with any other state or federal law,
23 rule, or regulation, applicable to any business authorized or
24 conducted under this article;
25 (9) make, in any manner, any false or deceptive statement or
26 representation, with regard to the rates, points, or other
27 financing terms or conditions for a mortgage transaction, or
28 engage in bait and switch advertising;
29 (10) negligently make any false statement or knowingly and
30 willfully make any omission of material fact in connection
31 with any information or reports filed with a governmental
32 agency or the NMLSR or in connection with any investigation
33 conducted by the director or another governmental agency;
34 (11) make any payment, threat, or promise, directly or
35 indirectly, to any person for the purposes of influencing the
36 independent judgment of the person in connection with a
37 mortgage transaction, or make any payment, threat, or
38 promise, directly or indirectly, to any appraiser of a property,
39 for the purposes of influencing the independent judgment of
40 the appraiser with respect to the value of the property;
41 (12) collect, charge, attempt to collect or charge, or use or
42 propose any agreement purporting to collect or charge any
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
129
1 fee prohibited by this article;
2 (13) cause or require a borrower to obtain property insurance
3 coverage in an amount that exceeds the replacement cost of
4 the improvements as established by the property insurer;
5 (14) fail to account truthfully for money belonging to a party
6 to a mortgage transaction; or
7 (15) knowingly withhold, abstract, remove, mutilate, destroy,
8 or secrete any books, records, computer records, or other
9 information subject to examination under this article.
10 Sec. 6. Except as otherwise provided, IC 4-21.5-3 governs any
11 action taken by the department under this chapter or IC 37-1-3-4
12 through IC 37-1-3-19. IC 4-22-2 applies to the adoption of rules by
13 the department under this article. All proceedings for
14 administrative review under IC 4-21.5-3 or judicial review under
15 IC 4-21.5-5 shall be held in Marion County.
16 Sec. 7. (a) After notice and hearing, the department may order
17 a creditor or a person acting on behalf of the creditor to cease and
18 desist from engaging in violations of this article. In any civil court
19 with jurisdiction:
20 (1) a respondent aggrieved by an order of the department
21 may obtain judicial review of the order; and
22 (2) the department may obtain an order of the court for the
23 enforcement of the department's order.
24 A proceeding for review or enforcement under this subsection shall
25 be initiated by the filing of a petition in the court. Copies of the
26 petition shall be served upon all parties of record.
27 (b) Not later than thirty (30) days after service of a petition for
28 review upon the department under subsection (a), or within such
29 further time as the court may allow, the department shall transmit
30 to the court the original or a certified copy of the entire record
31 upon which the order that is the subject of the review is based,
32 including any transcript of testimony, which need not be printed.
33 By stipulation of all parties to the review proceeding, the record
34 may be shortened. After conducting a hearing on the matter, the
35 court may:
36 (1) reverse or modify the order if the findings of fact of the
37 department are clearly erroneous in view of the reliable,
38 probative, and substantial evidence in the whole record;
39 (2) grant any temporary relief or restraining order the court
40 considers just; and
41 (3) enter an order:
42 (A) enforcing;
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
130
1 (B) modifying;
2 (C) enforcing as modified; or
3 (D) setting aside;
4 in whole or in part, the order of the department; or
5 (4) enter an order remanding the case to the department for
6 further proceedings.
7 (c) An objection not urged at the hearing shall not be considered
8 by the court unless the failure to urge the objection is excused for
9 good cause shown. A party may move the court to remand the case
10 to the department in the interest of justice for the purpose of:
11 (1) adducing additional specified and material evidence; and
12 (2) seeking a finding upon such evidence;
13 upon good cause shown for the failure to previously adduce this
14 evidence before the department.
15 (d) The jurisdiction of the court is exclusive and the court's final
16 judgment or decree is subject to review on appeal in the same
17 manner and form and with the same effect as in appeals from a
18 final judgment or decree. The department's copy of the testimony
19 shall be available at reasonable times to all parties for examination
20 without cost.
21 (e) A proceeding for review under this section must be initiated
22 not later than thirty (30) days after a copy of the order of the
23 department is received. If a proceeding is not initiated within the
24 time set forth in this subsection, the department may obtain a
25 decree of a civil court with jurisdiction for enforcement of the
26 department's order upon a showing that:
27 (1) the order was issued in compliance with this section;
28 (2) a proceeding for review was not initiated within the thirty
29 (30) day period prescribed by this subsection; and
30 (3) the respondent is subject to the jurisdiction of the court.
31 (f) With respect to unconscionable agreements or fraudulent or
32 unconscionable conduct by a respondent, the department may not
33 issue an order under this section but may bring a civil action for an
34 injunction under section 12 of this chapter.
35 Sec. 8. If it is claimed that a person has engaged in conduct
36 subject to an order by:
37 (1) the department under section 7(a) of this chapter; or
38 (2) a court under sections 9 through 11 of this chapter;
39 the department may accept an assurance in writing that the person
40 will not engage in the conduct in the future. If a person giving an
41 assurance of discontinuance fails to comply with the terms of the
42 assurance, the assurance is evidence that before the assurance was
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
131
1 issued the person engaged in the conduct described in the
2 assurance.
3 Sec. 9. The department may bring a civil action to restrain a
4 person from violating this article or other state or federal law, rule,
5 or regulation and for other appropriate relief.
6 Sec. 10. (a) As used in this section, "deceptive act" means an act
7 or a practice in which a person knowingly or intentionally:
8 (1) makes a material misrepresentation concerning; or
9 (2) conceals material information regarding the terms or
10 conditions of;
11 a first lien mortgage transaction.
12 (b) For purposes of this section, "knowingly" means having
13 actual knowledge at the time of the transaction.
14 (c) The department may bring a civil action to enjoin a
15 deceptive act performed in connection with a first lien mortgage
16 transaction.
17 Sec. 11. With respect to an action brought under:
18 (1) section 9 of this chapter to enjoin violations of this article;
19 or
20 (2) section 10 of this chapter to enjoin deceptive acts;
21 the department may apply to the court for appropriate temporary
22 relief against a respondent, pending final determination of the
23 proceedings. If the court finds after a hearing held upon notice to
24 the respondent that there is reasonable cause to believe that the
25 respondent is engaging in or is likely to engage in the conduct
26 sought to be restrained, the court may grant any temporary relief
27 or restraining order the court considers appropriate.
28 Sec. 12. (a) The department may bring a civil action against a
29 creditor or a person acting on behalf of the creditor to recover a
30 civil penalty for willfully violating this article. If the court finds
31 that the defendant has engaged in a course of repeated and willful
32 violations of this article, the court may assess a civil penalty of not
33 more than five thousand dollars ($5,000). A civil penalty may not
34 be imposed under this subsection:
35 (1) for violations of this article occurring more than two (2)
36 years before the action is brought; or
37 (2) for making unconscionable agreements or engaging in a
38 course of fraudulent or unconscionable conduct.
39 (b) If the department determines, after notice and an
40 opportunity to be heard, that a person has violated this article, the
41 department may, in addition to or instead of all other remedies
42 available under this section, impose upon the person a civil penalty
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
132
1 not greater than ten thousand dollars ($10,000) per violation.
2 (c) If the department determines, after notice and opportunity
3 to be heard, that a person has willfully violated this article, the
4 department may, in addition to or instead of all other remedies
5 available under this section, order restitution against the person
6 subject to this article for a violation of this article.
7 Sec. 13. The grant of powers to the department under this
8 article does not affect remedies available to debtors under this
9 article or under other principles of law or equity.
10 Sec. 14. The department may bring an action or a proceeding in
11 a court in a county:
12 (1) in which an act on which the action or proceeding is based
13 occurred;
14 (2) in which the respondent resides or transacts business; or
15 (3) in which the action or proceeding is otherwise authorized
16 by rule or venue laws.
17 ARTICLE 2. UNIFORM CONSUMER CREDIT CODE
18 Chapter 1. General Provisions
19 Sec. 1. (a) This article shall be liberally construed and applied
20 to promote the following underlying purposes and policies of this
21 article:
22 (1) To simplify, clarify, and modernize the law governing
23 retail installment sales, consumer credit, small loans, and
24 usury.
25 (2) To provide rate ceilings to assure an adequate supply of
26 credit to consumers.
27 (3) To further consumer understanding of the terms of credit
28 transactions and to foster competition among suppliers of
29 consumer credit so that consumers may obtain credit at
30 reasonable cost.
31 (4) To protect consumer buyers, lessees, and borrowers
32 against unfair practices by some suppliers of consumer credit,
33 having due regard for the interests of legitimate and
34 scrupulous creditors.
35 (5) To permit and encourage the development of fair and
36 economically sound consumer credit practices.
37 (6) To conform the regulation of consumer credit transactions
38 to the policies of the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15
39 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.) and to applicable state and federal laws,
40 rules, regulations, policies, and guidance.
41 (7) To make uniform the law, including administrative rules,
42 among the various jurisdictions.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
133
1 (b) A reference to a requirement imposed by this article includes
2 reference to a related rule or guidance of the department adopted
3 pursuant to this article.
4 (c) A reference to a federal law in this article is a reference to
5 the law as in effect December 31, 2024.
6 (d) This article applies to a transaction if the director
7 determines that the transaction:
8 (1) is in substance a disguised consumer credit transaction; or
9 (2) involves the application of subterfuge for the purpose of
10 avoiding this article.
11 A determination by the director under this subsection must be in
12 writing and shall be delivered to all parties to the transaction.
13 IC 4-21.5-3 applies to a determination made under this subsection.
14 (e) The authority of this article remains in effect, whether a
15 licensee, an individual, or a person subject to this article acts or
16 claims to act under any licensing or registration law of this state,
17 or claims to act without such authority.
18 (f) A violation of a state or federal law, regulation, or rule
19 applicable to consumer credit transactions is a violation of this
20 article.
21 (g) The department may enforce penalty provisions set forth in
22 15 U.S.C. 1640 for violations of disclosure requirements applicable
23 to mortgage transactions.
24 Sec. 2. Unless displaced by the particular provisions of this
25 article, the Uniform Commercial Code (IC 26-1) and the principles
26 of law and equity (including the law relative to capacity to
27 contract, principal and agent, estoppel, fraud, misrepresentation,
28 duress, coercion, mistake, bankruptcy, or other validating or
29 invalidating cause) shall supplement the provisions of this article.
30 Sec. 3. This article is a general statute intended to provide
31 unified coverage of its subject matter. Subsequent legislation may
32 not be construed to repeal any part of the article by implication if
33 the construction can reasonably be avoided.
34 Sec. 4. The provisions of this article are severable, so that if:
35 (1) any provisions of this article; or
36 (2) the application of this article to any person or
37 circumstances;
38 is held invalid, the invalidity does not affect other provisions or
39 applications of this article that can be given effect without the
40 invalid provision or application.
41 Sec. 5. (a) The dollar amounts in this article designated as
42 subject to change shall change, as provided in this section,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
134
1 according to the Consumer Price Index for Urban Wage Earners
2 and Clerical Workers: U.S. City Average, All Items, 1957-59 equals
3 100, compiled by Bureau of Labor Statistics, United States
4 Department of Labor, and referred to in this section as the Index.
5 The Index for October 1971, is the Reference Base Index.
6 (b) The dollar amounts shall change on January 1 of each
7 odd-numbered year if the percentage of change, calculated to the
8 nearest whole percentage point, between the Index at the end of the
9 preceding odd-numbered year and the Reference Base Index is ten
10 percent (10%) or more, except that:
11 (1) the portion of the percentage change in the Index in excess
12 of a multiple of ten percent (10%) shall be disregarded and
13 the dollar amounts shall change only in multiples of ten
14 percent (10%) of the amounts on March 5, 1971;
15 (2) the dollar amounts shall not change if the amounts
16 required by this section are those currently in effect pursuant
17 to this article as a result of earlier application of the section;
18 and
19 (3) in no event shall the dollar amounts be reduced below the
20 amounts appearing in this article on March 5, 1971.
21 (c) If the Index is revised after December 1967, the percentage
22 of change shall be calculated on the basis of the revised Index. If
23 the revision of the Index changes the Reference Base Index, a
24 revised Reference Base Index shall be determined by multiplying
25 the Reference Base Index by the ratio of the revised Index to the
26 current Index, as each was for the first month in which the revised
27 Index is available. If the Index is superseded, the Index is the one
28 represented by the Bureau of Labor Statistics as reflecting most
29 accurately changes in the purchasing power of the dollar for
30 consumers.
31 (d) The department shall issue a rule under IC 4-22-2
32 announcing:
33 (1) sixty (60) days before January 1 of each odd-numbered
34 year in which dollar amounts are to change, the changes in
35 dollar amounts required by subsection (b); and
36 (2) promptly after the changes occur, changes in the Index
37 required by subsection (c), including, when applicable, the
38 numerical equivalent of the Reference Base Index under a
39 revised Reference Base Index and the designation or title of
40 any index superseding the Index.
41 (e) A person does not violate this article through a transaction
42 otherwise complying with this article if the person relies on dollar
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
135
1 amounts either determined according to subsection (b) or
2 appearing in the last rule of the department announcing the then
3 current dollar amounts.
4 Sec. 6. (a) Except as otherwise provided in this article, a buyer,
5 lessee, or debtor may not waive or agree to forego rights or
6 benefits under this article.
7 (b) A claim by a buyer, lessee, or debtor against a creditor for
8 an excess charge, other violation of this article, or civil penalty, or
9 a claim against a buyer, lessee, or debtor for default or breach of
10 a duty imposed by this article, if disputed in good faith, may be
11 settled by agreement.
12 (c) A claim, whether or not disputed against a buyer, lessee, or
13 debtor may be settled for less value than the amount claimed.
14 (d) A settlement in which the buyer, lessee, or debtor waives or
15 agrees to forego rights or benefits under this article is invalid if the
16 court as a matter of law finds the settlement to have been
17 unconscionable at the time it was made. The competence of the
18 buyer, lessee, or debtor, any deception or coercion practiced upon
19 the buyer, lessee, or debtor, the nature and extent of the legal
20 advice received by the buyer, lessee, or debtor, and the value of the
21 consideration are relevant to the issue of unconscionability.
22 Sec. 7. (a) This article prescribes maximum charges for all
23 creditors, except lessors and those excluded under section 10 of this
24 chapter, extending consumer credit, including consumer credit
25 sales, consumer loans, and consumer related sales and loans, and
26 displaces existing limitations on the powers of those creditors based
27 on maximum charges.
28 (b) With respect to sellers of goods or services, small loan
29 companies, licensed lenders, consumer and sales finance
30 companies, industrial loan and investment companies, and
31 commercial banks and trust companies, this article displaces
32 existing limitations on their powers based solely on amount or
33 duration of credit.
34 (c) Except as provided in subsection (a) and IC 24-4.6-1, this
35 article does not displace limitations on powers of credit unions,
36 savings banks, savings or building and loan associations, or other
37 thrift institutions whether organized for the profit of shareholders
38 or as mutual organizations.
39 (d) Except as provided in subsections (a) and (b), this article
40 does not displace:
41 (1) limitations on powers of depository institutions with
42 respect to the amount of a loan to a single borrower, the ratio
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
136
1 of a loan to the value of collateral, the duration of a loan that
2 is a mortgage transaction, or other similar restrictions
3 designed to protect deposits; or
4 (2) limitations on powers an organization is authorized to
5 exercise under the laws of this state or the United States.
6 Sec. 8. All persons licensed on October 1, 1971, under:
7 (1) IC 24-5-4 (before its repeal on October 1, 1971);
8 (2) IC 28-7-4 (before its repeal on October 1, 1971);
9 (3) IC 28-7-2 (before its repeal on October 1, 1971); or
10 (4) IC 28-5-1-4;
11 are licensed to make supervised loans under this article, subject to
12 the renewal provisions contained in this article. All provisions of
13 this article apply to the persons previously licensed or authorized.
14 The department may deliver evidence of licensing to the persons
15 previously licensed or authorized.
16 Sec. 9. (a) Except as otherwise provided in this section, this
17 article applies to sales, leases, and loans made in this state and to
18 modifications, including refinancings, consolidations, and
19 deferrals, made in this state, of sales, leases, and loans, wherever
20 made. For purposes of this article, the following apply:
21 (1) A sale or modification of a sale agreement is made in this
22 state if the buyer's agreement or offer to purchase or to
23 modify is received by the seller or a person acting on behalf of
24 the seller in this state.
25 (2) A lease or modification of a lease agreement is made in this
26 state if the lessee's agreement or offer to lease or to modify is
27 received by the lessor or a person acting on behalf of the
28 lessor in this state.
29 (3) A loan or modification of a loan agreement is made in this
30 state if a writing signed by the debtor and evidencing the debt
31 is received by the lender or a person acting on behalf of the
32 lender in this state.
33 (4) Except as provided in subdivisions (5) and (6), a sale, lease,
34 or loan transaction occurs in Indiana if a consumer who is a
35 resident of Indiana enters into a consumer sale, lease, or loan
36 transaction with a creditor or a person acting on behalf of the
37 creditor in another state and the creditor or the person acting
38 on behalf of the creditor has advertised or solicited sales,
39 leases, or loans in Indiana by any means, including by mail,
40 brochure, telephone, print, radio, television, the Internet, or
41 electronic means.
42 (5) A sale, lease, or loan transaction does not occur in Indiana
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
137
1 if a consumer who is a resident of Indiana enters into a
2 consumer sale, lease, or loan transaction secured by an
3 interest in land located outside Indiana.
4 (6) A sale, lease, or loan transaction does not occur in Indiana
5 if a consumer who is a resident of Indiana enters into a
6 consumer sale, lease, or loan transaction at a creditor's place
7 of business in another state.
8 For purposes of subdivisions (1) through (4), an offer is received by
9 a creditor or a person acting on behalf of the creditor in Indiana if
10 the offer is physically delivered, or otherwise transmitted or
11 communicated, to a person who has actual or apparent authority
12 to act for the creditor or the person acting on behalf of the creditor
13 in Indiana, regardless of whether approval, acceptance, or
14 ratification by any other agent or representative of the creditor or
15 the person acting on behalf of the creditor in another state is
16 necessary to give legal consequence to the consumer credit
17 transaction.
18 (b) IC 37-2-6-1 through IC 37-2-6-7 apply to actions or other
19 proceedings brought in this state to enforce rights arising from
20 consumer credit sales, consumer leases, or consumer loans, or
21 extortionate extensions of credit, wherever made.
22 (c) Except as provided in subsection (b), a sale, lease, loan, or
23 modification thereof, made in another state to a person who was
24 not a resident of this state when the sale, lease, loan, or
25 modification was made is valid and enforceable in this state
26 according to its terms to the extent that it is valid and enforceable
27 under the laws of the state applicable to the transaction.
28 (d) For the purposes of this article, the residence of a buyer,
29 lessee, or debtor is the address given by the buyer, lessee, or debtor
30 as the buyer's, lessee's, or debtor's residence in any writing or
31 electronic communication made by the buyer, lessee, or debtor in
32 connection with a credit transaction. Until the buyer, lessee, or
33 debtor notifies the creditor or the person acting on behalf of the
34 creditor of a new or different address, the given address is
35 presumed to be unchanged.
36 (e) Notwithstanding other provisions of this section:
37 (1) except as provided in subsection (b), this article does not
38 apply if the buyer, lessee, or debtor is not a resident of this
39 state at the time of a credit transaction and the parties then
40 agree that the law of the buyer's, lessee's, or debtor's
41 residence applies; and
42 (2) this article applies if the buyer, lessee, or debtor is a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
138
1 resident of this state at the time of a credit transaction and the
2 parties then agree that the law of this state applies.
3 (f) Except as provided in subsection (e), the following
4 agreements by a buyer, lessee, or debtor are invalid with respect to
5 consumer credit sales, consumer leases, consumer loans, or
6 modifications thereof, to which this article applies:
7 (1) An agreement that the law of another state shall apply.
8 (2) An agreement that the buyer, lessee, or debtor consents to
9 the jurisdiction of another state.
10 (3) An agreement that fixes venue.
11 (g) The following provisions of this article specify the applicable
12 law governing certain cases:
13 (1) IC 37-2-7-1 (applicability of the provisions on powers and
14 functions of the department).
15 (2) IC 37-2-7-24 (applicability of the provisions on notification
16 and fees).
17 (h) If a creditor or a person acting on behalf of the creditor has
18 violated the provisions of this article that apply to the authority to
19 make consumer loans (IC 37-2-4-23), the loan is void and the
20 debtor is not obligated to pay either the principal or loan finance
21 charge, as set forth in IC 37-2-6-9.
22 Sec. 10. This article does not apply to the following:
23 (1) Extensions of credit to or by a government or
24 governmental agencies or instrumentalities.
25 (2) The sale of insurance by an insurer, except as otherwise
26 provided in the chapter on insurance (IC 37-2-5).
27 (3) Transactions under public utility, municipal utility, or
28 common carrier tariffs if a subdivision or agency of this state
29 or of the United States regulates the charges for the services
30 involved, the charges for delayed payment, and any discount
31 allowed for early payment.
32 (4) The rates and charges and the disclosure of rates and
33 charges of a licensed pawnbroker established in accordance
34 with a statute or ordinance concerning these matters.
35 (5) A sale of goods, services, or an interest in land in which the
36 goods, services, or interest in land are purchased primarily
37 for a purpose other than a personal, family, or household
38 purpose.
39 (6) A loan in which the debt is incurred primarily for a
40 purpose other than a personal, family, or household purpose.
41 (7) An extension of credit primarily for a business, a
42 commercial, or an agricultural purpose.

35 Sec. 11. In examinations or other regulatory activities conducted
36 by the department and related to licensees under this article, the
37 department may cooperate with the Indiana securities
38 commissioner in the regulation of individuals who, in addition to
39 conducting business regulated under this article, also conduct a

41 Chapter 2. Definitions
42 Sec. 1. The definitions set forth in this chapter apply throughout
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
141
1 this article:
2 Sec. 2. "Affiliate", with respect to any person subject to this
3 article, means a person that, directly or indirectly, through one (1)
4 or more intermediaries:
5 (1) controls;
6 (2) is controlled by; or
7 (3) is under common control with;
8 the person subject to this article.
9 Sec. 2.5. "Amount financed" means the total of the following to
10 the extent that payment is deferred:
11 (1) The cash price of the goods, services, or interest in land
12 less the amount of down payment whether made in cash or
13 property.
14 (2) The amount actually paid or to be paid by the seller
15 pursuant to an agreement with the buyer to discharge a
16 security interest or lien on property traded in.
17 (3) If not included in the cash price:
18 (A) any applicable sales, use, excise or documentary fees;
19 (B) amounts actually paid or to be paid by the seller for
20 registration, certificate of title, or license fees; and
21 (C) additional charges permitted by IC 37-2-3-5.
22 Sec. 3. "Agreement" means the bargain of the parties in fact as
23 found in their language or by implication from other
24 circumstances, including course of dealing or usage of trade or
25 course of performance.
26 Sec. 4. "Agricultural purpose" means a purpose related to the
27 production, harvest, exhibition, marketing, transportation,
28 processing, or manufacture of agricultural products by a natural
29 person who cultivates, plants, propagates, or nurtures the
30 agricultural products. "Agricultural products" includes
31 agricultural, horticultural, viticultural, and dairy products,
32 livestock, wildlife, poultry, bees, forest products, fish and shellfish,
33 and any and all products raised or produced on farms and any
34 processed or manufactured products thereof.
35 Sec. 5. "Average daily balance" means the sum of each of the
36 daily balances in a billing cycle divided by the number of days in
37 the billing cycle, and if the billing cycle is a month, the creditor
38 may elect to treat the number of days in each billing cycle as thirty
39 (30).
40 Sec. 6. "Balloon payment", with respect to a mortgage
41 transaction, means any payment that:
42 (1) the creditor requires the debtor to make at any time
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
142
1 during the term of the mortgage;
2 (2) represents the entire amount of the outstanding balance
3 with respect to the mortgage; and
4 (3) the entire amount of which is due as of a specified date or
5 at the end of a specified period;
6 if the aggregate amount of the minimum periodic payments
7 required under the mortgage would not fully amortize the
8 outstanding balance by the specified date or at the end of the
9 specified period. The term does not include a payment required by
10 a creditor under a due-on-sale clause (as defined in 12 U.S.C.
11 1701j-3(a)) or a payment required by a creditor under a provision
12 in the mortgage that permits the creditor to accelerate the debt
13 upon the debtor's default or failure to abide by the material terms
14 of the mortgage.
15 Sec. 7. "Bona fide nonprofit organization" means an
16 organization that does the following, as determined by the director
17 under criteria established by the director:
18 (1) Maintains tax exempt status under Section 501(c)(3) of the
19 Internal Revenue Code.
20 (2) Promotes affordable housing or provides home ownership
21 education or similar services.
22 (3) Conducts the organization's activities in a manner that
23 serves public or charitable purposes.
24 (4) Receives funding and revenue and charges fees in a
25 manner that does not encourage the organization or the
26 organization's employees to act other than in the best interests
27 of the organization's clients.
28 (5) Compensates the organization's employees in a manner
29 that does not encourage employees to act other than in the
30 best interests of the organization's clients.
31 (6) Provides to, or identifies for, debtors mortgage
32 transactions with terms that are favorable to the debtor (as
33 described in IC 37-2-1-10(15)) and comparable to mortgage
34 transactions and housing assistance provided under
35 government housing assistance programs.
36 (7) Maintains certification by the United States Department
37 of Housing and Urban Development or employs counselors
38 who are certified by the Indiana housing and community
39 development authority.
40 Sec. 7.5. "Cash price" means the price at which the goods,
41 services, or interest in land are offered for sale by the seller to cash
42 buyers in the ordinary course of business, and may include:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
143
1 (1) applicable sales, use, and excise and documentary fees;
2 (2) the cash price of accessories or related services such as
3 delivery, installation, servicing, repairs, alterations, service
4 contracts, and improvements; and
5 (3) amounts actually paid or to be paid by the seller for
6 registration, certificate of title, or license fees.
7 Sec. 7.7. "Civil court" means any court of Indiana having civil
8 jurisdiction.
9 Sec. 8. "Closing costs" with respect to a subordinate lien
10 mortgage transaction includes:
11 (1) fees or premiums for title examination, title insurance, or
12 similar purposes, including surveys;
13 (2) fees for preparation of a deed, settlement statement, or
14 other documents;
15 (3) escrows for future payments of taxes and insurance;
16 (4) fees for notarizing deeds and other documents;
17 (5) appraisal fees; and
18 (6) fees for credit reports.
19 Sec. 9. "Conspicuous" refers to a term or clause when it is so
20 written that a reasonable person against whom it is to operate
21 ought to have noticed it.
22 Sec. 10. "Consumer credit" means credit offered or extended to
23 a consumer primarily for a personal, family, or household purpose.
24 Sec. 10.5. "Consumer credit insurance" means insurance, other
25 than insurance on property, by which the satisfaction of debt in
26 whole or in part is a benefit provided, but does not include:
27 (1) insurance issued as an isolated transaction on the part of
28 the insurer not related to an agreement or plan for insuring
29 debtors of the creditor; or
30 (2) insurance indemnifying the creditor against loss due to the
31 debtor's default.
32 Sec. 11. "Consumer Credit Protection Act" refers to the
33 Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.), as
34 amended, and includes both the Truth in Lending Simplification
35 and Reform Act amendments (Public Law 96-221, Title VI, 94 Stat.
36 168) and any regulations issued under those laws. However, the
37 department may otherwise define this term by rule issued in
38 accordance with IC 37-2-7-7.
39 Sec. 12. "Consumer credit sale" is a sale of goods, services, or
40 an interest in land in which:
41 (1) credit is granted by a person who regularly engages as a
42 seller in credit transactions of the same kind;
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
144
1 (2) the buyer is a person other than an organization;
2 (3) the goods, services, or interest in land are purchased
3 primarily for a personal, family, or household purpose;
4 (4) either the debt is payable in installments or a credit service
5 charge is made; and
6 (5) with respect to a sale of goods or services, either:
7 (A) the amount of credit extended, the written credit limit,
8 or the initial advance does not exceed the exempt threshold
9 amount, as adjusted in accordance with the annual
10 adjustment of the exempt threshold amount, specified in
11 Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as
12 applicable); or
13 (B) the debt is secured by personal property used or
14 expected to be used as the principal dwelling of the buyer.
15 Unless the sale is made subject to this article by agreement
16 under IC 37-2-3-29, "consumer credit sale" does not include
17 a sale in which the seller allows the buyer to purchase goods
18 or services pursuant to a lender credit card or similar
19 arrangement or, except as provided with respect to disclosure
20 (IC 37-2-3-13), debtors' remedies (IC 37-2-6-8), providing
21 payoff amounts (IC 37-2-3-11), and powers and functions of
22 the department (IC 37-2-7), a sale of an interest in land which
23 is a first lien mortgage transaction.
24 Sec. 12.5. "Consumer lease" means a lease of goods:
25 (1) which a lessor regularly engaged in the business of leasing
26 makes to a person, other than an organization, who takes
27 under the lease primarily for a personal, family, or household
28 purpose;
29 (2) in which the amount payable under the lease does not
30 exceed the exempt threshold amount, as adjusted in
31 accordance with the annual adjustment of the exempt
32 threshold amount, specified in Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or
33 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as applicable); and
34 (3) which is for a term exceeding four (4) months.
35 The term does not include a lease made pursuant to a lender credit
36 card or similar arrangement.
37 Sec. 13. "Consumer loan" means a loan made by a person
38 regularly engaged in the business of making loans in which:
39 (1) the debtor is a person other than an organization;
40 (2) the debt is primarily for a personal, family, or household
41 purpose;
42 (3) either the debt is payable in installments or a loan finance
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
145
1 charge is made; and
2 (4) either:
3 (A) the amount of credit extended, the written credit limit,
4 or the initial advance does not exceed the exempt threshold
5 amount, as adjusted in accordance with the annual
6 adjustment of the exempt threshold amount, specified in
7 Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as
8 applicable); or
9 (B) the debt is secured by an interest in land or by personal
10 property used or expected to be used as the principal
11 dwelling of the debtor.
12 Except as described in IC 37-2-4-2, the term does not include a first
13 lien mortgage transaction.
14 Sec. 13.3. "Consumer related loan" refers to a loan in which the
15 following apply:
16 (1) The loan is made by a person who is not regularly engaged
17 as a lender in credit transactions of the same kind.
18 (2) The debtor is a person other than an organization.
19 (3) The debt is primarily for a personal, family, or household
20 purpose.
21 (4) Either the debt is payable in installments or a loan finance
22 charge is made.
23 (5) Either:
24 (A) the amount of credit extended, the written credit limit,
25 or the initial advance does not exceed the exempt threshold
26 amount, as adjusted in accordance with the annual
27 adjustment of the exempt threshold amount, specified in
28 Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as
29 applicable); or
30 (B) the debt is secured by an interest in land or by personal
31 property used or expected to be used as the principal
32 dwelling of the debtor.
33 Sec. 13.5. "Consumer related sale" means a sale of goods,
34 services, or an interest in land in which:
35 (1) credit is granted by a person that is not regularly engaged
36 as a seller in credit transactions of the same kind;
37 (2) the buyer is a person other than an organization;
38 (3) the goods, services, or interest in land are purchased
39 primarily for a personal, family, or household purpose;
40 (4) either the debt is payable in installments or a credit service
41 charge is made; and
42 (5) with respect to a sale of goods or services:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
146
1 (A) either the amount of credit extended, the written credit

3 threshold amount, as adjusted in accordance with the
4 annual adjustment of the exempt threshold amount,

6 1026.3(b), as applicable); or
7 (B) the debt is secured by personal property used or
