9 Sec. 14. "Credit" means the right granted by a creditor to a
10 debtor to defer payment of debt or to incur debt and defer its
11 payment.
12 Sec. 14.5. "Credit service charge" means the sum of:
13 (1) all charges payable directly or indirectly by the buyer and
14 imposed directly or indirectly by the seller as an incident to
15 the extension of credit, including any of the following types of
16 charges which are applicable: time price differential, service,
17 carrying or other charge, however denominated, premium or
18 other charge for any guarantee or insurance protecting the
19 seller against the buyer's default or other credit loss; and
20 (2) charges incurred for investigating the collateral or
21 credit-worthiness of the buyer.
22 The term does not include charges as a result of default, additional
23 charges (IC 37-2-3-5), delinquency charges (IC 37-2-3-6), or
24 deferral charges (IC 37-2-3-7). The term does not include charges
25 paid or payable to a third party that are not required by the seller
26 as a condition or incident to the extension of credit except for
27 borrower paid mortgage broker fees, including fees paid directly
28 to the broker or the seller (for delivery to the broker), whether the
29 fees are paid in cash or financed. However, borrower paid
30 mortgage broker fees do not include fees paid to a mortgage broker
31 by a creditor, including yield spread premiums and service release
32 fees.
33 Sec. 15. "Creditor" means a person:
34 (1) who regularly engages in the extension of consumer credit
35 that is subject to a credit service charge or loan finance
36 charge, as applicable, or is payable by written agreement in
37 more than four (4) installments (not including a down
38 payment); and
39 (2) to whom the obligation is initially payable, either on the
40 face of the note or contract, or by agreement when there is not
41 a note or contract.
42 Sec. 15.5. "Debt cancellation agreement" means an agreement
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
147
1 that provides coverage for payment or satisfaction of all or part of
2 a debt in the event of the loss of life, health, or income. The term
3 does not include a GAP agreement.
4 Sec. 15.7. "Department" refers to the department of financial
5 institutions.
6 Sec. 16. "Depository institution" has the meaning set forth in
7 the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (12 U.S.C. 1813(c)) and includes
8 any credit union.
9 Sec. 17. "Director" means the director of the department of
10 financial institutions or the director's designee.
11 Sec. 18. "Dwelling" means a residential structure that contains
12 one (1) to four (4) units, regardless of whether the structure is
13 attached to real property. The term includes an individual:
14 (1) condominium unit;
15 (2) cooperative unit;
16 (3) mobile home; or
17 (4) trailer;
18 that is used as a residence.
19 Sec. 19. "Earnings" means compensation paid or payable for
20 personal services, whether denominated as wages, salary,
21 commission, bonus, or otherwise, and includes periodic payments
22 under a pension or retirement program.
23 Sec. 20. "Employee" means an individual who is paid wages or
24 other compensation by an employer required under federal income
25 tax law to file Form W-2 on behalf of the individual.
26 Sec. 20.5. "Expedited payment service" means a service offered
27 to a consumer to ensure that a payment made by the consumer
28 with respect to a consumer credit sale or a consumer loan will be
29 reflected as paid and posted on an expedited basis.
30 Sec. 21. "Federal banking agencies" means the Board of
31 Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the
32 Comptroller of the Currency, the National Credit Union
33 Administration, and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation.
34 Sec. 21.5. "First lien mortgage transaction" means:
35 (1) a consumer loan; or
36 (2) a consumer credit sale;
37 that is or will be used by the debtor primarily for personal, family,
38 or household purposes and that is secured by a mortgage or a land
39 contract (or another consensual security interest equivalent to a
40 mortgage or a land contract) that constitutes a first lien on a
41 dwelling or on residential real estate upon which a dwelling is
42 constructed or intended to be constructed.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
148
1 Sec. 22. "Guaranteed asset protection agreement", "guaranteed
2 auto protection agreement", or "GAP agreement" means, with
3 respect to a consumer credit sale or a consumer loan involving a
4 motor vehicle or another titled asset, an agreement in which the
5 seller or lender agrees to cancel or waive all or part of the
6 outstanding debt after all property insurance benefits have been
7 exhausted after the occurrence of a specified event.
8 Sec. 22.5. "Goods" includes goods not in existence at the time
9 the transaction is entered into and merchandise certificates, but
10 excludes money, chattel paper, documents of title, and instruments.
11 Sec. 22.7. "Home solicitation sale" means a consumer credit sale
12 of goods, other than farm equipment, or services in which:
13 (1) the seller or a person acting for the seller engages in a
14 personal solicitation of the sale, including a solicitation over
15 the telephone, at a residence of the buyer and the buyer's
16 agreement or offer to purchase is there given to the seller or
17 a person acting for the seller; or
18 (2) the seller or the seller's agent, solicits a sale in a city or
19 town in which the seller does not have a permanent business
20 establishment, through mailings, advertisements, or telephone
21 calls, which require the buyer to meet the seller or the seller's
22 agent at a place other than the seller's permanent business
23 establishment.
24 The term does not include a sale made pursuant to a preexisting
25 revolving charge account, or a sale made pursuant to prior
26 negotiations between the parties at a business establishment at a
27 fixed location where goods or services are offered or exhibited for
28 sale.
29 Sec. 23. "Immediate family member" means a spouse, child,
30 sibling, parent, grandparent, or grandchild. The term includes
31 stepparents, stepchildren, stepsiblings, and adoptive relationships.
32 Sec. 24. "Individual" means a natural person.
33 Sec. 25. "Land contract" means a contract for the sale of real
34 estate in which the seller of the real estate retains legal title to the
35 real estate until the total contract price is paid by the buyer.
36 Sec. 25.5. Except as otherwise provided, "lender" means a
37 person regularly engaged in making consumer loans. The term
38 includes an assignee of the lender's right to payment but use of the
39 term does not in itself impose on an assignee any obligation of the
40 lender with respect to events occurring before the assignment.
41 Sec. 26. "Lender credit card or similar arrangement" means an
42 arrangement or loan agreement, other than a seller credit card,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
149
1 pursuant to which a lender gives a debtor the privilege of using a
2 credit card, letter of credit, or other credit confirmation or
3 identification in transactions out of which debt arises:
4 (1) by the lender's honoring a draft or similar order for the
5 payment of money drawn or accepted by the debtor;
6 (2) by the lender's payment or agreement to pay the debtor's
7 obligations; or
8 (3) by the lender's purchase from the obligee of the debtor's
9 obligations.
10 Sec. 27. "Licensee" means a person licensed as a creditor under
11 this article.
12 Sec. 27.5. "Loan" includes:
13 (1) the creation of debt by the lender's payment of or
14 agreement to pay money to the debtor or to a third party for
15 the account of the debtor;
16 (2) the creation of debt by a credit to an account with the
17 lender upon which the debtor is entitled to draw immediately;
18 (3) the creation of debt pursuant to a lender credit card or
19 similar arrangement; and
20 (4) the forbearance of debt arising from a loan.
21 Sec. 28. "Loan brokerage business" means any activity in which
22 a person, in return for any consideration from any source,
23 procures, attempts to procure, or assists in procuring, a mortgage
24 transaction from a third party or any other person, whether or not
25 the person seeking the mortgage transaction actually obtains the
26 mortgage transaction.
27 Sec. 28.5. (a) "Loan finance charge" means the sum of:
28 (1) all charges payable directly or indirectly by the debtor and
29 imposed directly or indirectly by the lender as an incident to
30 the extension of credit, including any of the following types of
31 charges which are applicable: interest or any amount payable
32 under a point, discount, or other system of charges, however
33 denominated, premium or other charge for any guarantee or
34 insurance protecting the lender against the debtor's default or
35 other credit loss; and
36 (2) charges incurred for investigating the collateral or
37 credit-worthiness of the debtor.
38 The term does not include charges as a result of default, additional
39 charges (IC 37-2-4-5), delinquency charges (IC 37-2-4-6), or
40 deferral charges (IC 37-3-4-7). The term does not include charges
41 paid or payable to a third party that are not required by the lender
42 as a condition or incident to the extension of credit except for
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
150
1 borrower paid mortgage broker fees, including fees paid directly
2 to the broker or the lender (for delivery to the broker), whether the
3 fees are paid in cash or financed. However, borrower paid
4 mortgage broker fees do not include fees paid to a mortgage broker
5 by a creditor, including yield spread premiums and service release
6 fees.
7 (b) If a lender makes a loan to a debtor by purchasing or
8 satisfying obligations of the debtor pursuant to a lender credit card
9 or similar arrangement, and the purchase or satisfaction is made
10 at less than the face amount of the obligation, the discount is not
11 part of the loan finance charge.
12 Sec. 29. "Loan processor or underwriter" means an individual
13 who performs clerical or support duties as an employee at the
14 direction of, and subject to the supervision and instruction of, a
15 person licensed to engage in mortgage transactions or a person
16 exempt from licensing. For purposes of this subdivision, the term
17 "clerical or support duties" may include, after the receipt of an
18 application, the following:
19 (1) The receipt, collection, distribution, and analysis of
20 information common for the processing or underwriting of a
21 mortgage transaction.
22 (2) The communication with a consumer to obtain the
23 information necessary for the processing or underwriting of
24 a loan, to the extent that the communication does not include:
25 (A) offering or negotiating loan rates or terms; or
26 (B) counseling consumers about mortgage transaction
27 rates or terms.
28 The term "loan processor or underwriter" does not include an
29 individual who is an employee of a person that is not engaged in
30 mortgage transactions as a creditor if that person is permitted to
31 voluntarily register with the department to sponsor the individual
32 under IC 37-1-1-6(8) to engage solely in the activities described in
33 this subdivision. An individual engaging solely in loan processor or
34 underwriter activities shall not represent to the public through
35 advertising or other means of communicating or providing
36 information, including the use of business cards, stationery,
37 brochures, signs, rate lists, or other promotional items, that the
38 individual can or will perform any of the activities of a mortgage
39 loan originator. However, an individual who is licensed as a
40 mortgage loan originator under IC 37-1 and 750 IAC 9-3, and who
41 is sponsored by a person, as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8), to engage
42 solely as a third party loan processor or underwriter, is subject to

3 Sec. 29.5. "Merchandise certificate" means a writing issued by

6 Sec. 30. "Mortgage loan originator" means an individual who,

8 gain, regularly engages in taking a mortgage transaction
9 application or in offering or negotiating the terms of a mortgage

25 party processor or underwriter.

28 (B) a loan broker;

32 (C);

35 applicable state law.

37 timeshare plans (as defined in 11 U.S.C. 101(53D)).
38 Sec. 31. "Mortgage servicer" means the last person to whom a
39 mortgagor or the mortgagor's successor in interest has been
40 instructed by a mortgagee to send payments on a loan secured by
41 a mortgage.

1 (1) a consumer loan; or

9 Sec. 33. "Nationwide Multistate Licensing System and Registry"

19 Sec. 34. "Nontraditional mortgage product" means any

22 Sec. 35. "Official fees" means:

37 any other entity, however organized.

39 required or permitted by written agreement to be made in more
40 than four (4) installments not including a down payment.
41 Sec. 38. "Person" includes an individual or an organization.

41 Sec. 44.7. "Revolving charge account" means an arrangement
42 between a seller and a buyer pursuant to which:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
155
1 (1) the seller may permit the buyer to purchase goods or
2 services on credit either from the seller or pursuant to a seller
3 credit card;
4 (2) the unpaid balances of amounts financed arising from
5 purchases and the credit service and other appropriate
6 charges are debited to an account;
7 (3) a credit service charge if made is not precomputed but is
8 computed on the outstanding unpaid balances of the buyer's
9 account from time to time; and
10 (4) the buyer has the privilege of paying the balances in
11 installments.
12 Sec. 45. "Revolving loan account" means an arrangement
13 between a lender and a debtor pursuant to which:
14 (1) the lender may permit the debtor to obtain loans from
15 time to time;
16 (2) the unpaid balances of principal and the loan finance and
17 other appropriate charges are debited to an account;
18 (3) a loan finance charge if made is not precomputed but is
19 computed on the outstanding unpaid balances of the debtor's
20 account from time to time; and
21 (4) the debtor has the privilege of paying the balances in
22 installments.
23 Sec. 45.1. "Sale of an interest in land" includes a lease in which
24 the lessee has an option to purchase the interest and all or a
25 substantial part of the rental or other payments previously made
26 by the lessee are applied to the purchase price.
27 Sec. 45.3. "Sale of goods" includes any agreement in the form of
28 a bailment or lease of goods if the bailee or lessee agrees to pay as
29 compensation for use a sum substantially equivalent to or in excess
30 of the aggregate value of the goods involved and it is agreed that
31 the bailee or lessee will become, or for no other or a nominal
32 consideration has the option to become, the owner of the goods
33 upon full compliance with the bailee's or the lessee's obligations
34 under the agreement.
35 Sec. 45.5. "Sale of services" means furnishing or agreeing to
36 furnish services and includes making arrangements to have
37 services furnished by another.
38 Sec. 45.7. Except as otherwise provided,"seller" means a person

27 under a consumer loan without resulting in default.

3 Chapter 3. Credit Sales
4 Sec. 1. (a) This chapter applies to consumer credit sales,
5 including home solicitation sales, and consumer leases.
6 (b) Sections 14 through 27 of this chapter apply to consumer
7 credit sales and consumer leases.
8 (c) Sections 29 through 33 of this chapter apply to consumer
9 related sales.
10 (d) Licensing under IC 37-2-4-24 applies to consumer credit
11 sales that are subordinate lien mortgage transactions.
12 Sec. 2. A sale, refinancing, or consolidation is considered
13 precomputed if the debt is expressed as a sum comprising the
14 amount financed and the amount of the credit service charge
15 computed in advance.
16 Sec. 3. The cash price stated by the seller to the buyer pursuant
17 to section 13 of this chapter is presumed to be the cash price.
18 Sec. 4. (a) Except as provided in subsections (h) and (k), with
19 respect to a consumer credit sale, a seller may contract for and
20 receive a credit service charge not exceeding that permitted by this
21 section.
22 (b) The credit service charge, calculated according to the
23 actuarial method, may not exceed the equivalent of the greater of:
24 (1) the total of:
25 (A) thirty-six percent (36%) per year on that part of the
26 unpaid balances of the amount financed which is two
27 thousand dollars ($2,000) or less;
28 (B) twenty-one percent (21%) per year on that part of the
29 unpaid balances of the amount financed which is more
30 than two thousand dollars ($2,000) but does not exceed
31 four thousand dollars ($4,000); and
32 (C) fifteen percent (15%) per year on that part of the
33 unpaid balances of the amount financed which is more
34 than four thousand dollars ($4,000); or
35 (2) twenty-five percent (25%) per year on the unpaid balances
36 of the amount financed.
37 (c) In the case of a sale agreement entered into before July 1,
38 2020, this section does not limit or restrict the manner of
39 contracting for the credit service charge, whether by way of
40 add-on, discount, or otherwise, so long as the rate of the credit
41 service charge does not exceed that permitted by this section. If the
42 sale is precomputed:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
158
1 (1) the credit service charge may be calculated on the
2 assumption that all scheduled payments will be made when
3 due; and
4 (2) the effect of prepayment is governed by the provisions on
5 rebate upon prepayment in section 12 of this chapter.
6 (d) The following apply to a sale agreement for a consumer
7 credit sale (or for the refinancing or consolidation of a consumer
8 credit sale) that is entered into after June 30, 2020:
9 (1) The credit service charge authorized by this section must
10 be:
11 (A) contracted for between the seller and the debtor; and
12 (B) calculated by applying a rate not exceeding the rate set
13 forth in subsection (b) to unpaid balances of the amount
14 financed.
15 (2) A sale agreement for a precomputed consumer credit sale
16 is prohibited.
17 (3) Subject to subsection (m), in addition to the credit service
18 charge authorized by subsection (b) and to any other fees
19 permitted by this chapter, and not subject to the rate set forth
20 in subsection (b), the seller may contract for and receive as a
21 condition for, or an incident to, the extension of credit a
22 nonrefundable prepaid finance charge under subsection (k),
23 whether the charge is:
24 (A) paid separately in cash or by check before or at
25 consummation; or
26 (B) withheld from the proceeds of the consumer credit sale.
27 (e) For the purposes of this section, the term of a sale agreement
28 commences with the date the credit is granted or, if goods are
29 delivered or services performed more than thirty (30) days after
30 that date, with the date of commencement of delivery or
31 performance except as follows:
32 (1) This subdivision applies to a delay attributable to the
33 customer. Where the customer requests delivery after the
34 thirty (30) day period or where delivery occurs after the
35 thirty (30) day period for a reason attributable to the
36 customer (including but not limited to failure to close on a
37 residence or failure to obtain lease approval), the term of the
38 sale agreement shall commence with the date credit is
39 granted.
40 (2) This subdivision applies to a partial delivery. Where any
41 portion of the order has been delivered within the thirty (30)
42 day period, the term of the sale agreement shall commence
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
159
1 with the date credit is granted.
2 Differences in the lengths of months are disregarded and a day
3 may be counted as one-thirtieth (1/30) of a month. Subject to
4 classifications and differentiations the seller may reasonably
5 establish, a part of a month in excess of fifteen (15) days may be
6 treated as a full month if periods of fifteen (15) days or less are
7 disregarded and that procedure is not consistently used to obtain
8 a greater yield than would otherwise be permitted.
9 (f) With respect to a consumer credit sale made pursuant to a
10 revolving charge account, the parties to the sale may contract for
11 the payment by the buyer of a credit service charge not exceeding
12 that permitted in this section, subject to the following:
13 (1) The credit service charge contracted for and received may
14 not exceed a charge in each monthly billing cycle which is two
15 and eighty-three thousandths percent (2.083%) of an amount
16 not greater than:
17 (A) the average daily balance of the account;
18 (B) the unpaid balance of the account on the same day of
19 the billing cycle; or
20 (C) subject to subsection (g), the median amount within a
21 specified range within which the average daily balance of
22 the account or the unpaid balance of the account, on the
23 same day of the billing cycle, is included.
24 For purposes of clauses (B) and (C), a variation of not more
25 than four (4) days from month to month is "the same day of
26 the billing cycle".
27 (2) If the billing cycle is not monthly, the maximum charge is
28 that percentage which bears the same relation to the
29 applicable monthly maximum percentage as the number of
30 days in the billing cycle bears to thirty (30).
31 (3) Notwithstanding subdivision (1), if there is an unpaid
32 balance on the date as of which the credit service charge is
33 applied, the seller may contract for and receive a charge not
34 exceeding fifty cents ($0.50) if the billing cycle is monthly or
35 longer, or the pro rata part of fifty cents ($0.50) which bears
36 the same relation to fifty cents ($0.50) as the number of days
37 in the billing cycle bears to thirty (30) if the billing cycle is
38 shorter than monthly. However, a seller may not contract for
39 or receive a charge under this subdivision if the seller has
40 made an annual charge for the same period as permitted by

42 chapter.

7 the credit service charge; and
8 (5) appraisal fees.
9 (d) An additional charge may be made for a GAP agreement,
10 subject to the following:
11 (1) A GAP agreement or GAP coverage may not be required
12 by the seller, and that fact must be disclosed in writing to the
13 consumer.
14 (2) The charge for the initial term of coverage under the GAP
15 agreement must be disclosed in writing to the consumer. The
16 charge may be disclosed on a unit-cost basis only in the case
17 of the following transactions:
18 (A) Revolving charge accounts.
19 (B) Closed-end credit transactions, if the request for
20 coverage is made by mail or telephone.
21 (C) Closed-end credit transactions, if the GAP agreement
22 limits the total amount of indebtedness eligible for
23 coverage.
24 (3) If the term of coverage under the GAP agreement is less
25 than the term of the consumer credit sale, the term of
26 coverage under the GAP agreement must be disclosed in
27 writing to the consumer.
28 (4) The consumer must sign or initial an affirmative written
29 request for coverage after receiving all required disclosures.
30 (5) The GAP agreement must include the following:
31 (A) In the case of GAP coverage for a new motor vehicle,
32 the manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) for the
33 motor vehicle.
34 (B) In the case of GAP coverage for a used motor vehicle,
35 the average retail value for the motor vehicle, as
36 determined by use of a third party valuation service
37 provider that is customarily relied upon in the used motor
38 vehicle commercial marketplace.
39 (C) The name of the financing entity taking assignment of
40 the agreement.
41 (D) The name and address of the consumer.
42 (E) The name of the creditor selling the agreement.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
165
1 (F) Information advising the consumer that the consumer
2 may be able to obtain similar coverage from the
3 consumer's primary insurance carrier.
4 (G) A coverage provision that includes a minimum
5 deductible of five hundred dollars ($500).
6 (H) A provision providing for a minimum thirty (30) day
7 free-look period.
8 (I) In the case of a consumer credit sale involving a motor
9 vehicle, a provision excluding the sale of GAP coverage if
10 the amount financed under the consumer credit sale (not
11 including the cost of the GAP agreement, the cost of any
12 credit insurance, and the cost of any warranties or service
13 agreements) is less than eighty percent (80%) of the
14 manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP), in the case
15 of a new motor vehicle, or the average retail value (as
16 determined by use of a third party valuation service
17 provider that is customarily relied upon in the used motor
18 vehicle commercial marketplace), in the case of a used
19 motor vehicle.
20 (J) In the case of a GAP agreement in which the charge for
21 the agreement exceeds four hundred dollars ($400),
22 specific instructions that may be used by the consumer to
23 cancel the agreement and obtain a refund of the unearned
24 GAP charge before prepayment in full, in accordance with
25 the procedures, and subject to the conditions, set forth in
26 subdivision (6).
27 (6) If the charge for the GAP agreement exceeds four hundred
28 dollars ($400), the consumer is entitled to cancel the
29 agreement and obtain a refund of the unearned GAP charge
30 before prepayment in full. Refunds of unearned GAP charges
31 shall be made subject to the following conditions:
32 (A) A refund of the charge for a GAP agreement must be
33 calculated using a method that is no less favorable to the
34 consumer than a refund calculated on a pro rata basis.
35 (B) The consumer is entitled to a refund of the unearned
36 GAP agreement charge as outlined in the GAP agreement.
37 (C) The seller of the GAP agreement is responsible for
38 making a timely refund to the consumer of unearned GAP
39 agreement charges under the terms and conditions of the
40 GAP agreement.
41 (7) Upon prepayment in full of the consumer credit sale:
42 (A) the GAP coverage is automatically terminated; and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
166
1 (B) the seller of the GAP agreement must issue a refund in
2 accordance with subdivision (6).
3 (8) A creditor that sells GAP agreements must:
4 (A) insure its GAP agreement obligations under a
5 contractual liability insurance policy issued by an insurer
6 authorized to engage in the insurance business in Indiana;
7 and
8 (B) retain appropriate records, as required under this
9 article, regarding GAP agreements sold, refunded, and
10 expired.
11 Sec. 6. (a) With respect to a consumer credit sale, refinancing,
12 or consolidation, the parties may contract for a delinquency charge
13 of not more than the following:
14 (1) Five dollars ($5) on any installment or minimum payment
15 due that is not paid in full not later than ten (10) days after its
16 scheduled due date, in the case of a consumer credit sale,
17 refinancing, or consolidation that is made before July 1, 2019.
18 The amount of five dollars ($5) in this subdivision is subject
19 to change under IC 37-2-1-5. In addition, the parties may
20 provide by contract for a delinquency charge that is subject
21 to change. If the parties provide by contract for a delinquency
22 charge that is subject to change, the seller shall disclose in the
23 contract that the amount of the delinquency charge is subject
24 to change under IC 37-2-1-5 or this section.
25 (2) In the case of a consumer credit sale, refinancing, or
26 consolidation that is made after June 30, 2019, the following:
27 (A) Five dollars ($5) on any installment or minimum
28 payment due that is not paid in full not later than ten (10)
29 days after its scheduled due date, if installments under the
30 consumer credit sale, refinancing, or consolidation are due
31 every fourteen (14) days or less. The amount of five dollars
32 ($5) in this clause is not subject to change under
33 IC 37-2-1-5.
34 (B) Twenty-five dollars ($25) on any installment or
35 minimum payment due that is not paid in full not later
36 than ten (10) days after its scheduled due date, if
37 installments under the consumer credit sale, refinancing,
38 or consolidation are due every fifteen (15) days or more.
39 The amount of twenty-five dollars ($25) in this clause is not
40 subject to change under IC 37-2-1-5.
41 (C) Twenty-five dollars ($25) on any installment or
42 minimum payment due that is not paid in full not later
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
167
1 than ten (10) days after its scheduled due date, in the case
2 of a consumer credit sale, refinancing, or consolidation
3 that is payable in a single installment that is due at least
4 thirty (30) days after the consumer credit sale, refinancing,
5 or consolidation is made. The amount of twenty-five
6 dollars ($25) in this clause is not subject to change under
7 IC 37-2-1-5.
8 (b) A delinquency charge under this section may be collected
9 only once on an installment however long it remains in default. A
10 delinquency charge on consumer credit sales made under a
11 revolving charge account may be applied each month that the
12 payment is less than the minimum required payment. A
13 delinquency charge may be collected any time after it accrues. No
14 delinquency charge may be collected if:
15 (1) the installment has been deferred and a deferral charge
16 under section 7 of this chapter has been paid or incurred;
17 (2) a charge for a skip-a-payment service has been paid or
18 incurred, as provided in section 5 of this chapter; or
19 (3) a charge for an optional expedited payment service has
20 been paid or incurred, as provided in section 5 of this chapter.
21 (c) A creditor may not, directly or indirectly, charge or collect
22 a delinquency charge on a payment that:
23 (1) is paid not later than ten (10) days after its scheduled due
24 date; and
25 (2) is otherwise a full payment of the payment due for the
26 applicable installment period;
27 if the only delinquency with respect to the consumer credit sale,
28 refinancing, or consolidation is attributable to a delinquency
29 charge assessed on an earlier installment.
30 (d) If two (2) or more installments, or parts of two (2) or more
31 installments, of a precomputed consumer credit sale are in default
32 for ten (10) days or more, the creditor may elect to convert the
33 consumer credit sale from a precomputed consumer credit sale to
34 a consumer credit sale in which the credit service charge is based
35 on unpaid balances. A creditor that makes this election shall make
36 a rebate under the provisions on rebates upon prepayment under
37 section 12 of this chapter as of the maturity date of the first
38 delinquent installment, and thereafter may make a credit service
39 charge as authorized by the provisions on credit service charges for
40 consumer credit sales under section 4 of this chapter. The amount
41 of the rebate shall not be reduced by the amount of any permitted
42 minimum charge under section 12 of this chapter. Any deferral
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
168
1 charges made on installments due at or after the maturity date of
2 the first delinquent installment shall be rebated, and no further
3 deferral charges shall be made.
4 Sec. 7. (a) This section applies only to a consumer credit sale,
5 refinancing, or consolidation, that is entered into before July 1,
6 2020. With respect to a precomputed consumer credit sale,
7 refinancing, or consolidation, the parties before or after default
8 may agree in writing to a deferral of all or part of one (1) or more
9 unpaid installments, and the seller may make and collect a charge
10 not exceeding the lesser of thirty-six percent (36%) per year or the
11 rate previously stated to the buyer pursuant to section 13 of this
12 chapter applied to the amount or amounts deferred for the period
13 of deferral calculated without regard to differences in lengths of
14 months, but proportionately for a part of a month, counting each
15 day as one-thirtieth (1/30) of a month. A deferral charge may be
16 collected at the time it is assessed or at any time thereafter.
17 (b) The seller, in addition to the deferral charge, may make
18 appropriate additional charges under section 5 of this chapter, and
19 the amount of these charges which is not paid in cash may be added
20 to the amount deferred for the purpose of calculating the deferral
21 charge.
22 (c) The parties may agree in writing at the time of a
23 precomputed consumer credit sale, refinancing, or consolidation
24 that if an installment is not paid within ten (10) days after its due
25 date, the seller may unilaterally grant a deferral and make charges
26 as provided in this section. No deferral charge may be made for a
27 period after the date that the seller elects to accelerate the maturity
28 of the agreement.
29 (d) A delinquency charge made by the seller on an installment
30 may not be retained if a deferral charge is made pursuant to this
31 section with respect to the period of delinquency.
32 Sec. 8. With respect to a consumer credit sale, refinancing, or
33 consolidation, the seller may by agreement with the buyer
34 refinance the unpaid balance and may contract for and receive a
35 credit service charge based on the amount financed resulting from
36 the refinancing at a rate not exceeding that permitted by the
37 provisions on credit service charge for consumer credit sales under
38 section 4 of this chapter. For the purpose of determining the credit
39 service charge permitted, the amount financed resulting from the
40 refinancing comprises the following:
41 (1) If:
42 (A) the transaction was not precomputed, the total of the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
169
1 unpaid balance and accrued charges on the date of
2 refinancing; or
3 (B) the transaction was precomputed, in the case of a
4 transaction entered into before July 1, 2020, the amount
5 which the buyer would have been required to pay upon
6 prepayment pursuant to the provisions on rebate upon
7 prepayment (section 12 of this chapter) on the date of
8 refinancing.
9 (2) Appropriate additional charges under section 5 of this
10 chapter, payment of which is deferred.
11 Sec. 9. If a buyer owes an unpaid balance to a seller with respect
12 to a consumer credit sale, refinancing, or consolidation, and
13 becomes obligated on another consumer credit sale, refinancing, or
14 consolidation with the same seller, the parties may agree to a
15 consolidation resulting in a single schedule of payments pursuant
16 to either of the following:
17 (1) The parties may agree to refinance the unpaid balance
18 with respect to the previous sale pursuant to the provisions on
19 refinancing (section 8 of this chapter) and to consolidate the
20 amount financed resulting from the refinancing by adding it
21 to the amount financed with respect to the subsequent sale.
22 The seller may contract for and receive a credit service
23 charge based on the aggregate amount financed resulting
24 from the consolidation at a rate not exceeding that permitted
25 by the provisions on credit service charge for consumer credit
26 sales (section 4 of this chapter).
27 (2) The parties may agree to consolidate by adding together
28 the unpaid balances with respect to the two sales.
29 Sec. 10. (a) If the agreement with respect to a consumer credit

9 Sec. 11. (a) Subject to the provisions on rebate upon prepayment

23 (c) The creditor or mortgage servicer shall provide, in writing,

42 (A) one hundred dollars ($100); or

1 (B) the credit service charge that accrues on the sale from

7 business days (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and
8 Sundays) after the creditor or mortgage servicer receives the
9 debtor's second written request, and the creditor or mortgage

11 A liability under this subsection is an excess charge under
12 IC 37-2-6-9.
13 (d) As used in this subsection, "mortgage transaction" means a

26 Sec. 12. (a) Except for subsections (b) and (i), this section applies
27 only to a sale agreement entered into before July 1, 2020. Except as
28 provided in subsection (b), upon prepayment in full of the unpaid
29 balance of a precomputed consumer credit sale, refinancing, or
30 consolidation, an amount not less than the unearned portion of the
31 credit service charge calculated according to this section shall be
32 rebated to the buyer. If the rebate required is less than one dollar
33 ($1), no rebate need be made.
34 (b) Upon prepayment in full of a consumer credit sale,
35 refinancing, or consolidation, other than one pursuant to a
36 revolving charge account, if the credit service charge then earned
37 is less than any permitted minimum credit service charge under
38 section 4(h) of this chapter contracted for, whether or not the sale,
39 refinancing, or consolidation is precomputed, the seller may collect
40 or retain the minimum charge, as if earned, not exceeding the
41 credit service charge contracted for.
42 (c) The unearned portion of the credit service charge is a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
173
1 fraction of the credit service charge of which the numerator is the
2 sum of the periodic balances scheduled to follow the computational
3 period in which prepayment occurs, and the denominator is the
4 sum of all periodic balances under either the sale agreement or, if
5 the balance owing resulted from a refinancing (section 8 of this
6 chapter) or a consolidation (section 9 of this chapter), under the
7 refinancing agreement or consolidation agreement.
8 (d) In this section:
9 (1) "periodic balance" means the amount scheduled to be
10 outstanding on the last day of a computational period before
11 deducting the payment, if any, scheduled to be made on that
12 day;
13 (2) "computational period" means one (1) month if one-half
14 (1/2) or more of the intervals between scheduled payments
15 under the agreement is one (1) month or more, and otherwise
16 means one (1) week;
17 (3) the "interval" to the due date of the first scheduled
18 installment or the final scheduled payment date is measured
19 from the date of a sale, refinancing, or consolidation, or any
20 later date prescribed for calculating maximum credit service
21 charges under section 4(e) of this chapter and includes either
22 the first or last day of the interval; and
23 (4) if the interval to the due date of the first scheduled
24 installment does not exceed one (1) month by more than
25 fifteen (15) days when the computational period is one (1)
26 month, or eleven (11) days when the computational period is
27 one (1) week, the interval shall be considered as one (1)
28 computational period.
29 (e) This subsection applies only if the schedule of payments is
30 not regular.
31 (1) If the computational period is one (1) month and:
32 (A) if the number of days in the interval to the due date of
33 the first scheduled installment is less than one (1) month by
34 more than five (5) days, or more than one (1) month by
35 more than five (5) but not more than fifteen (15) days, the
36 unearned credit service charge shall be increased by an
37 adjustment for each day by which the interval is less than
38 one (1) month and, at the option of the seller, may be
39 reduced by an adjustment for each day by which the
40 interval is more than one (1) month; the adjustment for
41 each day shall be one-thirtieth (1/30) of that part of the
42 credit service charge earned in the computational period
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
174
1 prior to the due date of the first scheduled installment
2 assuming that period to be one (1) month; and
3 (B) if the interval to the final scheduled payment date is a
4 number of computational periods plus an additional
5 number of days less than a full month, the additional
6 number of days shall be considered a computational period
7 only if sixteen (16) days or more. This clause applies
8 whether or not clause (A) applies.
9 (2) Notwithstanding subdivision (1), if the computational
10 period is one (1) month, the number of days in the interval to
11 the due date of the first installment exceeds one (1) month by
12 not more than fifteen (15) days, and the schedule of payments
13 is otherwise regular, the seller, at the seller's option, may
14 exclude the extra days and the charge for the extra days in
15 computing the unearned credit service charge; but if the seller
16 does so and a rebate is required before the due date of the
17 first scheduled installment, the seller shall compute the earned
18 charge for each elapsed day as one-thirtieth (1/30) of the
19 amount the earned charge would have been if the first
20 interval had been one (1) month.
21 (3) If the computational period is one (1) week and:
22 (A) if the number of days in the interval to the due date of
23 this first scheduled installment is less than five (5) days or
24 more than nine (9) days but not more than eleven (11)
25 days, the unearned credit service charge shall be increased
26 by an adjustment for each day by which the interval is less
27 than seven (7) days and, at the option of the seller, may be
28 reduced by an adjustment for each day by which the
29 interval is more than seven (7) days; the adjustment for
30 each day shall be one-seventh (1/7) of that part of the
31 credit service charge earned in the computational period
32 prior to the due date of the first scheduled installment
33 assuming that period to be one (1) week; and
34 (B) if the interval to the final scheduled payment date is a
35 number of computational periods plus an additional
36 number of days less than a full week, the additional
37 number of days shall be considered a computational period
38 only if five (5) days or more. This clause applies whether or
39 not clause (A) applies.
40 (f) If a deferral under section 7 of this chapter has been agreed
41 to, the unearned portion of the credit service charge shall be
42 computed without regard to the deferral. The amount of deferral
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
175
1 charge earned at the date of prepayment shall also be calculated.
2 If the deferral charge earned is less than the deferral charge paid,
3 the difference shall be added to the unearned portion of the credit
4 service charge. If any part of a deferral charge has been earned but
5 has not been paid, that part shall be subtracted from the unearned
6 portion of the credit service charge or shall be added to the unpaid
7 balance.
8 (g) This section does not preclude the collection or retention by
9 the seller of delinquency charges under section 6 of this chapter.
10 (h) If the maturity is accelerated for any reason and judgment
11 is obtained, the buyer is entitled to the same rebate as if payment
12 had been made on the date judgment is entered.
13 (i) Upon prepayment in full of a consumer credit sale by the
14 proceeds of consumer credit insurance, the buyer or the buyer's
15 estate shall pay the same credit service charge or receive the same
16 rebate as though the buyer had prepaid the agreement on the date
17 the proceeds of the insurance are paid to the seller, but no later
18 than ten (10) business days after satisfactory proof of loss is
19 furnished to the seller. This subsection applies whether or not the
20 credit sale is precomputed.
21 (j) Upon prepayment in full of a transaction with a term of more
22 than sixty-one (61) months, the unearned part of the credit service
23 charge shall be computed by applying the disclosed annual
24 percentage rate that would yield the credit service charge
25 originally contracted for to the unpaid balances of the amount
26 financed for the full computational periods following the
27 prepayment, as originally scheduled or as deferred.
28 Sec. 13. (a) For purposes of this section, "consumer credit sale"
29 includes a sale that is a sale of an interest in land and that is a first
30 lien mortgage transaction if the sale is otherwise a consumer credit
31 sale.
32 (b) The seller shall disclose to the buyer to whom credit is
33 extended with respect to a consumer credit sale, and the lessor
34 shall disclose to the lessee with respect to a consumer lease, the
35 information required by the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15
36 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
37 (c) For purposes of subsection (b), disclosures shall not be
38 required on a consumer credit sale if the transaction is exempt
39 from the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
40 Sec. 14. A seller may not use multiple agreements with intent to
41 obtain a higher credit service charge than would otherwise be
42 permitted by this article or to avoid disclosure of an annual
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
176
1 percentage rate pursuant to section 13 of this chapter. The excess
2 amount of credit service charge provided for in agreements in
3 violation of this section is an excess charge for the purposes of the
4 provisions on the effect of violations on rights of parties (IC
5 37-2-6-9) and the provisions on civil actions by the department (IC
6 37-2-7-14).
7 Sec. 15. (a) For the purposes of this section, "terms of the
8 refinancing" means:
9 (1) in the case of a fixed-rate consumer credit sale, the
10 individual payment amounts, the charges as a result of default
11 by the buyer, and the rate of the credit service charge; and
12 (2) in the case of a variable rate consumer credit sale, the
13 method used to determine the individual payment amounts,
14 the charges as a result of default by the buyer, the method
15 used to determine the rate of the credit service charge, the
16 circumstances under which the rate of the credit service
17 charge may increase, and any limitations on the increase in
18 the rate of the credit service charge.
19 (b) With respect to a consumer credit sale, other than one
20 pursuant to a revolving charge account or one on which only credit
21 service charges are payable before the time that the final scheduled
22 payment is due, if any scheduled payment is more than twice as
23 large as the average of earlier scheduled payments, the buyer has
24 the right to refinance the amount of that payment at the time it is
25 due without penalty. The terms of the refinancing shall be no less
26 favorable to the buyer than the terms of the original sale. This
27 section does not apply to the extent that the payment schedule is
28 adjusted to the seasonal or irregular income of the buyer.
29 Sec. 16. The obligation of a lessee upon expiration of a consumer
30 lease may not exceed three (3) times the average payment allocable
31 to a monthly period under the lease. This limitation does not apply
32 to charges for damages to the leased property or for other default.
33 Sec. 17. (a) With respect to a consumer credit sale, a seller may
34 take a security interest in the property sold. In addition, a seller
35 may take a security interest in goods upon which services are
36 performed or in which goods sold are installed or to which they are
37 annexed, or in land to which the goods are affixed or which is
38 maintained, repaired, or improved as a result of the sale of the
39 goods or services, if, in the case of a subordinate lien mortgage
40 transaction, the debt secured is four thousand dollars ($4,000) or
41 more, or, in the case of a security interest in goods the debt secured
42 is three hundred dollars ($300) or more. Except as provided with
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
177
1 respect to cross-collateral in section 19 of this chapter, a seller may
2 not otherwise take a security interest in property of the buyer to
3 secure the debt arising from a consumer credit sale.
4 (b) With respect to a consumer lease, a lessor may not take a
5 security interest in property of the lessee to secure the debt arising
6 from the lease.
7 (c) A security interest taken in violation of this section is void.
8 (d) The amounts of four thousand dollars ($4,000) and three
9 hundred dollars ($300) in subsection (a) are subject to change
10 pursuant to the provisions on adjustment of dollar amounts in
11 IC 37-2-1-5. However, notwithstanding IC 37-2-1-5(a), the
12 Reference Base Index to be used with respect to the amount of:
13 (1) three hundred dollars ($300) is the Index for October
14 1992; and
15 (2) four thousand dollars ($4,000) is the Index for October
16 2012.
17 Sec. 18. The leasing of live domestic animals (as defined in
18 IC 34-30-30-1) under this chapter is prohibited.
19 Sec. 19. (a) As used in this section, "seller" does not include an
20 assignee not related to the original seller.
21 (b) In addition to contracting for a security interest pursuant to
22 the provisions on security in sales or leases in section 17 of this
23 chapter, a seller in a consumer credit sale may secure the debt
24 arising from the sale by contracting for a security interest in other
25 property if as a result of a prior sale the seller has an existing
26 security interest in the other property. The seller may also contract
27 for a security interest in the property sold in the subsequent sale as
28 security for the previous debt.
29 (c) If the seller contracts for a security interest in other property
30 pursuant to this section, the rate of credit service charge thereafter
31 on the aggregate unpaid balances so secured may not exceed that
32 permitted if the balances so secured were consolidated pursuant to
33 the provisions on consolidation involving a refinancing in section
34 9(a) of this chapter. The seller has a reasonable time after so
35 contracting to make any adjustments required by this section.
36 Sec. 20. (a) If debts arising from two (2) or more consumer
37 credit sales, other than sales pursuant to a revolving charge
38 account, are secured by cross-collateral under section 19 of this
39 chapter or consolidated into one (1) debt payable on a single
40 schedule of payments, and the debt is secured by security interests
41 taken with respect to one (1) or more of the sales, payments
42 received by the seller after the taking of the cross-collateral or the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
178
1 consolidation are deemed, for the purpose of determining the
2 amount of the debt secured by the various security interests, to
3 have been first applied to the payment of the debts arising from the
4 sales first made. To the extent debts are paid according to this
5 section, security interests in items of property terminate as the debt
6 originally incurred with respect to each item is paid.
7 (b) Payments received by the seller upon a revolving charge
8 account are deemed, for the purpose of determining the amount of
9 the debt secured by the various security interests, to have been
10 applied first to the payment of credit service charges in the order
11 of their entry to the account and then to the payment of debts in
12 the order in which the entries to the account showing the debts
13 were made.
14 (c) If the debts consolidated arose from two (2) or more sales
15 made on the same day, payments received by the seller are deemed,
16 for the purpose of determining the amount of the debts secured by
17 the various security interests, to have been applied first to the
18 payment of the smallest debt.
19 Sec. 21. A seller or lessor may not take an assignment of
20 earnings of the buyer or lessee for payment or as security for
21 payment of a claim, whether arising out of a consumer credit sale,
22 consumer lease, or otherwise. An assignment of earnings in
23 violation of this section is unenforceable by the assignees of the
24 earnings and revocable by the buyer or lessee. This section does not
25 prohibit an employee from authorizing deductions from the
26 employee's earnings if the authorization is revocable and is
27 otherwise permitted by law.
28 Sec. 22. With respect to a consumer credit sale or consumer
29 lease the seller or lessor may not give or offer to give a rebate or
30 discount or otherwise pay or offer to pay value to the buyer or
31 lessee as an inducement for a sale or lease in consideration of the
32 buyer or lessee giving to the seller or lessor the names of
33 prospective purchasers or lessees, or otherwise aiding the seller or
34 lessor in making a sale or lease to another person, if the earning of
35 the rebate, discount or other value is contingent upon the
36 occurrence of an event subsequent to the time the buyer or lessee
37 agrees to buy or lease. If a buyer or lessee is induced by a violation
38 of this section to enter into a consumer credit sale or consumer
39 lease, the agreement is unenforceable by the seller or lessor and the
40 buyer or lessee, at the buyer's or lessee's option, may rescind the
41 agreement or retain the goods delivered and the benefit of any
42 services performed, without any obligation to pay for them.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
179
1 Sec. 23. The buyer or lessee is authorized to pay the original
2 seller or lessor until the buyer or lessee receives notification of
3 assignment of the rights to payment pursuant to a consumer credit
4 sale or consumer lease and that payment is to be made to the
5 assignee. A notification which does not reasonably identify the
6 rights assigned is ineffective. If requested by the buyer or lessee,
7 the assignee must seasonably furnish reasonable proof that the
8 assignment has been made and unless the assignee does so the
9 buyer or lessee may pay the seller or lessor.
10 Sec. 24. With respect to a consumer credit sale or consumer
11 lease the agreement may provide for the payment by the buyer or
12 lessee of reasonable attorney's fees and after default and referral
13 to an attorney not a salaried employee of the seller, or of the lessor
14 or the lessor's assignee. A provision in violation of this section is
15 unenforceable.
16 Sec. 25. Except for reasonable expenses incurred in realizing on
17 a security interest, the agreement with respect to a consumer credit
18 sale may not provide for any charges as a result of default by the
19 buyer other than those authorized by this article. A provision in
20 violation of this section is unenforceable.
21 Sec. 26. A buyer or lessee may not authorize any person to
22 confess judgment on a claim arising out of a consumer credit sale
23 or consumer lease. An authorization in violation of this section is
24 void.
25 Sec. 27. (a) This section applies to consumer credit sales,
26 including revolving charge accounts.
27 (b) Except as provided in subsection (c), a creditor shall credit
28 a payment to a consumer's account as of the date of receipt, except
29 when a delay in crediting does not result in a finance charge or
30 other charge, including a delinquency charge under section 6 of
31 this chapter. A delay in posting does not violate this section so long
32 as the payment is credited as of the date of receipt.
33 (c) If a creditor specifies in writing requirements for the
34 consumer to follow in making payments, but accepts a payment
35 that does not conform to the requirements, the creditor shall credit
36 the payment within five (5) days of receipt of the payment.
37 (d) If a creditor fails to credit a payment as required by this
38 section in time to avoid the imposition of a finance or other charge,
39 including a delinquency charge, the creditor shall adjust the
40 consumer's account so that the charges imposed are credited to the
41 consumer's account during the next payment period.
42 Sec. 28. The requirements of 16 CFR 429 must be met in regard
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
180
1 to the following provisions concerning a home solicitation sale:
2 (1) Period within which cancellation may be made by the
3 buyer.
4 (2) Notice of cancellation.
5 (3) Form of cancellation.
6 (4) Form of agreement or offer to purchase.
7 (5) Statement of buyer's rights.
8 (6) Restoration of down payment.
9 (7) Retention of cancellation fee.
10 (8) Duty of buyer.
11 (9) Any other relevant requirements in 16 CFR 429.
12 Sec. 29. The parties to a sale other than a consumer credit sale
13 may agree in a writing signed by the parties that the sale is subject
14 to the provisions of this article applying to consumer credit sales.
15 If the parties so agree, the sale is a consumer credit sale for the
16 purposes of this article.
17 Sec. 30. (a) With respect to a consumer related sale not made
18 pursuant to a revolving charge account, the parties may contract
19 for an amount comprising the amount financed and a credit service
20 charge not in excess of twenty-five percent (25%) per year
21 calculated according to the actuarial method on the unpaid
22 balances of the amount financed.
23 (b) With respect to a consumer related sale made pursuant to a
24 revolving charge account, the parties may contract for a credit
25 service charge not in excess of that permitted by the provisions on
26 credit service charge for revolving charge accounts set forth in
27 section 4(f) of this chapter.
28 (c) A person engaged in consumer related sales is not required
29 to comply with IC 37-2-7-24 through IC 37-2-7-26.
30 Sec. 31. Except for the rate of the credit service charge set forth
31 in section 4 of this chapter and the rights to prepay and to rebate
32 upon prepayment, sections 4 through 12 of this chapter apply to a
33 consumer related sale.
34 Sec. 32. (a) An agreement with respect to a consumer related
35 sale may provide for only the following charges as a result of the
36 buyer's default:
37 (1) Reasonable attorney's fees and reasonable expenses
38 incurred in realizing on a security interest.
39 (2) Deferral charges not in excess of twenty-five percent
40 (25%) per year of the amount deferred for the period of
41 deferral.
42 (3) Other charges that could have been made had the sale
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
181
1 been a consumer credit sale.
2 (b) A provision in violation of this section is unenforceable.
3 Sec. 33. With respect to a sale other than a consumer credit sale
4 or a consumer related sale, the parties may contract for the
5 payment by the buyer of any credit service charge.
6 Chapter 4. Loans
7 Sec. 0.1. The following amendments to IC 24-4.5-3 (before its
8 repeal) applied as follows:
9 (1) The amendments made to IC 24-4.5-3-201 (before its
10 repeal) by P.L.163-1999 did not apply to consumer loans in
11 existence before July 1, 1999.
12 (2) The amendments made to IC 24-4.5-3-209(1) (before its
13 repeal) by P.L.159-2001 applied to a contract between a
14 lender and a debtor that is entered into or renewed after June
15 30, 2001.
16 Sec. 1. (a) This chapter applies to consumer loans, including
17 supervised loans.
18 (b) Sections 15 through 21 of this chapter apply to consumer
19 loans. Except as otherwise provided, all provisions of this article
20 applying to consumer loans apply to supervised loans.
21 (c) Sections 41 through 44 of this chapter apply to consumer
22 related loans.
23 (d) Except for the rate of the loan finance charge and the rights
24 to prepay and to rebate upon prepayment, sections 4 through 13 of
25 this chapter apply to a consumer related loan.
26 (e) The licensing provisions of this chapter apply to consumer
27 credit sales under IC 37-2-3 that are subordinate lien mortgage
28 transactions.
29 Sec. 2. Unless a loan is made subject to this chapter by
30 agreement under section 41 of this chapter, and except with respect
31 to:
32 (1) disclosure (section 14 of this chapter);
33 (2) debtors' remedies (IC 37-2-6-8);
34 (3) providing payoff amounts (section 12 of this chapter);
35 (4) providing property tax information (section 46 of this
36 chapter);
37 (5) powers and functions of the department (IC 37-2-7-3);
38 (6) the department's examination and investigatory authority
39 (IC 37-2-7-5); and
40 (7) the department's administrative enforcement authority
41 (IC 37-2-7-9);
42 the term "consumer loan" does not include a first lien mortgage

182
1 transaction.
2 Sec. 3. A loan, refinancing, or consolidation is considered
3 precomputed if the debt is expressed as a sum comprising the
4 principal and the amount of the loan finance charge computed in
5 advance.
6 Sec. 4. (a) This section does not apply to a supervised loan.
7 Except as provided in subsections (f) and (i), with respect to a
8 consumer loan, a lender may contract for a loan finance charge,
9 calculated according to the actuarial method, not exceeding
10 twenty-five percent (25%) per year on the unpaid balances of the
11 principal.
12 (b) In the case of a loan agreement entered into before July 1,
13 2020, this section does not limit or restrict the manner of
14 contracting for the loan finance charge, whether by way of add-on,
15 discount, or otherwise, so long as the rate of the loan finance
16 charge does not exceed that permitted by this section. If the loan is
17 precomputed:
18 (1) the loan finance charge may be calculated on the
19 assumption that all scheduled payments will be made when
20 due; and
21 (2) the effect of prepayment is governed by the provisions on
22 rebate upon prepayment in section 13 of this chapter.
23 (c) The following apply to a loan agreement for a consumer loan
24 (or for the refinancing or consolidation of a consumer loan) that is
25 entered into after June 30, 2020:
26 (1) The consumer loan is subject to this section, including the
27 limitations set forth in:
28 (A) subsection (a) with respect to the loan finance charge;
29 and
30 (B) subsection (i)(2) with respect to the amount of the
31 authorized nonrefundable prepaid finance charge, in the
32 case of a consumer loan that is not secured by an interest
33 in land.
34 (2) The loan finance charge authorized by this section must
35 be:
36 (A) contracted for between the lender and the debtor; and
37 (B) calculated by applying a rate not exceeding the rate set
38 forth in subsection (a) to unpaid balances of the principal.
39 (3) A loan agreement for a precomputed consumer loan is
40 prohibited.
41 (4) Subject to subsection (l), in addition to the loan finance
42 charge authorized by subsection (a) and to any other fees
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
183
1 permitted by this chapter, and not subject to the twenty-five
2 percent (25%) rate set forth in subsection (a), the lender may
3 contract for and receive as a condition for, or an incident to,
4 the extension of credit a nonrefundable prepaid finance
5 charge under subsection (i), whether the charge is:
6 (A) paid separately in cash or by check before or at
7 consummation; or
8 (B) withheld from the proceeds of the consumer loan.
9 (d) For the purposes of this section, the term of a loan
10 commences with the date the loan is made. Differences in the
11 lengths of months are disregarded, and a day may be counted as
12 one-thirtieth (1/30) of a month. Subject to classifications and
13 differentiations the lender may reasonably establish, a part of a
14 month in excess of fifteen (15) days may be treated as a full month
15 if periods of fifteen (15) days or less are disregarded and if that
16 procedure is not consistently used to obtain a greater yield than
17 would otherwise be permitted. For purposes of computing average
18 daily balances, the creditor may elect to treat all months as
19 consisting of thirty (30) days.
20 (e) With respect to a consumer loan made pursuant to a
21 revolving loan account:
22 (1) the loan finance charge shall be deemed not to exceed the
23 maximum annual percentage rate if the loan finance charge
24 contracted for and received does not exceed a charge in each
25 monthly billing cycle which is two and eighty-three
26 thousandths percent (2.083%) of an amount not greater than:
27 (A) the average daily balance of the debt;
28 (B) the unpaid balance of the debt on the same day of the
29 billing cycle; or
30 (C) subject to subsection (f), the median amount within a
31 specified range within which the average daily balance or
32 the unpaid balance of the debt, on the same day of the
33 billing cycle, is included; for the purposes of this clause and
34 clause (B), a variation of not more than four (4) days from
35 month to month is "the same day of the billing cycle";
36 (2) if the billing cycle is not monthly, the loan finance charge
37 shall be deemed not to exceed the maximum annual
38 percentage rate if the loan finance charge contracted for and
39 received does not exceed a percentage which bears the same
40 relation to one-twelfth (1/12) the maximum annual percentage
41 rate as the number of days in the billing cycle bears to thirty
42 (30); and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
184
1 (3) notwithstanding subsection (a), if there is an unpaid
2 balance on the date as of which the loan finance charge is
3 applied, the lender may contract for and receive a charge not
4 exceeding fifty cents ($0.50) if the billing cycle is monthly or
5 longer, or the pro rata part of fifty cents ($0.50) which bears
6 the same relation to fifty cents ($0.50) as the number of days
7 in the billing cycle bears to thirty (30) if the billing cycle is
8 shorter than monthly, but no charge may be made pursuant
9 to this subdivision if the lender has made an annual charge for
10 the same period as permitted by the provisions on additional
11 charges in section 5(a)(3) of this chapter.
12 (f) Subject to classifications and differentiations the lender may
13 reasonably establish, the lender may make the same loan finance
14 charge on all amounts financed within a specified range. A loan
15 finance charge does not violate subsection (a) if:
16 (1) when applied to the median amount within each range, it
17 does not exceed the maximum permitted by subsection (a);
18 and
19 (2) when applied to the lowest amount within each range, it
20 does not produce a rate of loan finance charge exceeding the
21 rate calculated according to subdivision (1) by more than
22 eight percent (8%) of the rate calculated according to
23 subdivision (1).
24 (g) With respect to a consumer loan not made pursuant to a
25 revolving loan account, the lender may contract for and receive a
26 minimum loan finance charge of not more than thirty dollars ($30).
27 The minimum loan finance charge allowed under this subsection
28 may be imposed only if the lender does not contract for or receive
29 a nonrefundable prepaid finance charge under subsection (i) and:
30 (1) the debtor prepays in full a consumer loan, refinancing, or

32 consolidation is precomputed;
33 (2) the loan, refinancing, or consolidation prepaid by the
34 debtor is subject to a loan finance charge that:
35 (A) is contracted for by the parties; and
36 (B) does not exceed the rate prescribed in subsection (a);
37 and
38 (3) the loan finance charge earned at the time of prepayment
39 is less than the minimum loan finance charge contracted for
40 under this subsection.

1 this chapter, a lender may contract for and receive the following

1 from licensing under section 24(a) of this chapter, a charge

3 (A) A debt cancellation agreement or debt cancellation

23 agreement, the entire charge may be excluded from the
24 loan finance charge and imposed as an additional charge
25 under this section if at least one (1) of the events is the loss
26 of life, health, or income.
27 The additional charges provided for in subdivisions (7) through
28 (10) are not subject to refund or rebate.
29 (b) An additional charge may be made for insurance in
30 connection with the loan, other than insurance protecting the
31 lender against the debtor's default or other credit loss:
32 (1) with respect to insurance against loss of or damage to
33 property or against liability, if the lender furnishes a clear
34 and specific statement in writing to the debtor, setting forth
35 the cost of the insurance if obtained from or through the
36 lender and stating that the debtor may choose the person,
37 subject to the lender's reasonable approval, through whom
38 the insurance is to be obtained; and
39 (2) with respect to consumer credit insurance providing life,
40 accident, unemployment or other loss of income, or health
41 coverage, if the insurance coverage is not a factor in the
42 approval by the lender of the extension of credit and this fact
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
190
1 is clearly disclosed in writing to the debtor, and if, in order to
2 obtain the insurance in connection with the extension of
3 credit, the debtor gives specific affirmative written indication
4 of the desire to do so after written disclosure of the cost of the
5 insurance.
6 (c) An additional charge may be made for a GAP agreement,
7 subject to the following:
8 (1) A GAP agreement or GAP coverage may not be required
9 by the lender, and that fact must be disclosed in writing to the
10 consumer.
11 (2) The charge for the initial term of coverage under the GAP
12 agreement must be disclosed in writing to the consumer. The
13 charge may be disclosed on a unit-cost basis only in the case
14 of the following transactions:
15 (A) Revolving loan accounts.
16 (B) Closed-end credit transactions, if the request for
17 coverage is made by mail or telephone.
18 (C) Closed-end credit transactions, if the GAP agreement
19 limits the total amount of indebtedness eligible for
20 coverage.
21 (3) If the term of coverage under the GAP agreement is less
22 than the term of the consumer loan, the term of coverage
23 under the GAP agreement must be disclosed in writing to the
24 consumer.
25 (4) The consumer must sign or initial an affirmative written
26 request for coverage after receiving all required disclosures.
27 (5) The GAP agreement must include the following:
28 (A) In the case of GAP coverage for a new motor vehicle,
29 the manufacturer's suggested retail price (MSRP) for the
30 motor vehicle.
31 (B) In the case of GAP coverage for a used motor vehicle,
32 the average retail value for the motor vehicle, as
33 determined by use of a third party valuation service
34 provider that is customarily relied upon in the used motor
35 vehicle commercial marketplace.
36 (C) The name of the financing entity taking assignment of
37 the agreement, as applicable.
38 (D) The name and address of the consumer.
39 (E) The name of the lender selling the agreement.
40 (F) Information advising the consumer that the consumer
41 may be able to obtain similar coverage from the
42 consumer's primary insurance carrier.

191
1 (G) A coverage provision that includes a minimum
2 deductible of five hundred dollars ($500).
3 (H) A provision providing for a minimum thirty (30) day
4 trial period.
5 (I) In the case of a consumer loan made with respect to a
6 motor vehicle, a provision excluding the sale of GAP

35 assignee, is responsible for making a timely refund to the

37 terms and conditions of the GAP agreement.
38 (7) Upon prepayment in full of the consumer loan:
39 (A) the GAP coverage is automatically terminated; and
40 (B) the seller of the GAP agreement must issue a refund in
41 accordance with subdivision (6).
42 (8) A lender that sells GAP agreements must:

8 Sec. 6. (a) With respect to a consumer loan, refinancing, or
9 consolidation, the parties may contract for a delinquency charge of
10 not more than the following:
11 (1) Five dollars ($5) on any installment or minimum payment
12 due that is not paid in full not later than ten (10) days after its
13 scheduled due date, in the case of a consumer loan,
14 refinancing, or consolidation that is made before July 1, 2019.
15 The amount of five dollars ($5) in this subdivision is subject
16 to change under IC 37-2-1-5. In addition, the parties may
17 provide by contract for a delinquency charge that is subject
18 to change. If the parties provide by contract for a delinquency
19 charge that is subject to change, the lender shall disclose in
20 the contract that the amount of the delinquency charge is
21 subject to change under IC 37-2-1-5 or this section.
22 (2) In the case of a consumer loan, refinancing, or
23 consolidation that is made after June 30, 2019, the following:
24 (A) Five dollars ($5) on any installment or minimum
25 payment due that is not paid in full not later than ten (10)
26 days after its scheduled due date, if installments under the
27 consumer loan, refinancing, or consolidation are due every
28 fourteen (14) days or less. The amount of five dollars ($5)
29 in this clause is not subject to change under IC 37-2-1-5.
30 (B) Twenty-five dollars ($25) on any installment or
31 minimum payment due that is not paid in full not later
32 than ten (10) days after its scheduled due date, if
33 installments under the consumer loan, refinancing, or
34 consolidation are due every fifteen (15) days or more. The
35 amount of twenty-five dollars ($25) in this clause is not
36 subject to change under IC 37-2-1-5.
37 (C) Twenty-five dollars ($25) on any installment or
38 minimum payment due that is not paid in full not later
39 than ten (10) days after its scheduled due date, in the case
40 of a consumer loan, refinancing, or consolidation that is
41 payable in a single installment that is due at least thirty
42 (30) days after the consumer loan, refinancing, or
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
193
1 consolidation is made. The amount of twenty-five dollars
2 ($25) in this clause is not subject to change under
3 IC 37-2-1-5.
4 (b) A delinquency charge under this section may be collected
5 only once on an installment however long it remains in default.
6 With regard to a delinquency charge on consumer loans made
7 under a revolving loan account, the delinquency charge may be
8 applied each month that the payment is less than the minimum
9 required payment on the account. A delinquency charge may be
10 collected any time after it accrues. A delinquency charge may not
11 be collected if:
12 (1) the installment has been deferred and a deferral charge

14 (2) a charge for a skip-a-payment service under section 5(a)(9)

16 section 5(a)(9)(C) of this chapter; or
17 (3) a charge for an optional expedited payment service under
18 section 5(a)(10) of this chapter has been paid or incurred, as
19 provided in section 5(a)(10)(E) of this chapter.
20 (c) A creditor may not, directly or indirectly, charge or collect
21 a delinquency charge on a payment that:
22 (1) is paid not later than ten (10) days after its scheduled due
23 date; and
24 (2) is otherwise a full payment of the payment due for the
25 applicable installment period;
26 if the only delinquency with respect to the consumer loan,
27 refinancing, or consolidation is attributable to a delinquency
28 charge assessed on an earlier installment.
29 (d) If two (2) or more installments, or parts of two (2) or more
30 installments, of a precomputed loan are in default for ten (10) days
31 or more, the lender may elect to convert the loan from a
32 precomputed loan to a loan in which the finance charge is based on
33 unpaid balances. A lender that makes this election shall make a
34 rebate under the provisions on rebates upon prepayment set forth
35 in section 13 of this chapter as of the maturity date of the first
36 delinquent installment, and thereafter may make a loan finance
37 charge as authorized by the provisions on loan finance charges for
38 consumer loans (section 4 of this chapter) or supervised loans
39 (section 35 of this chapter). The amount of the rebate shall not be

42 installments due at or after the maturity date of the first delinquent

1 installment shall be rebated, and no further deferral charges shall

3 Sec. 7. (a) This section applies only to a consumer loan,
4 refinancing, or consolidation, that is entered into before July 1,
5 2020. With respect to a precomputed consumer loan, refinancing,
6 or consolidation, the parties before or after default may agree in
7 writing to a deferral of all or part of one (1) or more unpaid
8 installments, and the lender may make and collect a charge not
9 exceeding the lesser of thirty-six percent (36%) per year or the rate
10 previously stated to the debtor pursuant to section 14 of this
11 chapter applied to the amount or amounts deferred for the period
12 of deferral calculated without regard to difference in the lengths of
13 months, but proportionally for a part of a month, counting each
14 day as one-thirtieth (1/30) of a month. A deferral charge may be
15 collected at the time it is assessed or at any time thereafter.
16 (b) The lender, in addition to the deferral charge, may make
17 appropriate additional charges under section 5 of this chapter, and
18 the amount of these charges which is not paid in cash may be added
19 to the amount deferred for the purpose of calculating the deferral
20 charge.
21 (c) The parties may agree in writing at the time of a
22 precomputed consumer loan, refinancing, or consolidation that if
23 an installment is not paid within ten (10) days after its due date, the
24 lender may unilaterally grant a deferral and make charges as
25 provided in this section. No deferral charge may be made for a
26 period after the date that the lender elects to accelerate the
27 maturity of the agreement.
28 (d) A delinquency charge made by the lender on an installment
29 may not be retained if a deferral charge is made pursuant to this
30 section with respect to the period of delinquency.

11 Sec. 9. (a) If a debtor owes an unpaid balance to a lender with

20 consumer loan, refinancing, or consolidation was precomputed, in

24 to consolidate the principal resulting from the refinancing by

31 supervised loans (section 35 of this chapter), whichever is
32 appropriate.

20 Sec. 11. (a) If the agreement with respect to a consumer loan,
21 refinancing, or consolidation contains covenants by the debtor to

25 add the amounts paid to the debt. Within a reasonable time after
26 advancing any sums, the lender shall state to the debtor in writing
27 the amount of the sums advanced, any charges with respect to this
28 amount, and any revised payment schedule and, if the duties of the
29 debtor performed by the lender pertain to insurance, a brief
30 description of the insurance paid for by the lender including the
31 type and amount of coverages. No further information need be
32 given.

1 Sec. 12. (a) Subject to the provisions on rebate upon prepayment

12 creditor;

39 Sec. 13. (a) Except for subsections (b) and (i), this section applies
40 only to a loan agreement entered into before July 1, 2020. Except
41 as provided in subsection (b), upon prepayment in full of the
42 unpaid balance of a precomputed consumer loan, refinancing, or
