200
1 consolidation, an amount not less than the unearned portion of the
2 loan finance charge calculated according to this section shall be
3 rebated to the debtor. If the rebate otherwise required is less than
4 one dollar ($1), no rebate need be made.
5 (b) Upon prepayment in full of a consumer loan, refinancing, or
6 consolidation, other than one under a revolving loan account, if the
7 loan finance charge earned is less than any permitted minimum
8 loan finance charge under section 4(g) or 35(g) of this chapter
9 contracted for, whether or not the consumer loan, refinancing, or
10 consolidation is precomputed, the lender may collect or retain the
11 minimum loan finance charge, as if earned, not exceeding the loan
12 finance charge contracted for.
13 (c) The unearned portion of the loan finance charge is a fraction
14 of the loan finance charge of which the numerator is the sum of the
15 periodic balances scheduled to follow the computational period in
16 which prepayment occurs, and the denominator is the sum of all
17 periodic balances under either the loan agreement or, if the
18 balance owing resulted from a refinancing under section 8 of this
19 chapter or a consolidation under section 9 of this chapter, under
20 the refinancing agreement or consolidation agreement.
21 (d) In this section:
22 (1) "periodic balance" means the amount scheduled to be
23 outstanding on the last day of a computational period before
24 deducting the payment, if any, scheduled to be made on that
25 day;
26 (2) "computation period" means one (1) month if one-half
27 (1/2) or more of the intervals between scheduled payments
28 under the agreement is one (1) month or more, and otherwise
29 means one (1) week;
30 (3) the "interval" to the due date of the first scheduled
31 installment or the final scheduled payment date is measured
32 from the date of a loan, refinancing, or consolidation, and
33 includes either the first or last day of the interval; and
34 (4) if the interval to the due date of the first scheduled
35 installment does not exceed one (1) month by more than
36 fifteen (15) days when the computational period is one (1)
37 month, or eleven (11) days when the computational period is
38 one (1) week, the interval shall be considered as one (1)
39 computational period.
40 (e) This subsection applies only if the schedule of payments is
41 not regular.
42 (1) If the computational period is one (1) month and:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
201
1 (A) if the number of days in the interval to the due date of
2 the first scheduled installment is less than one (1) month by
3 more than five (5) days, or more than one (1) month by
4 more than five (5) but not more than fifteen (15) days, the
5 unearned loan finance charge shall be increased by an
6 adjustment for each day by which the interval is less than
7 one (1) month and, at the option of the lender, may be
8 reduced by an adjustment for each day by which the
9 interval is more than one (1) month; the adjustment for
10 each day shall be one-thirtieth (1/30) of that part of the
11 loan finance charge earned in the computational period
12 prior to the due date of the first scheduled installment
13 assuming that period to be one (1) month; and
14 (B) if the interval to the final scheduled payment date is a
15 number of computational periods plus an additional
16 number of days less than a full month, the additional
17 number of days shall be considered a computational period
18 only if sixteen (16) days or more. This clause applies
19 whether or not clause (A) applies.
20 (2) Notwithstanding subdivision (1), if the computational
21 period is one (1) month, the number of days in the interval to
22 the due date of the first installment exceeds one (1) month by
23 not more than fifteen (15) days, and the schedule of payments
24 is otherwise regular, the lender, at the lender's option, may
25 exclude the extra days and the charge for the extra days in
26 computing the unearned loan finance charge; but if the lender
27 does so and a rebate is required before the due date of the
28 first scheduled installment, the lender shall compute the
29 earned charge for each elapsed day as one-thirtieth (1/30) of
30 the amount the earned charge would have been if the first
31 interval had been one (1) month.
32 (3) If the computational period is one (1) week and:
33 (A) if the number of days in the interval to the due date of
34 the first scheduled installment is less than five (5) days, or
35 more than nine (9) days, but not more than eleven (11)
36 days, the unearned loan finance charge shall be increased
37 by an adjustment for each day by which the interval is less
38 than seven (7) days and, at the option of the lender, may be
39 reduced by an adjustment for each day by which the
40 interval is more than seven (7) days; the adjustment for
41 each day shall be one-seventh (1/7) of that part of the loan
42 finance charge earned in the computational period prior to
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
202
1 the due date of the first scheduled installment, assuming
2 that period to be one (1) week; and
3 (B) if the interval to the final scheduled payment date is a
4 number of computational periods plus an additional
5 number of days less than a full week, the additional
6 number of days shall be considered a computational period
7 only if five (5) days or more. This clause applies whether or
8 not clause (A) applies.
9 (f) If a deferral has been agreed to under section 7 of this
10 chapter, the unearned portion of the loan finance charge shall be
11 computed without regard to the deferral. The amount of deferral
12 charge earned at the date of prepayment shall also be calculated.
13 If the deferral charge earned is less than the deferral charge paid,
14 the difference shall be added to the unearned portion of the loan
15 finance charge. If any part of a deferral charge has been earned
16 but has not been paid, that part shall be subtracted from the
17 unearned portion of the loan finance charge or shall be added to
18 the unpaid balance.
19 (g) This section does not preclude the collection or retention by
20 the lender of delinquency charges under section 6 of this chapter.
21 (h) If the maturity is accelerated for any reason and judgment
22 is obtained, the debtor is entitled to the same rebate as if payment
23 had been made on the date judgment is entered.
24 (i) Upon prepayment in full of a consumer loan by the proceeds
25 of consumer credit insurance, the debtor or the debtor's estate
26 shall pay the same loan finance charge or receive the same rebate
27 as though the debtor had prepaid the agreement on the date the
28 proceeds of the insurance are paid to the lender, but no later than
29 ten (10) business days after satisfactory proof of loss is furnished
30 to the lender. This subsection applies whether or not the loan is
31 precomputed.
32 (j) Upon prepayment in full of a transaction with a term of more
33 than sixty-one (61) months, the unearned loan finance charge shall
34 be computed by applying the disclosed annual percentage rate that
35 would yield the loan finance charge originally contracted for to the
36 unpaid balances of the amount financed for the full computational
37 periods following the prepayment, as originally scheduled or as
38 deferred.
39 Sec. 14. (a) For the purposes of this section, "consumer loan"
40 includes a loan that is a first lien mortgage transaction if the loan
41 is otherwise a consumer loan.
42 (b) The lender shall disclose to the debtor to whom credit is
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
203
1 extended with respect to a consumer loan the information required
2 by the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
3 (c) For purposes of subsection (b), disclosures shall not be
4 required on a consumer loan if the transaction is exempt from the
5 Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
6 Sec. 15. (a) This section does not apply to a first lien mortgage
7 transaction.
8 (b) Except as provided in IC 37-5-4-3 with respect to a high cost
9 home loan (as defined in IC 37-5-2-8), with respect to a consumer
10 loan, other than one pursuant to a revolving loan account or one on
11 which only loan finance charges are payable prior to the time that
12 the final scheduled payment is due, if any scheduled payment is
13 more than twice as large as the average of earlier scheduled
14 payments, the debtor has the right to refinance the amount of that
15 payment at the time it is due without penalty. The terms of the
16 refinancing shall be no less favorable to the debtor than the terms
17 of the original loan. This section does not apply to the extent that
18 the payment schedule is adjusted to the seasonal or irregular
19 income of the debtor.
20 (c) For the purposes of this section, "terms of the refinancing"
21 means:
22 (1) in the case of a fixed-rate consumer loan, the individual
23 payment amounts, the charges as a result of default by the
24 debtor, and the rate of the loan finance charge; and
25 (2) in the case of a variable rate consumer loan, the method
26 used to determine the individual payment amounts, the
27 charges as a result of default by the debtor, the method used
28 to determine the rate of the loan finance charge, the
29 circumstances under which the rate of the loan finance charge
30 may increase, and any limitations on the increase in the rate
31 of the loan finance charge.
32 (d) If a consumer loan is made under the authority of the
33 Alternative Mortgage Transaction Parity Act (12 U.S.C. 3802 et
34 seq.), the note evidencing the mortgage must contain a reference to
35 the applicable federal law.
36 Sec. 16. (a) A lender may not take an assignment of earnings of
37 the debtor for payment or as security for payment of a debt arising
38 out of a consumer loan or otherwise. An assignment of earnings in
39 violation of this section is unenforceable by the assignee of the
40 earnings and revocable by the debtor. This section does not
41 prohibit an employee from authorizing deductions from his
42 earnings if the authorization is revocable and is otherwise
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
204
1 permitted by law.
2 (b) A sale of unpaid earnings made in consideration of the
3 payment of money to or for the account of the seller of the earnings
4 is deemed to be a loan to the seller secured by an assignment of
5 earnings.
6 Sec. 17. With respect to a consumer loan the agreement may
7 provide for the payment by the debtor of reasonable attorney's fees
8 after default and referral to an attorney not a salaried employee of
9 the lender. A provision in violation of this section is unenforceable.
10 Sec. 18. Except for reasonable expenses incurred in realizing on
11 a security interest, the agreement with respect to a consumer loan
12 may not provide for charges as a result of default by the debtor
13 other than those authorized by this article. A provision in violation
14 of this section is unenforceable.
15 Sec. 19. The debtor is authorized to pay the original lender until
16 the debtor receives notification of assignment of rights to payment
17 pursuant to a consumer loan and that payment is to be made to the
18 assignee. A notification which does not reasonably identify the
19 rights assigned is ineffective. If requested by the debtor, the
20 assignee must seasonably furnish reasonable proof that the
21 assignment has been made and unless the assignee does so the
22 debtor may pay the original lender.
23 Sec. 20. A debtor may not authorize any person to confess
24 judgment on a claim arising out of a consumer loan. An
25 authorization in violation of this section is void.
26 Sec. 21. (a) This section applies to consumer loans, including
27 revolving loan accounts.
28 (b) Except as provided in subsection (c), a creditor shall credit
29 a payment to a consumer's account as of the date of receipt, except
30 when a delay in crediting does not result in a finance charge or
31 other charge, including a delinquency charge under section 6 of
32 this chapter. A delay in posting does not violate this section so long
33 as the payment is credited as of the date of receipt.
34 (c) If a creditor specifies in writing requirements for the
35 consumer to follow in making payments, but accepts a payment
36 that does not conform to the requirements, the creditor shall credit
37 the payment within five (5) days of receipt of the payment.
38 (d) If a creditor fails to credit a payment as required by this
39 section in time to avoid the imposition of a finance or other charge,
40 including a delinquency charge, the creditor shall adjust the
41 consumer's account so that the charges imposed are credited to the
42 consumer's account during the next payment period.

18 the two (2) year period set forth in subsection (b)(4) during which
19 a surety bond required by this section must remain in effect after
20 the occurrence of an event described in subsection (b)(4)(A) or
21 (b)(4)(B), as applicable.
22 (d) The penal sum of the surety bond shall be maintained in an
23 amount that reflects the dollar amount of mortgage transactions
24 originated as determined by the director. If the principal amount
25 of a surety bond required under this section is reduced by payment
26 of a claim or judgment, the creditor or exempt person for whom
27 the bond is issued shall immediately notify the director of the
28 reduction and, not later than thirty (30) days after notice by the
29 director, file a new or an additional surety bond in an amount set
30 by the director. The amount of the new or additional bond set by
31 the director must be at least the amount of the bond before
32 payment of the claim or judgment.
33 (e) If for any reason a surety terminates a bond issued under
34 this section, the creditor or the exempt person shall immediately
35 notify the department and file a new surety bond in an amount
36 determined by the director.
37 (f) Cancellation of a surety bond issued under this section does
38 not affect any liability incurred or accrued during the period when
39 the surety bond was in effect.
40 (g) The director may obtain satisfaction from a surety bond
41 issued under this section if the director incurs expenses, issues a
42 final order, or recovers a final judgment under this chapter.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
216
1 (h) Notices required under this section must be made in writing
2 and submitted through the NMLSR or any other electronic
3 registration system that may be approved by the director.
4 Sec. 30. (a) Subject to subsection (g), the director shall designate

11 Sec. 31. (a) A license issued under this article must be renewed
12 not later than December 31 of each calendar year. A license issued
13 under section 24 of this chapter must be renewed through the
14 NMLSR. The minimum standards for license renewal for a
15 creditor include the following:
16 (1) If the creditor is licensed in accordance with section 23 of
17 this chapter, the creditor has:
18 (A) paid all required fees for renewal of the license; and
19 (B) filed all reports and information required by the
20 director.
21 (2) If the creditor is licensed under section 24 of this chapter,
22 the following:
23 (A) The creditor has continued to meet the surety bond
24 requirement under section 29 of this chapter.
25 (B) The creditor has filed the creditor's call report in a
26 manner that satisfies section 33(d) of this chapter.
27 (C) The creditor has paid all required fees for renewal of
28 the license.
29 (D) The creditor and individuals described in section 26(b)
30 of this chapter have certified to the department that they
31 continue to meet all the standards for licensing established
32 under section 26 of this chapter.
33 (E) The creditor has filed all reports and information
34 required by the director.
35 (F) The creditor has provided in the creditor's renewal
36 application any information describing material changes
37 in the information contained in the creditor's original
38 application for licensure, or in any previous application,
39 including any previous renewal application, along with any
40 other information the director requires in order to
41 evaluate the renewal of the license issued under this article.
42 (b) A license issued by the department authorizing a person to
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
220
1 engage as a creditor in consumer loans or consumer credit sales
2 under this article may be revoked or suspended by the department
3 if the person fails to:
4 (1) file any renewal form required by the department; or
5 (2) pay any license renewal fee described under section
6 26(h)(3) of this chapter;
7 not later than sixty (60) days after the due date.
8 (c) A person whose license is revoked or suspended under this
9 section may do either of the following:
10 (1) Pay all delinquent fees and apply for reinstatement of the
11 license.
12 (2) Appeal the revocation or suspension to the department for
13 an administrative review under IC 4-21.5-3.
14 Pending the decision from a hearing under IC 4-21.5-3 concerning
15 license revocation or suspension, a license remains in force.

23 Sec. 32. (a) The department may issue to a person licensed to:

30 (1) include:

32 with the department, which date may not be less than ten
33 (10) days from the date of the order;
34 (B) a description of the action contemplated by the
35 department; and
36 (C) a statement of the facts or conduct supporting the

38 (2) be accompanied by a notice stating that the licensee is
39 entitled to:
40 (A) a reasonable opportunity to be heard; and
41 (B) show the licensee's compliance with all lawful
42 requirements for retention of the license;

221
1 at the meeting described in subdivision (1)(A).
2 (c) After the meeting described in subsection (b)(1)(A), the
3 department may revoke or suspend the license if the department
4 finds that:
5 (1) the licensee has repeatedly and willfully violated:
6 (A) this article or any applicable rule, order, or guidance
7 document adopted or issued by the department; or
8 (B) any other state or federal laws, rules, or regulations
9 applicable to consumer credit transactions;
10 (2) the licensee does not meet the licensing qualifications
11 under section 26 of this chapter;
12 (3) the licensee obtained the license for the benefit of, or on
13 behalf of, a person who does not qualify for the license;
14 (4) the licensee knowingly or intentionally made material

16 from, the department; or
17 (5) facts or conditions exist that, had they existed at the time
18 the licensee applied for the license, would have been grounds
19 for the department to deny the issuance of the license.

27 under IC 4-21.5-3-6; and
28 (4) any other terms and conditions that apply to the
29 revocation or suspension.

32 findings supporting the order.

37 of this article.

42 the revocation, suspension, or relinquishment does not impair or

7 Sec. 33. (a) Every creditor required to be licensed under this
8 article shall maintain records in conformity with United States
9 generally accepted accounting principles and practices, or in any
10 other form that may be preapproved at the discretion of the
11 director, in a manner that will enable the department to determine
12 whether the licensee is complying with the provisions of this article.
13 The record keeping system of a licensee shall be sufficient if the
14 licensee makes the required information reasonably available. The
15 department shall determine the sufficiency of the records and
16 whether the licensee has made the required information reasonably
17 available. The department shall be given free access to the records
18 wherever located. The records pertaining to any loan shall be
19 retained for two (2) years after making the final entry relating to
20 the loan, but in the case of a revolving loan account the two (2)
21 years is measured from the date of each entry. A person licensed
22 or required to be licensed under this chapter is subject to
23 IC 28-1-2-30.5 with respect to any records maintained by the
24 person. A person that is exempt (either under this article or under
25 IC 37-1-1-6(8)) from licensing and that sponsors one (1) or more
26 licensed mortgage loan originators as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8)
27 or by 750 IAC 9, shall:
28 (1) cooperate with the department; and
29 (2) provide access to records and documents;
30 as required by the department in carrying out examinations of the
31 activities of the licensed mortgage loan originators sponsored by
32 the person.
33 (b) The unique identifier of any person originating a mortgage
34 transaction must be clearly shown on all mortgage transaction
35 application forms and any other documents as required by the
36 director.
37 (c) Every licensee that engages in mortgage transactions shall
38 use automated examination and regulatory software designated by
39 the director, including third party software. Use of the software
40 consistent with guidance documents and policies issued by the
41 director is not a violation of IC 28-1-2-30.
42 (d) Each:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
223
1 (1) creditor that is licensed by the department to engage in
2 mortgage transactions; and
3 (2) person that is exempt (either under this article or under
4 IC 37-1-1-6(8)) from licensing and that:
5 (A) employs one (1) or more licensed mortgage loan
6 originators; or
7 (B) sponsors one (1) or more licensed mortgage loan
8 originators as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750
9 IAC 9;
10 shall submit to the NMLSR a call report, which must be in the
11 form and contain information the NMLSR requires.
12 (e) Every creditor required to be licensed under this article shall
13 file with the department a composite report as required by the
14 department, but not more frequently than annually, in the form
15 prescribed by the department relating to all consumer loans made
16 by the licensee. The department shall consult with comparable
17 officials in other states for the purpose of making the kinds of
18 information required in the reports uniform among the states.
19 Information contained in the reports shall be confidential and may
20 be published only in composite form. The department may impose
21 a fee in an amount fixed by the department under IC 28-11-3-5 for
22 each day that a creditor fails to file the report required by this
23 subsection.
24 (f) A creditor required to be licensed under this article shall file
25 notification with the department if the licensee:
26 (1) has a change in name, address, or principals;
27 (2) opens a new branch, closes an existing branch, or relocates
28 an existing branch;
29 (3) files for bankruptcy or reorganization; or
30 (4) is subject to revocation or suspension proceedings by a
31 state or governmental authority with regard to the licensee's
32 activities;
33 not later than thirty (30) days after the date of the event described
34 in this subsection.
35 (g) Every licensee shall file notification with the department if
36 the licensee or any director, executive officer, or manager of the
37 licensee has been convicted of a felony under the laws of Indiana or
38 any other jurisdiction. The licensee shall file the notification
39 required by this subsection not later than thirty (30) days after the
40 date of the event described in this subsection.
41 Sec. 34. (a) As used in this section, "automated loan machine"
42 means an unmanned machine that performs routine lending
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92
224
1 functions.
