29 regulated under this article, also conduct activities of a loan broker
30 subject to IC 23-2.5.
31 Sec. 9. A licensee may carry on other business at a location
32 where the licensee engages in first lien mortgage transactions
33 unless the licensee carries on other business for the purpose of
34 evasion or violation of this article.
35 Chapter 2. Definitions
36 Sec. 1. The definitions set forth in this chapter apply throughout
37 this article.
38 Sec. 2. "Affiliate", with respect to any person subject to this
39 article, means a person that, directly or indirectly, through one (1)
40 or more intermediaries:
41 (1) controls;
42 (2) is controlled by; or
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

94
1 (3) is under common control with;
2 the person subject to this article.
3 Sec. 3. "Agreement" means the bargain of the parties in fact as
4 found in the parties' language or by implication from other
5 circumstances, including course of dealing or usage of trade or
6 course of performance.
7 Sec. 4. "Agricultural products" includes agricultural products,
8 horticultural products, viticultural products, dairy products,
9 livestock, wildlife, poultry, bees, forest products, fish and shellfish,
10 any products raised or produced on farms, and any products
11 processed or manufactured from products raised or produced on
12 farms.
13 Sec. 5. "Agricultural purpose" means a purpose related to the
14 production, harvest, exhibition, marketing, transportation,
15 processing, or manufacture of agricultural products by a natural
16 person who cultivates, plants, propagates, or nurtures the
17 agricultural products.
18 Sec. 6. "Balloon payment", with respect to a mortgage
19 transaction, means any payment:
20 (1) that the creditor requires the debtor to make at any time
21 during the term of the mortgage;
22 (2) that represents the entire amount of the outstanding
23 balance with respect to the mortgage; and
24 (3) the entire amount of which is due as of a specified date or
25 at the end of a specified period;
26 if the aggregate amount of the minimum periodic payments
27 required under the mortgage would not fully amortize the
28 outstanding balance by the specified date or at the end of the
29 specified period. The term does not include a payment required by
30 a creditor under a due-on-sale clause (as defined in 12 U.S.C.
31 1701j-3(a)) or a payment required by a creditor under a provision
32 in the mortgage that permits the creditor to accelerate the debt
33 upon the debtor's default or failure to abide by the material terms
34 of the mortgage.
35 Sec. 7. "Bona fide nonprofit organization" means an
36 organization that does the following, as determined by the director,
37 under criteria established by the director:
38 (1) Maintains tax exempt status under Section 501(c)(3) of the
39 Internal Revenue Code.
40 (2) Promotes affordable housing or provides home ownership
41 education or similar services.
42 (3) Conducts the organization's activities in a manner that
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

95
1 serves public or charitable purposes.
2 (4) Receives funding and revenue and charges fees in a
3 manner that does not encourage the organization or the
4 organization's employees to act other than in the best interests
5 of the organization's clients.
6 (5) Compensates the organization's employees in a manner
7 that does not encourage employees to act other than in the
8 best interests of the organization's clients.
9 (6) Provides to, or identifies for, debtors mortgage
10 transactions with terms that are favorable to the debtor (as
11 described in IC 37-1-1-6(16)) and comparable to mortgage
12 transactions and housing assistance provided under
13 government housing assistance programs.
14 (7) Maintains certification by the United States Department
15 of Housing and Urban Development or employs counselors
16 who are certified by the Indiana housing and community
17 development authority.
18 Sec. 8. "Civil court" means any court in Indiana having
19 jurisdiction of civil cases.
20 Sec. 9. "Consumer credit sale" is a sale of goods, services, or an
21 interest in land in which:
22 (1) credit is granted by a person who engages as a seller in
23 credit transactions of the same kind;
24 (2) the buyer is a person other than an organization;
25 (3) the goods, services, or interest in land are purchased
26 primarily for a personal, family, or household purpose;
27 (4) either the debt is payable in installments or a credit service
28 charge is made; and
29 (5) with respect to a sale of goods or services, either:
30 (A) the amount of credit extended, the written credit limit,
31 or the initial advance does not exceed the exempt threshold
32 amount, as adjusted in accordance with the annual
33 adjustment of the exempt threshold amount, specified in
34 Regulation Z (12 CFR 226.3 or 12 CFR 1026.3(b), as
35 applicable); or
36 (B) the debt is secured by personal property used or
37 expected to be used as the principal dwelling of the buyer.
38 Sec. 10. "Credit" means the right granted by a creditor to a
39 debtor to defer payment of debt or to incur debt and defer its
40 payment.
41 Sec. 11. "Creditor" means a person:
42 (1) that regularly engages in the extension of first lien
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

96
1 mortgage transactions that are subject to a credit service
2 charge or loan finance charge, as applicable, or are payable
3 by written agreement in more than four (4) installments (not
4 including a down payment); and
5 (2) to which the obligation is initially payable, either on the
6 face of the note or contract, or by agreement if there is not a
7 note or contract.
8 The term does not include a person described in section 39 of this
9 chapter in a tablefunded transaction. A creditor may be an
10 individual, a limited liability company, a sole proprietorship, a
11 partnership, a trust, a joint venture, a corporation, an
12 unincorporated organization, or other form of entity, however
13 organized.
14 Sec. 12. "Department" refers to the department of financial
15 institutions.
16 Sec. 13. "Depository institution" has the meaning set forth in
17 the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (12 U.S.C. 1813(c)) and includes
18 any credit union.
19 Sec. 14. "Director" refers to the director of the department of
20 financial institutions or the director's designee.
21 Sec. 15. "Dwelling" means a residential structure that contains
22 one (1) to four (4) units, regardless of whether the structure is
23 attached to real property. The term includes an individual:
24 (1) condominium unit;
25 (2) cooperative unit;
26 (3) mobile home; or
27 (4) trailer;
28 that is used as a residence.
29 Sec. 16. "Employee" means an individual who is paid wages or
30 other compensation by an employer required under federal income
31 tax law to file Form W-2 on behalf of the individual.
32 Sec. 17. "Federal banking agencies" means the Board of
33 Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the
34 Comptroller of the Currency, the Office of Thrift Supervision, the
35 National Credit Union Administration, and the Federal Deposit
36 Insurance Corporation.
37 Sec. 18. "First lien mortgage transaction" means:
38 (1) a consumer loan; or
39 (2) a consumer credit sale;
40 that is or will be used by the debtor primarily for personal, family,
41 or household purposes and that is secured by a mortgage or a land
42 contract (or another consensual security interest equivalent to a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

97
1 mortgage or a land contract) that constitutes a first lien on a
2 dwelling or on residential real estate upon which a dwelling is
3 constructed or intended to be constructed.
4 Sec. 19. "Immediate family member" means a spouse, child,
5 sibling, parent, grandparent, or grandchild. The term includes
6 stepparents, stepchildren, stepsiblings, and adoptive relationships.
7 Sec. 20. "Individual" means a natural person.
8 Sec. 21. "Land contract" means a contract for the sale of real
9 estate in which the seller of the real estate retains legal title to the
10 real estate until the total contract price is paid by the buyer.
11 Sec. 22. "Licensee" means a person licensed to engage in
12 mortgage transactions as a creditor.
13 Sec. 23. "Loan" includes:
14 (1) the creation of debt by:
15 (A) the creditor's payment of or agreement to pay money
16 to the debtor or to a third party for the account of the
17 debtor; or
18 (B) the extension of credit by a person who engages as a
19 seller in credit transactions primarily secured by an
20 interest in land;
21 (2) the creation of debt by a credit to an account with the
22 creditor upon which the debtor is entitled to draw
23 immediately; and
24 (3) the forbearance of debt arising from a loan.
25 Sec. 24. "Loan brokerage business" means any activity in which
26 a person, in return for any consideration from any source,
27 procures, attempts to procure, or assists in procuring, a mortgage
28 transaction from a third party or any other person, whether or not
29 the person seeking the mortgage transaction actually obtains the
30 mortgage transaction.
31 Sec. 25. "Loan processor or underwriter" means an individual
32 who performs clerical or support duties as an employee at the
33 direction of, and subject to the supervision and instruction of, a
34 person licensed to engage in mortgage transactions or a person
35 exempt from licensing. For purposes of this section, the term
36 "clerical or support duties" may include, after the receipt of an
37 application, the following:
38 (1) The receipt, collection, distribution, and analysis of
39 information common for the processing or underwriting of a
40 mortgage transaction.
41 (2) The communication with a consumer to obtain the
42 information necessary for the processing or underwriting of
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

98
1 a loan, to the extent that the communication does not include:
2 (A) offering or negotiating loan rates or terms; or
3 (B) counseling consumers about mortgage transaction
4 rates or terms.
5 The term does not include an individual who is an employee of a
6 person that is not engaged in mortgage transactions as a creditor
7 if that person is permitted to voluntarily register with the
8 department to sponsor the individual under IC 37-1-1-6(8) to
9 engage solely in the activities described in this subdivision.
10 Sec. 26. "Mortgage loan originator" means an individual who,
11 for compensation or gain, or in the expectation of compensation or
12 gain, regularly engages in taking a mortgage transaction
13 application or in offering or negotiating the terms of a mortgage
14 transaction that either is made under this article or under IC 37-2
15 is made by an employee of a person licensed to engage in mortgage
16 transactions or by an employee of a person that is exempt from
17 licensing, while the employee is engaging in the loan brokerage
18 business. The term does not include the following:
19 (1) An individual engaged solely as a loan processor or
20 underwriter as long as the individual works exclusively as an
21 employee of a person licensed to engage in mortgage
22 transactions or as an employee of a person exempt from
23 licensing. However, the term includes an individual who is
24 licensed as a mortgage loan originator under this article and
25 750 IAC 9-3 and who is an employee of a person that is not
26 engaged in mortgage transactions as a creditor if that person
27 voluntarily registers with the department to sponsor the
28 individual under IC 37-1-1-6(8) to engage solely as a third
29 party processor or underwriter.
30 (2) Unless the person or entity is compensated by:
31 (A) a creditor;
32 (B) a loan broker;
33 (C) another mortgage loan originator; or
34 (D) any agent of a creditor, a loan broker, or another
35 mortgage loan originator described in clauses (A) through
36 (C);
37 a person or entity that performs only real estate brokerage
38 activities and is licensed or registered in accordance with
39 applicable state law.
40 (3) A person solely involved in extensions of credit relating to
41 timeshare plans (as defined in 11 U.S.C. 101(53D)).
42 Sec. 27. "Mortgage servicer" means the last person to whom a
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

99
1 mortgagor or the mortgagor's successor in interest has been
2 instructed by a mortgagee to send payments on a loan secured by
3 a mortgage.
4 Sec. 28. "Mortgage transaction" means:
5 (1) a consumer loan; or
6 (2) a consumer credit sale;
7 that is or will be used by the debtor primarily for personal, family,
8 or household purposes and that is secured by a mortgage or a land
9 contract (or another consensual security interest equivalent to a
10 mortgage or a land contract) on a dwelling or on residential real
11 estate upon which a dwelling is constructed or intended to be
12 constructed.
13 Sec. 29. "Nationwide Multistate Licensing System and Registry"
14 (or "Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry" or
15 "NMLSR") means a multistate licensing system owned and
16 operated by the State Regulatory Registry, LLC, or by any
17 successor or affiliated entity, for the licensing and registration of
18 creditors, mortgage loan originators, and other persons in the
19 mortgage or financial services industries. The term includes any
20 other name or acronym that may be assigned to the system by the
21 State Regulatory Registry, LLC, or by any successor or affiliated
22 entity.
23 Sec. 30. "Organization" means a corporation, a government or
24 government subdivision, an agency, a trust, an estate, a
25 partnership, a limited liability company, a cooperative, an
26 association, a joint venture, an unincorporated organization, or
27 any other entity, however organized.
28 Sec. 31. "Payable in installments", with respect to a debt or an
29 obligation, means that payment is required or permitted by written
30 agreement to be made in more than four (4) installments not
31 including a down payment.
32 Sec. 32. "Person" includes an individual or an organization.
33 Sec. 33. "Principal" of a mortgage transaction means the total
34 of:
35 (1) the net amount paid to, receivable by, or paid or payable
36 for the account of the debtor; and
37 (2) to the extent that payment is deferred, amounts actually
38 paid or to be paid by the creditor for registration, certificate
39 of title, or license fees if not included in subdivision (1).
40 Sec. 34. "Real estate brokerage activity" means any activity that
41 involves offering or providing real estate brokerage services to the
42 public, including the following:
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

100
1 (1) Acting as a real estate agent or real estate broker for a
2 buyer, seller, lessor, or lessee of real property.
3 (2) Bringing together parties interested in the sale, purchase,
4 lease, rental, or exchange of real property.
5 (3) Negotiating, on behalf of any party, any part of a contract
6 relating to the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange of real
7 property (other than in connection with providing financing
8 with respect to the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange
9 of real property).
10 (4) Engaging in any activity for which a person engaged in the
11 activity is required to be registered or licensed as a real estate
12 agent or real estate broker under any applicable law.
13 (5) Offering to engage in any activity, or act in any capacity,
14 described in this subsection.
15 Sec. 35. "Registered mortgage loan originator" means any
16 individual who:
17 (1) meets the definition of mortgage loan originator and is an
18 employee of:
19 (A) a depository institution;
20 (B) a subsidiary that is owned and controlled by a
21 depository institution and regulated by a federal banking
22 agency; or
23 (C) an institution regulated by the Farm Credit
24 Administration; and
25 (2) is registered with, and maintains a unique identifier
26 through, the NMLSR.
27 Sec. 36. "Regularly engaged", with respect to a person who
28 extends or originates first lien mortgage transactions, refers to a
29 person who:
30 (1) extended or originated more than five (5) first lien
31 mortgage transactions in the preceding calendar year; or
32 (2) extends or originates, or will extend or originate, more
33 than five (5) first lien mortgage transactions in the current
34 calendar year if the person did not extend or originate more
35 than five (5) first lien mortgage transactions in the preceding
36 calendar year.
37 Sec. 37. "Residential real estate" means any real property that
38 is located in Indiana and on which there is located or intended to
39 be constructed a dwelling.
40 Sec. 38. "Revolving first lien mortgage transaction" means a
41 first lien mortgage transaction in which:
42 (1) the creditor permits the debtor to obtain advances from
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

101
1 time to time;
2 (2) the unpaid balances of principal, finance charges, and
3 other appropriate charges are debited to an account; and
4 (3) the debtor has the privilege of paying the balances in
5 installments.
6 Sec. 39. "Tablefunded" means a transaction in which:
7 (1) a person closes a first lien mortgage transaction in the
8 person's own name as a mortgagee with funds provided by
9 one (1) or more other persons; and
10 (2) the transaction is assigned, not later than one (1) business
11 day after the funding of the transaction, to the mortgage
12 creditor providing the funding.
13 Sec. 40. "Unique identifier" means a number or other identifier
14 assigned by protocols established by the NMLSR.
15 Chapter 3. Miscellaneous Provisions
16 Sec. 1. (a) A creditor or mortgage servicer shall provide, in
17 writing, an accurate payoff amount for a first lien mortgage
18 transaction to the debtor not later than seven (7) business days
19 (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and Sundays) after the
20 creditor or mortgage servicer receives the debtor's written request
21 for the accurate payoff amount. A payoff statement provided by a
22 creditor or mortgage servicer under this subsection must show the
23 date the statement was prepared and itemize the unpaid principal
24 balance and each fee, charge, or other sum included within the
25 payoff amount. A creditor or mortgage servicer who fails to
26 provide an accurate payoff amount is liable for:
27 (1) one hundred dollars ($100) if an accurate payoff amount
28 is not provided by the creditor or mortgage servicer not later
29 than seven (7) business days (excluding legal public holidays,
30 Saturdays, and Sundays) after the creditor or mortgage
31 servicer receives the debtor's first written request; and
32 (2) the greater of:
33 (A) one hundred dollars ($100); or
34 (B) the loan finance charge that accrues on the first lien
35 mortgage transaction from the date the creditor or
36 mortgage servicer receives the first written request until
37 the date on which the accurate payoff amount is provided;
38 if an accurate payoff amount is not provided by the creditor
39 or mortgage servicer not later than seven (7) business days
40 (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and Sundays)
41 after the creditor or mortgage servicer receives the debtor's
42 second written request, and the creditor or mortgage servicer
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

102
1 fails to comply with subdivision (1).
2 (b) This subsection applies to a first lien mortgage transaction,
3 or the refinancing or consolidation of a first lien mortgage
4 transaction, that:
5 (1) is closed after June 30, 2009; and
6 (2) has an interest rate that is subject to change at one (1) or
7 more times during the term of the first lien mortgage
8 transaction.
9 A creditor in a transaction to which this subsection applies may not
10 contract for and may not charge the debtor a prepayment fee or
11 penalty.
12 (c) This subsection applies to a first lien mortgage transaction
13 with respect to which any installment or minimum payment due is
14 delinquent for at least sixty (60) days. The creditor, servicer, or the
15 creditor's agent shall acknowledge a written offer made in
16 connection with a proposed short sale not later than five (5)
17 business days (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and
18 Sundays) after the date of the offer if the offer complies with the
19 requirements for a qualified written request set forth in 12 U.S.C.
20 2605(e)(1)(B). The creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent is required
21 to acknowledge a written offer made in connection with a proposed
22 short sale from a third party acting on behalf of the debtor only if
23 the debtor has provided written authorization for the creditor,
24 servicer, or creditor's agent to do so. Not later than thirty (30)
25 business days (excluding legal public holidays, Saturdays, and
26 Sundays) after receipt of an offer under this subsection, the
27 creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent shall respond to the offer
28 with an acceptance or a rejection of the offer. The thirty (30) day
29 period described in this subsection may be extended for not more
30 than fifteen (15) business days (excluding legal public holidays,
31 Saturdays, and Sundays) if, before the end of the thirty (30) day
32 period, the creditor, the servicer, or the creditor's agent notifies the
33 debtor of the extension and the reason the extension is needed.
34 Payment accepted by a creditor, servicer, or creditor's agent in
35 connection with a short sale constitutes payment in full satisfaction
36 of the first lien mortgage transaction unless the creditor, servicer,
37 or creditor's agent obtains:
38 (1) the following statement: "The debtor remains liable for
39 any amount still owed under the first lien mortgage
40 transaction."; or
41 (2) a statement substantially similar to the statement set forth
42 in subdivision (1);
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

103
1 acknowledged by the initials or signature of the debtor, on or
2 before the date on which the short sale payment is accepted. As
3 used in this subsection, "short sale" means a transaction in which
4 the property that is the subject of a first lien mortgage transaction
5 is sold for an amount that is less than the amount of the debtor's
6 outstanding obligation under the first lien mortgage transaction.
7 A creditor or mortgage servicer that fails to respond to an offer
8 within the time prescribed by this subsection is liable in accordance
9 with 12 U.S.C. 2605(f) in any action brought under that section.
10 (d) This section is not intended to provide the owner of real
11 estate subject to the issuance of process under a judgment or
12 decree of foreclosure any protection or defense against a deficiency
13 judgment for purposes of the borrower protections from liability
14 that must be disclosed under 12 CFR 1026.38(p)(3) on the form
15 required by 12 CFR 1026.38 ("Closing Disclosures" form under
16 the Amendments to the 2013 Integrated Mortgage Disclosures Rule
17 Under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (Regulation X)
18 and the Truth In Lending Act (Regulation Z) and the 2013 Loan
19 Originator Rule Under the Truth in Lending Act (Regulation Z)).
20 Sec. 2. (a) Except as provided in subsection (b), the creditor
21 shall comply with disclosure requirements applicable to first lien
22 mortgage transactions in the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15
23 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
24 (b) Disclosures are not required if the transaction is exempt
25 from the Consumer Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1601 et seq.).
26 Sec. 3. (a) A violation of a state or federal law, regulation, or
27 rule applicable to first lien mortgage transactions is a violation of
28 this article.
29 (b) The department may enforce penalty provisions set forth in
30 15 U.S.C. 1640 for violations of disclosure requirements applicable
31 to first lien mortgage transactions.
32 Sec. 4. (a) Unless a person subject to this article has first
33 obtained a mortgage license from the department and annually
34 maintains the license, the person shall not engage in Indiana as a
35 creditor in first lien mortgage transactions. A separate mortgage
36 license is required for each legal entity that engages in Indiana as
37 a creditor in first lien mortgage transactions. However, a separate
38 mortgage license is not required for each branch of a legal entity
39 licensed by the department.
40 (b) Each:
41 (1) creditor licensed by the department to engage in mortgage
42 transactions; and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

104
1 (2) person exempt from licensing that:
2 (A) employs a licensed mortgage loan originator; or
3 (B) sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as
4 permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
5 shall register with and maintain a valid unique identifier issued by
6 the NMLSR. Each licensed mortgage loan originator must be
7 employed by, or sponsored as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by
8 750 IAC 9, and associated with, a licensed creditor (or a person
9 exempt from licensing) that is registered with the NMLSR.
10 (c) An individual engaging solely in loan processor or
11 underwriter activities shall not represent to the public, through
12 advertising or other means of communicating or providing
13 information, including the use of business cards, stationery,
14 brochures, signs, rate lists, or other promotional items, that the
15 individual can or will perform any of the activities of a mortgage
16 loan originator. An individual who:
17 (1) is licensed as a mortgage loan originator under this article
18 and 750 IAC 9-3; and
19 (2) is sponsored by a person, as permitted by IC 37-1-1-6(8),
20 to engage solely as a third party loan processor or
21 underwriter;
22 is subject to the prohibition set forth in this subsection with respect
23 to the individual's engagement under the sponsorship.
24 (d) An applicant for a mortgage license must apply for the
25 license in the form prescribed by the director. Each form:
26 (1) must contain content as set forth by rule, instruction, or
27 procedure of the director; and
28 (2) may be changed or updated as necessary by the director to
29 carry out the purposes of this article.
30 (e) To fulfill the purposes of this article, the director may
31 establish relationships or contracts with the NMLSR or other
32 entities designated by the NMLSR to:
33 (1) collect and maintain records; and
34 (2) process transaction fees or other fees related to licensees
35 or other persons subject to this article.
36 (f) For the purpose of participating in the NMLSR, the director
37 or the department may:
38 (1) waive or modify, in whole or in part, by rule or order, any
39 of the requirements of this article; and
40 (2) establish new requirements as reasonably necessary to
41 participate in the NMLSR.
42 Sec. 5. (a) The department shall receive and act on all
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

105
1 applications for licenses to engage in first lien mortgage
2 transactions. Applications must be made as prescribed by the
3 director. If, at any time, the information or record contained in:
4 (1) an application filed under this section; or
5 (2) a renewal application filed under section 10 of this
6 chapter;
7 is or becomes inaccurate or incomplete in a material respect, the
8 applicant shall promptly file a correcting amendment with the
9 department.
10 (b) A license may not be issued unless the department finds that
11 the professional training and experience, financial responsibility,
12 character, and fitness of:
13 (1) the applicant and any significant affiliate of the applicant;
14 (2) each executive officer, director, or manager of the
15 applicant, or any other individual having a similar status or
16 performing a similar function for the applicant; and
17 (3) if known, each person directly or indirectly owning of
18 record or owning beneficially at least ten percent (10%) of the
19 outstanding shares of any class of equity security of the
20 applicant;
21 are such as to warrant belief that the business will be operated
22 honestly and fairly within the purposes of this article.
23 (c) The director is entitled to request evidence of compliance
24 with this section at:
25 (1) the time of application;
26 (2) the time of renewal of a license; or
27 (3) any other time considered necessary by the director.
28 (d) Evidence of compliance with this section must include:
29 (1) criminal background checks, as described in section 6 of
30 this chapter, including a national criminal history background
31 check (as defined in IC 10-13-3-12) by the Federal Bureau of
32 Investigation, for any individual described in subsection (b);
33 (2) credit histories as described in section 7 of this chapter;
34 (3) surety bond requirements as described in section 8 of this
35 chapter;
36 (4) a review of licensure actions in Indiana and in other states;
37 and
38 (5) other background checks considered necessary by the
39 director.
40 (e) For purposes of this section and in order to reduce the points
41 of contact that the director has to maintain for purposes of this
42 section, the director may use the NMLSR as a channeling agent for
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

106
1 requesting and distributing information to and from any source as
2 directed by the director.
3 (f) The department may deny an application under this section
4 if the director of the department determines that the application
5 was submitted for the benefit of, or on behalf of, a person who does
6 not qualify for a license.
7 (g) Upon written request, the applicant is entitled to a hearing
8 on the question of the qualifications of the applicant for a license
9 in the manner provided in IC 4-21.5.
10 (h) The applicant shall pay the following fees at the time
11 designated by the department:
12 (1) An initial license fee as established by the department
13 under IC 28-11-3-5.
14 (2) An annual renewal fee as established by the department
15 under IC 28-11-3-5.
16 (3) Examination fees as established by the department under
17 IC 28-11-3-5.
18 (i) A fee as established by the department under IC 28-11-3-5
19 may be charged for each day a fee under subsection (h)(2) or (h)(3)
20 is delinquent.
21 (j) Except in a transaction approved under section 20 of this
22 chapter, a license issued under this section is not assignable or
23 transferable.
24 (k) If the department of state revenue notifies the department
25 that a person is on the most recent tax warrant list, the department
26 shall not issue or renew the person's license until:
27 (1) the person provides to the department a statement from
28 the department of state revenue that the person's tax warrant
29 has been satisfied; or
30 (2) the department receives a notice from the commissioner of
31 the department of state revenue under IC 6-8.1-8-2(k).
32 Sec. 6. (a) When the director requests a national criminal
33 history background check under section 5(d)(1) of this chapter for

38 this chapter. The individual to whom the request is made shall pay

41 The national criminal history background check may be used by
42 the director to determine the individual's compliance with this
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

1 section. The director or the department may not release the results
2 of the national criminal history background check to any private

4 (b) For purposes of this section and in order to reduce the points
5 of contact that the Federal Bureau of Investigation may have to
6 maintain for purposes of this section, the director may use the

10 Sec. 7. (a) If the director requests a credit report for an

12 to whom the request is made shall pay any fees or costs associated

18 reporting agency described in Section 603(p) of the Fair Credit
19 Reporting Act (15 U.S.C. 1681a(p)).

21 when determining if an individual has demonstrated financial
22 responsibility:
23 (1) Bankruptcies filed within the last ten (10) years.
24 (2) Current outstanding judgments, except judgments solely
25 as a result of medical expenses.
26 (3) Current outstanding tax liens or other government liens or
27 filings.
28 (4) Foreclosures within the past three (3) years.
29 (5) A pattern of serious delinquent accounts within the past
30 three (3) years.
31 Sec. 8. (a) Each:
32 (1) creditor; and

6 (3) be in effect:

10 sponsors a licensed mortgage loan originator as permitted
11 by IC 37-1-1-6(8) or by 750 IAC 9;
12 as applicable;

14 (2) years after:

16 terminated; or

40 (c) The director may adopt rules or guidance documents with

2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

109
1 (1) a creditor described in subsection (a)(1); or
2 (2) an exempt person described in subsection (a)(2);
3 the director may, at the discretion of the director, waive or shorten
4 the two (2) year period set forth in subsection (b)(4) during which
5 a surety bond required by this section must remain in effect after

8 (d) The penal sum of the surety bond shall be maintained in an
9 amount that reflects the dollar amount of mortgage transactions
10 originated as determined by the director. If the principal amount
11 of a surety bond required under this section is reduced by payment
12 of a claim or judgment, the creditor or exempt person for whom
13 the bond is issued shall immediately notify the director of the
14 reduction and, not later than thirty (30) days after notice by the
15 director, file a new or an additional surety bond in an amount set
16 by the director. The amount of the new or additional bond set by
17 the director must be at least the amount of the bond before
18 payment of the claim or judgment.
19 (e) If for any reason a surety terminates a bond issued under
20 this section, the creditor or the exempt person shall immediately
21 notify the department and file a new surety bond in an amount
22 determined by the director.
23 (f) Cancellation of a surety bond issued under this section does
24 not affect any liability incurred or accrued during the period when
25 the surety bond was in effect.
26 (g) The director may obtain satisfaction from a surety bond
27 issued under this section if the director incurs expenses, issues a
28 final order, or recovers a final judgment under this chapter.
29 (h) Notices required under this section must be made in writing

16 (A) obtain information from the NMLSR, unless the

18 (B) initiate any civil action based on information obtained

31 are confidential and privileged by law and are not subject to
32 inspection under IC 5-14-3, subject to subpoena, subject to
33 discovery, or admissible in evidence in any civil action.
34 However, the director may use the documents, materials, or
35 other information available to the director in furtherance of
36 any action brought in connection with the director's duties
37 under this article.

39 (A) to the director; or

41 under this subsection does not result in a waiver of any

1 the documents, materials, or information.

20 (g) Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any:
21 (1) application, renewal, or other form or document that:
22 (A) relates to mortgage licenses issued by the department;
23 and
24 (B) is made or produced in an electronic format;
25 (2) document filed as an electronic record in a multistate
26 automated repository established and operated for the
27 licensing or registration of mortgage lenders, brokers, or loan
28 originators; or
29 (3) electronic record filed through the NMLSR;
30 is considered a valid original document when reproduced in paper
31 form by the department.
32 Sec. 10. (a) A mortgage license issued by the department must
33 be renewed through the NMLSR not later than December 31 of
34 each calendar year. The minimum standards for license renewal
35 for a creditor include the following:
36 (1) The creditor has continued to meet the surety bond
37 requirement under section 8 of this chapter.
38 (2) The creditor has filed the creditor's call report in a
39 manner that satisfies section 19(d) of this chapter.
40 (3) The creditor has paid all required fees for renewal of the
41 license.
42 (4) The creditor and individuals described in section 5(b) of
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

113
1 this chapter have certified to the department that they
2 continue to meet all the standards for licensing contained in
3 section 5 of this chapter.
4 (5) The creditor has provided in the creditor's renewal
5 application:
6 (A) any information describing material changes in the
7 information contained in the creditor's original application
8 for licensure, or in any previous application, including any
9 previous renewal application; and
10 (B) any other information the director requires in order to
11 evaluate the renewal of the license.
12 (b) A license issued by the department authorizing a person to
13 engage in first lien mortgage transactions as a creditor under this
14 article may be revoked or suspended by the department if the
15 person fails to:
16 (1) file any renewal form required by the department; or
17 (2) pay any license renewal fee described under section 5 of
18 this chapter;
19 not later than sixty (60) days after the due date.
20 (c) A person whose license is revoked or suspended under this
21 section may do either of the following:
22 (1) Pay all delinquent fees and apply for reinstatement of the
23 license.
24 (2) Appeal the revocation or suspension to the department for
25 an administrative review under IC 4-21.5-3. Pending the
26 decision resulting from the hearing under IC 4-21.5-3
27 concerning the license revocation or suspension, the license
28 remains in force.
29 (d) If, at any time, the information or record contained in:
30 (1) an original application for licensure filed under section 5
31 of this chapter; or
32 (2) a renewal application filed under this section;
33 is or becomes inaccurate or incomplete in a material respect, the
34 applicant shall promptly file a correcting amendment with the
35 department.
36 Sec. 11. (a) The department may issue to a person licensed as a
37 creditor to engage in first lien mortgage transactions an order to
38 show cause why the person's license should not be revoked or
39 suspended for a period determined by the department.
40 (b) An order issued under subsection (a) must:
41 (1) include:
42 (A) a statement of the place, date, and time for a meeting
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

114
1 with the department, which date may not be less than ten
2 (10) days from the date of the order;
3 (B) a description of the action contemplated by the
4 department; and
5 (C) a statement of the facts or conduct supporting the
6 issuance of the order; and
7 (2) be accompanied by a notice stating that the licensee is
8 entitled to:
9 (A) a reasonable opportunity to be heard; and
10 (B) show the licensee's compliance with all lawful
11 requirements for retention of the license;
12 at the meeting described in subdivision (1)(A).
13 (c) After the meeting described in subsection (b)(1)(A), the
14 department may revoke or suspend the license if the department
15 finds that:
16 (1) the licensee has repeatedly and willfully violated:
17 (A) this article or any applicable rule, order, or guidance
18 document adopted or issued by the department; or
19 (B) any other state or federal law, regulation, or rule
20 applicable to first lien mortgage transactions;
21 (2) the licensee does not meet the licensing qualifications
22 contained in section 5 of this chapter;
23 (3) the licensee obtained the license for the benefit of, or on
24 behalf of, another person;
25 (4) the licensee knowingly or intentionally made material
26 misrepresentations to, or concealed material information
27 from, the department; or
28 (5) facts or conditions exist that, had they existed at the time
29 the licensee applied for the license, would have been grounds
30 for the department to deny the issuance of the license.
31 (d) Whenever the department revokes or suspends a license, the
32 department shall enter an order to that effect and notify the
33 licensee of:
34 (1) the revocation or suspension;
35 (2) if a suspension has been ordered, the duration of the
36 suspension;
37 (3) the procedure for appealing the revocation or suspension
38 under IC 4-21.5-3-6; and
39 (4) any other terms and conditions that apply to the
40 revocation or suspension.
41 Not later than five (5) days after the entry of the order, the
42 department shall deliver to the licensee a copy of the order and the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

115
1 findings supporting the order.
2 (e) Any person holding a license as a creditor to engage in first
3 lien mortgage transactions may relinquish the license by notifying
4 the department in writing of the relinquishment. However, a
5 relinquishment under this subsection does not affect the person's
6 liability for acts previously committed and coming within the scope
7 of this article.
8 (f) If the director determines it to be in the public interest, the
9 director may pursue revocation of a license of a licensee that has
10 relinquished the license under subsection (e).
11 (g) If a person's license is revoked, suspended, or relinquished,
12 the revocation, suspension, or relinquishment does not impair or
13 affect any obligation owed by any person under any preexisting
14 lawful contract.
15 (h) If the director has just cause to believe an emergency exists
16 from which it is necessary to protect the interests of the public, the
17 director may proceed with the revocation of a license through an
18 emergency or another temporary order under IC 4-21.5-4.
19 Sec. 12. (a) If the director determines that a current or former
20 director, an officer, or a manager of a creditor:
21 (1) has committed a violation of a statute, a rule, a final cease
22 and desist order, any condition imposed in writing by the
23 director in connection with the granting of any application or
24 other request by the creditor, or any written agreement
25 between the creditor and the director or the department;
26 (2) has committed fraudulent or unconscionable conduct; or
27 (3) has been convicted of a felony under the laws of Indiana or
28 any other jurisdiction;
29 the director, subject to subsection (b), may issue and serve upon
30 the officer, director, or manager a notice of the director's intent to
31 issue an order removing the person from the person's office or
32 employment, an order prohibiting any participation by the person
33 in the conduct of the affairs of any creditor, or an order both
34 removing the person and prohibiting the person's participation.
35 (b) A violation, practice, or breach specified in subsection (a) is
36 subject to the authority of the director under subsections (a) and
37 (c) if the director finds any of the following:
38 (1) The interests of the creditor's customers could be seriously
39 prejudiced by reason of the violation or practice.
40 (2) The violation, practice, or breach involves an act of fraud,
41 dishonesty, theft, breach of trust, money laundering, or the
42 wrongful taking of property on the part of the officer,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

116
1 director, or manager involved.
2 (3) The violation, practice, or breach demonstrates a willful
3 or continuing disregard by the officer, director, or manager
4 for state and federal laws and regulations, and for the
5 consumer protections contained in this article.
6 (c) A person who has been convicted of a felony under the laws
7 of Indiana or any other jurisdiction may not serve as an officer, a
8 director, or a manager of a creditor, or serve in any similar
9 capacity, unless the person obtains the written consent of the
10 director.
11 (d) A creditor that willfully permits a person to serve the
12 creditor in violation of subsection (c) is subject to a civil penalty of
13 five hundred dollars ($500) for each day the violation continues.
14 (e) A creditor shall give the department written notice of the
15 resignation, discharge, or termination of an employee, independent
16 contractor, or agent against whom allegations were made that
17 accused the employee, independent contractor, or agent of:
18 (1) violating this article or other laws, regulations, rules, or
19 industry standards of conduct applicable to first lien
20 mortgage transactions; or
21 (2) fraud, dishonesty, theft, breach of trust, money
22 laundering, or the wrongful taking of property.
23 The creditor shall provide the department the notice required
24 under this subsection not later than thirty (30) days after the
25 effective date of the resignation, discharge, or termination.
26 Sec. 13. (a) A notice issued under this chapter must:
27 (1) be in writing;
28 (2) contain a statement of the facts constituting the alleged
29 practice, violation, or breach;
30 (3) state the facts alleged in support of the violation, practice,
31 or breach;
32 (4) state the director's intention to enter an order under
33 section 15(a) of this chapter;
34 (5) be delivered to the board of directors of the creditor;
35 (6) be delivered to the officer, director, or manager
36 concerned;
37 (7) specify the procedures that must be followed to initiate a
38 hearing to contest the facts alleged; and
39 (8) if the director suspends or prohibits an officer, a director,
40 or a manager of the creditor from participating in the affairs
41 of the creditor, as described in subsection (e), include a
42 statement of the suspension or prohibition.
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

117
1 (b) If a hearing is requested not later than ten (10) days after
2 service of the written notice, the department shall hold a hearing
3 concerning the alleged practice, violation, or breach. The hearing
4 shall be held not later than forty-five (45) days after receipt of the
5 request. The department, based on the evidence presented at the
6 hearing, shall enter a final order under section 15 of this chapter.
7 (c) If no hearing is requested within the time specified in
8 subsection (b), the director may proceed to issue a final order
9 under section 15 of this chapter on the basis of the facts set forth in
10 the written notice.
11 (d) An officer, a director, or a manager who is removed from a
12 position under a removal order that has become final may not
13 participate in the conduct of the affairs of any mortgage licensee
14 without the approval of the director.
15 (e) The director may, for the protection of the creditor or the
16 interests of its customers, suspend from office or prohibit from
17 participation in the affairs of the creditor an officer, a director, or
18 a manager of a creditor who is the subject of a written notice
19 served by the director under section 12(a) of this chapter. A
20 suspension or prohibition under this subsection becomes effective
21 upon service of the notice under section 12(a) of this chapter.
22 Unless stayed by a court in a proceeding authorized by subsection
23 (f), the suspension or prohibition remains in effect pending
24 completion of the proceedings related to the notice served under
25 section 12(a) of this chapter and until the effective date of an order
26 entered by the department under subsection (b) or the director
27 under subsection (c). Copies of the notice shall also be served upon
28 the creditor or affiliate of which the person is an officer, a director,
29 or a manager.
30 (f) Not more than fifteen (15) days after an officer, a director, or
31 a manager has been suspended from office or prohibited from
32 participation in the conduct of the affairs of the creditor or affiliate
33 under subsection (e), the officer, director, or manager may apply
34 to a court having jurisdiction for a stay of the suspension or
35 prohibition pending completion of the proceedings related to the
36 written notice served under section 12(a) of this chapter, and the
37 court may stay the suspension or prohibition.
38 (g) The department shall maintain an official record of a
39 proceeding under this chapter.
40 Sec. 14. If the director enters into a consent to a final order
41 under section 15 of this chapter with a creditor, a director, an
42 officer, or a manager, the director is not required to issue and
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

118
1 serve a notice of charges upon the creditor, director, officer, or
2 manager under section 12 of this chapter. A consent agreement
3 may be negotiated and entered into before or after the issuance of
4 a notice of charges. The director shall provide a copy of the consent
5 order to the board of directors of the creditor.
6 Sec. 15. (a) If, after a hearing described in section 13(b) of this
7 chapter, the department finds that the conditions specified in
8 section 12 of this chapter have been established, the department
9 may issue a final order. If a hearing is not requested within the
10 time specified in section 13(b) of this chapter, the director may
11 issue a final order on the basis of the facts set forth in the written
12 notice served under section 12(a) of this chapter.
13 (b) Unless the director has entered into a consent agreement
14 described in section 14 of this chapter, a final order must include
15 separately stated findings of fact and conclusions of law for all
16 aspects of the order.
17 (c) In a final order under this section, the department or the
18 director, as appropriate, may order one (1) or more of the
19 following with respect to an officer, a director, or a manager of a
20 creditor:
21 (1) The removal of the officer, director, or manager from the
22 person's office, position, or employment.
23 (2) A prohibition against any participation by the officer,
24 director, or manager in the conduct of the affairs of any
25 creditor.
26 (3) If the subject of the order is an officer or a director of a
27 creditor, and subject to section 17 of this chapter, the
28 imposition of a civil penalty not to exceed fifteen thousand
29 dollars ($15,000) for each practice, violation, or act that:
30 (A) is described in section 12 of this chapter; and
31 (B) is found to exist by the department or the director.
32 (d) A final order shall be issued in writing not later than ninety
33 (90) days after conclusion of a hearing held under section 13(b) of
34 this chapter, unless this period is waived or extended with the
35 written consent of all parties or for good cause shown.
36 (e) If the officer, director, or manager does not appear
37 individually or by an authorized representative at a hearing held
38 under section 13(b) of this chapter, the officer, director, or
39 manager is considered to have consented to the issuance of a final
40 order.
41 (f) The remedies provided in this chapter are in addition to
42 other remedies contained in this article.

119
1 Sec. 16. (a) A final order issued under this chapter is effective on
2 the eleventh day after service of the order. However, a final order
3 issued upon consent under section 14 of this chapter is effective at
4 the time specified in the order.
5 (b) A final order remains effective and enforceable as provided
6 in the order.
7 (c) The department or a reviewing court may stay, modify, or
8 vacate a final order.
9 Sec. 17. (a) The director or the department, as appropriate, shall
10 consider the following factors in determining the amount of a civil
11 penalty that should be assessed against a director or an officer in
12 a final order issued under section 15(c)(3) of this chapter:
13 (1) The appropriateness of the civil penalty with respect to the
14 financial resources and good faith of the individual charged.
15 (2) The gravity of the practice, violation, or act.
16 (3) The history of previous practices, violations, or acts.
17 (4) The economic benefit derived by the individual from the
18 practice, violation, or act.
19 (5) Other factors that justice requires.
20 (b) A creditor may not indemnify a director or an officer for a
21 civil penalty imposed in a final order under section 15(c)(3) of this
22 chapter.
23 (c) Civil penalties shall be deposited in the financial institutions
24 fund established by IC 28-11-2-9.
25 Sec. 18. The director may enforce any of the following by
26 applying for appropriate relief to a court having jurisdiction:
27 (1) An order issued under this chapter.
28 (2) A written agreement entered into by the department or the
29 director and any director, officer, or employee of a creditor.
30 (3) Any condition imposed in writing by the department or the
31 director on any director, officer, or employee of a creditor.
32 Sec. 19. (a) Every licensee shall maintain records in a manner
33 that will enable the department to determine whether the licensee
34 is complying with this article. The record keeping system of a
35 licensee is sufficient if the licensee makes the required information
36 reasonably available. The department shall determine the
37 sufficiency of the records and whether the licensee has made the
38 required information reasonably available. The department shall
39 be given free access to the records wherever the records are
40 located. Records concerning any first lien mortgage transaction
41 shall be retained for two (2) years after the making of the final
42 entry relating to the transaction, but in the case of a revolving first

1 lien mortgage transaction, the two (2) years required under this

6 (1) cooperate with the department; and
7 (2) provide access to records and documents;
8 as required by the department in carrying out examinations of the

11 (b) The unique identifier of any person originating a mortgage

15 (c) Every licensee shall use automated examination and

1 prescribed by the department.
2 (f) A licensed creditor shall file notification with the department

4 (1) has a change in name, address, or any of its principals;

25 agency or self-regulatory organization. The licensee shall file the

27 days after the date of the event described in this subsection.
28 Sec. 20. (a) As used in this section, "control" means possession
29 of the power directly or indirectly to:
30 (1) direct or cause the direction of the management or policies
31 of a creditor, whether through the beneficial ownership of
32 voting securities, by contract, or otherwise; or
33 (2) vote at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the voting
34 securities of a creditor, whether the voting rights are derived
35 through the beneficial ownership of voting securities, by
36 contract, or otherwise.
37 (b) An organization or an individual acting directly, indirectly,
38 or through or in concert with one (1) or more other organizations
39 or individuals may not acquire control of any creditor unless the
40 department has received and approved an application for change
41 in control. The department has not more than one hundred twenty
42 (120) days after receipt of an application to issue a notice
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

122
1 approving the proposed change in control. The application must
2 contain the name and address of the organization, individual, or
3 individuals who propose to acquire control and any other
4 information required by the director.
5 (c) The period for approval under subsection (b) may be
6 extended:
7 (1) in the discretion of the director for an additional thirty
8 (30) days; and
9 (2) not more than two (2) additional times for not more than
10 forty-five (45) days each time if:
11 (A) the director determines that the organization,
12 individual, or individuals who propose to acquire control
13 have not submitted substantial evidence of the
14 qualifications described in subsection (d);
15 (B) the director determines that any material information
16 submitted is substantially inaccurate; or
17 (C) the director has been unable to complete the
18 investigation of the organization, individual, or individuals
19 who propose to acquire control because of any delay
20 caused by or the inadequate cooperation of the
21 organization, individual, or individuals.
22 (d) The department shall issue a notice approving the
23 application only after it is satisfied that both of the following apply:
24 (1) The organization, individual, or individuals who propose
25 to acquire control are qualified by competence, experience,
26 character, and financial responsibility to control and operate
27 the creditor in a legal and proper manner.
28 (2) The interests of the owners and creditors of the creditor
29 and the interests of the public generally will not be
30 jeopardized by the proposed change in control.
31 (e) The director may determine, in the director's discretion, that
32 subsection (b) does not apply to a transaction if the director
33 determines that the direct or beneficial ownership of the creditor
34 will not change as a result of the transaction.
35 (f) The president or other chief executive officer of a creditor
36 shall report to the director any transfer or sale of securities of the
37 creditor that results in direct or indirect ownership by a holder or
38 an affiliated group of holders of at least ten percent (10%) of the
39 outstanding securities of the creditor. The report required by this
40 subsection must be made not later than ten (10) days after the
41 transfer of the securities on the books of the creditor.
42 (g) Depending on the circumstances of the transaction, the
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

123
1 director may reserve the right to require the organization,
2 individual, or individuals who propose to acquire control of a
3 creditor licensed by the department to engage in mortgage
4 transactions, to apply for a new license under section 4 of this
5 chapter, instead of acquiring control of the licensee under this
6 section.
7 Sec. 21. A creditor in a first lien mortgage transaction shall
8 comply with IC 6-1.1-12-43, to the extent applicable.
9 Sec. 22. (a) A violation by a creditor in a first lien mortgage
10 transaction of Section 125 of the Consumer Credit Protection Act
11 (15 U.S.C. 1635) (concerning a debtor's right to rescind a
12 transaction) constitutes a violation of this article. A creditor may
13 not accrue interest during the period when a first lien mortgage
14 transaction may be rescinded under Section 125 of the Consumer
15 Credit Protection Act (15 U.S.C. 1635).
16 (b) A creditor must make available for disbursement the
17 proceeds of a transaction subject to subsection (a) on the later of:
18 (1) the date the creditor is reasonably satisfied that the debtor
19 has not rescinded the transaction; or
20 (2) the first business day after the expiration of the rescission
21 period under subsection (a).
22 Sec. 23. A creditor in a first lien mortgage transaction that:
23 (1) qualifies as a home equity conversion mortgage under the
24 Federal Housing Administration's program; or
25 (2) otherwise constitutes a reverse mortgage;
26 shall provide the debtor with a pamphlet that is approved by the
27 department and that describes the availability of reverse mortgage
28 counseling services provided by housing counselors approved by
29 the Secretary of the United States Department of Housing and
30 Urban Development, as provided in 24 CFR 206.41(a). The debtor
31 must receive the counseling described in this section and present
32 the creditor with the certificate described in 24 CFR 206.41(c)
33 before the creditor may make a first lien mortgage transaction
34 described in this section to the debtor.
35 Chapter 4. Administration
36 Sec. 1. This chapter applies to a person that engages as a
37 creditor in first lien mortgage transactions in Indiana. The
38 authority of this chapter remains in effect, whether a licensee,
39 individual, or person subject to this article acts or claims to act
40 under any licensing or registration law of Indiana or claims to act
41 without such authority.
42 Sec. 2. (a) In addition to other powers granted by this article,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

124
1 the department within the limitations provided by law may:
2 (1) receive and act on complaints, take action designed to
3 obtain voluntary compliance with this article, or commence
4 proceedings on the department's own initiative;
5 (2) counsel persons and groups on their rights and duties
6 under this article;
7 (3) establish programs for the education of consumers with
8 respect to credit practices and problems;
9 (4) make studies appropriate to effectuate the purposes and
10 policies of this article and make the results available to the
11 public;
12 (5) adopt, amend, and repeal rules, orders, policies, and forms
13 to carry out the provisions of this article;
14 (6) maintain more than one (1) office within Indiana; and
15 (7) appoint any necessary attorneys, hearing examiners,
16 clerks, and other employees and agents and fix their
17 compensation, and authorize attorneys appointed under this
18 section to appear for and represent the department in court.
19 (b) Liability may not be imposed under this article for an act
20 done or omitted in conformity with a rule, written notice, written
21 opinion, written interpretation, or written directive of the
22 department notwithstanding the fact that after the act is done or
23 omitted the rule, written notice, written opinion, written
24 interpretation, or written directive may be:
25 (1) amended or repealed; or
26 (2) determined by judicial or other authority to be invalid;
27 for any reason.
28 Sec. 3. (a) In administering this article and in order to determine
29 whether the provisions of this article are being complied with by
30 persons engaging in acts subject to this article, the department may
31 examine the records of persons and may make investigations of
32 persons as may be necessary to determine compliance. Records
33 subject to examination under this section include the following:
34 (1) Training, operating, and policy manuals.
35 (2) Minutes of:
36 (A) management meetings; and
37 (B) other meetings.
38 (3) Financial records, credit files, and data bases.
39 (4) Other records that the department determines are
40 necessary to perform its investigation or examination.
41 The department may also administer oaths or affirmations,
42 subpoena witnesses, and compel the attendance of witnesses,
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

125
1 including officers, principals, mortgage loan originators,
2 employees, independent contractors, agents, and customers of
3 licensees, and other individuals or persons subject to this article.
4 The department may also adduce evidence and require the
5 production of any matter that is relevant to an investigation. The
6 department shall determine the sufficiency of the records
7 maintained and whether the person has made the required
8 information reasonably available. The records concerning any
9 transaction subject to this article shall be retained for two (2) years
10 after the making of the final entry relating to the first lien
11 mortgage transaction, but in the case of a revolving first lien
12 mortgage transaction the two (2) year period is measured from the
13 date of each entry.
14 (b) The department's examination and investigatory authority
15 under this article includes the following:
16 (1) The authority to require a creditor to refund overcharges
17 resulting from the creditor's noncompliance with the terms of
18 a first lien mortgage transaction.
19 (2) The authority to require a creditor to comply with the
20 penalty provisions set forth in IC 37-1-3-1.
21 (3) The authority to investigate complaints filed with the
22 department by debtors.
23 (c) The department shall be given free access to the records
24 wherever the records are located. In making any examination or
25 investigation authorized by this article, the director may control
26 access to any documents and records of the licensee or person
27 under examination or investigation. The director may take
28 possession of the documents and records or place a person in
29 exclusive charge of the documents and records in the place where
30 the documents are usually kept. During the period of control, a
31 licensee or person may not remove or attempt to remove any of the
32 documents and records except under a court order or with the
33 consent of the director. Unless the director has reasonable grounds
34 to believe the documents or records of the licensee or person have
35 been, or are, at risk of being altered or destroyed for purposes of
36 concealing a violation of this article, the licensee or person shall
37 have access to the documents or records as necessary to conduct
38 the licensee's or person's ordinary business affairs. If the person's
39 records are located outside Indiana, the records shall be made
40 available to the department at a convenient location within
41 Indiana, or the person shall pay the reasonable and necessary
42 expenses for the department or the department's representative to
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

126
1 examine the records where they are maintained. The department
2 may designate comparable officials of the state in which the
3 records are located to inspect the records on behalf of the
4 department.
5 (d) Upon a person's failure without lawful excuse to obey a
6 subpoena or to give testimony and upon reasonable notice by the
7 department to all affected persons, the department may apply to
8 any civil court with jurisdiction for an order compelling
9 compliance.
10 (e) The department shall not make public:
11 (1) the name or identity of a person whose acts or conduct the
12 department investigates under this section; or
13 (2) the facts discovered in the investigation.
14 However, this subsection does not apply to civil actions or
15 enforcement proceedings under this article.
16 (f) To discover violations of this article or to secure information
17 necessary for the enforcement of this article, the department may
18 investigate any:
19 (1) licensee; or
20 (2) person that the department suspects to be operating:
21 (A) without a license, when a license is required under this
22 article; or
23 (B) otherwise in violation of this article.
24 The department has all investigatory and enforcement authority
25 under this article that the department has under IC 28-11 with
26 respect to financial institutions. If the department conducts an
27 investigation under this section, the licensee or other person
28 investigated shall pay all reasonably incurred costs of the
29 investigation in accordance with the fee schedule adopted under
30 IC 28-11-3-5. Any costs required to be paid under this section shall
31 be paid not later than sixty (60) days after the person being
32 assessed the costs receives a notice from the department of the
33 costs assessed. The department may impose a fee, in an amount
34 fixed by the department under IC 28-11-3-5, for each day the
35 assessed costs are not paid, beginning on the first day after the
36 sixty (60) day period described in this subsection.
37 (g) If a creditor contracts with an outside vendor to provide a
38 service that would otherwise be undertaken internally by the

40 procedures, the person that provides the service to the creditor
41 shall, at the request of the director, submit to an examination by
42 the department. If the director determines that an examination
2026 IN 169—LS 6567/DI 92

127
1 under this subsection is necessary or desirable, the examination
2 may be made at the expense of the person to be examined. If the

11 Sec. 4. To carry out the purposes of this article, the director
12 may:
13 (1) retain attorneys, accountants, or other professionals and
14 specialists as examiners, auditors, or investigators to conduct
15 or assist in the conduct of examinations or investigations;
16 (2) enter into agreements or relationships with other

18 improve efficiencies and reduce regulatory burden by
19 sharing:
20 (A) resources;
21 (B) standardized or uniform methods or procedures; and
22 (C) documents, records, information, or evidence obtained
23 under this section;
24 (3) use, hire, contract, or employ public or privately available
25 analytical systems, methods, or software to examine or
